zh: 美洲狮为体型庞大、状若猫科之动物，产于美洲。
en: Pumas are large, cat-like animals which are found in America.

zh: 当有人向伦敦动物园报告在伦敦以南四十五英里处发现野生美洲狮时，此等消息并未受到认真对待。
en: When reports came into London Zoo that a wild puma had been spotted forty-five miles south of London, they were not taken seriously.

zh: 然而，随着证据日渐增多，伦敦动物园的专家感到有必要展开调查，因声称见过美洲狮的人所作描述彼此竟出奇地一致。
en: However, as the evidence began to accumulate, experts from the 5 Zoo felt obliged to investigate, for the descriptions given by people who claimed to have seen the puma were extraordinarily similar.

zh: 搜寻美洲狮始于一座小村庄，一名采摘黑莓的妇人曾见一只「大猫」距其仅五码之遥。
en: The hunt for the puma began in a small village where a woman picking blackberries saw 'a large cat'only five yards away from her.

zh: 她一发现，那动物便立刻逃去；专家证实，美洲狮除非陷入绝境，否则不会攻击人类。
en: It immediately ran away when she saw it, and experts confirmed that a puma will not attack a human being unless it is cornered.

zh: 搜寻颇为困难，因有人上午在某处见其踪影，至晚间却在二十英里之外的另一处发现它。
en: The search proved difficult, for the puma was often observed at one place in the morning and at another place twenty miles away in the evening.

zh: 无论它行至何处，身后总留下一串死鹿及兔等小动物之尸骸。
en: Wherever it went, it left behind it a trail of dead deer and small animals like rabbits.

zh: 多处见其爪印，灌木丛上还挂着美洲狮毛。
en: Paw prints were seen in a number of places and puma fur was found clinging to bushes.

zh: 数人抱怨夜间有「类猫之叫声」；一名外出垂钓的商人曾见美洲狮踞于树上。
en: Several people complained of 'cat-like noises'at night and a businessman on a fishing trip saw the puma up a tree.

zh: 专家们现已完全确信此兽即为美洲狮，然其究竟从何而来？
en: The experts were now fully convinced that the animal was a puma, but where had it come from?

zh: 因国内各动物园均无报告美洲狮走失，此兽定曾为私人收藏家所蓄，其后设法逃脱。
en: As no pumas had been reported missing from any zoo in the country, this one must have been in the possession of a private collector and somehow managed to escape.

zh: 搜寻持续数周，但美洲狮终未被捕获。
en: The hunt went on for several weeks, but the puma was not caught.

zh: 思及一头危险的野兽仍流落宁静的乡间，实令人不安。
en: It is disturbing to think that a dangerous wild animal is still at large in the quiet countryside.

zh: 本教区牧师动辄为种种义举筹款，却从未筹足修缮教堂大钟之费用。
en: Our vicar is always raising money for one cause or another, but he has never managed to get enough money to have the church clock repaired.

zh: 昔日昼夜报时之大钟多年前已损坏，自此便寂然无声。
en: The big clock which used to strike the hours day and night was damaged many years ago and has been silent ever since.

zh: 然有一夜，牧师猝然惊醒：大钟竟又敲响报时！
en: One night, however, our vicar woke up with a start: the clock was striking the hours!

zh: 他看表时针指一时，大钟却敲了十三下方止。
en: Looking at his watch, he saw that it was one o'clock, but the bell struck thirteen times before it stopped.

zh: 牧师手持电筒登上钟塔察看究竟。
en: Armed with a torch, the vicar went up into the clock tower to see what was going on.

zh: 电筒光下，他瞥见一人影，旋即认出乃本地杂货商比尔·威尔金斯。
en: In the torchlight, he caught sight of a figure whom he immediately recognized as Bill Wilkins, our local grocer.

zh: 「比尔，你在此做甚么？」
en: 'Whatever are you doing up here Bill?

zh: 牧师惊诧地问道。
en: 'asked the vicar in surprise.

zh: 「我在设法修钟。」比尔答道。
en: I’m trying to repair the bell,'answered Bil.

zh: 「我连日来每夜皆登此钟塔，已历数周。」
en: 'I've been coming up here night after night for weeks now.

zh: 「原想给您一个惊喜。」
en: You see, I was hoping to give you a surprise.

zh: 「您确也令我大吃一惊！」
en: ' ‘You certainly did give me a surprise!

zh: 牧师道。「只怕全村人都被您吵醒了。」
en: '’said the vicar.‘You've probably woken up everyone in the village as well.

zh: 「不过，钟能再走，我亦欣慰。」
en: Still, I’m glad the bell is working again.

zh: 「问题正在于此，牧师。」比尔答道。
en: ' 'That's the trouble, vicar,'answered Bill.

zh: 「钟倒是走得正常，然恐怕一时之际仍将敲十三下，我亦无可奈何。」
en: It's working all right, but I’m afraid that at one o'clock it will strike thirteen times and there's nothing I can do about it.

zh: 「我们会渐渐习惯的，比尔。」牧师说。
en: ' 'We'llget used to that, Bill,'said the vicar.

zh: 「十三虽不及一，总胜于无。」
en: Thirteen is not as good as one, but it's better than nothing.

zh: 「且下楼饮一杯茶吧。」
en: Now let's go downstairs and have a cup of tea.

zh: 不久前，考古学家于爱琴海的基亚岛上有了一项有趣的发现。
en: Some time ago, an interesting discovery was made by archaeologists on the Aegean island of Kea.

zh: 一支美国考察队考察了阿伊娅·伊里尼海岬古城中矗立的一座庙宇。
en: An American team explored a temple which stands in an ancient city on the promontory of Ayia Irini.

zh: 该城昔时必曾繁荣，因其文明程度甚高。
en: The city at one time must have been prosperous, for it enjoyed a high level of civilization.

zh: 住宅——常高达三层——皆以石料筑成。
en: Houses —often three storeys high 一 were built of stone.

zh: 室内宽敞，墙壁装饰华美。
en: They had large rooms with beautifully decorated walls.

zh: 城内甚至备有排水系统，因狭窄街道之下发现大量陶管。
en: The city was even equipped with a drainage system, for a great many clay pipes were found beneath the narrow streets.

zh: 考古学家考察的这座庙宇自公元前十五世纪起便用作礼拜之所。
en: The temple which the archaeologists explored was used as a place of worship from the fifteenth century 10 B.

zh: 此种用途一直延续至罗马时代。
en: C.until Roman times.

zh: 在庙中最神圣的内室，发现十五尊雕像的陶土碎片。
en: In the most sacred room of the temple, clay fragments of fifteen statues were found.

zh: 每片皆代表一位女神，且曾一度施彩。
en: Each of these represented a goddess and had, at one time, been painted.

zh: 其中一尊躯体之残片见于公元前十五世纪遗存之中。
en: The body of one statue was found among remains dating from the fifteenth century B.

zh: 其缺失之首恰巧见于公元前五世纪遗存之中。
en: Its missing head happened to be among remains of the fifth century B.

zh: 此首极可能于古典时代即已出土并获精心保存。
en: This head must have been found in Classical times and carefully preserved.

zh: 彼时便已极为古老而珍贵。
en: It was very old and precious even then.

zh: 当考古学家将碎片拼合复原时，惊讶地发现这位女神竟呈颇具现代气息的女子形象。
en: When the archaeologists reconstructed the fragments, they were amazed to find 15 that the goddess turned out to be a very modern-looking woman.

zh: 像高三英尺，双手叉腰而立。
en: She stood three feet high and her hands rested on her hips.

zh: 身着曳地长裙。
en: She was wearing a full-length skirt which swept the ground.

zh: 尽管年代久远，其姿态仍极为优雅；然迄今为止，考古学家仍未能确知其身份。
en: Despite her great age, she was very graceful indeed, but, so far, the archaeologists have been unable to discover her identity.

zh: 如今，从事体力劳动的人往往远比坐办公室者挣钱为多。
en: These days, people who do manual work often receive far more money than people who work in offices.

zh: 坐办公室者常被称作「白领工人」，只因他们上班时多着衬衫系领带。
en: People who work in offices are frequently referred to as 'white-collar workers'for the simple reason that they usually wear a collar and tie to go to work.

zh: 人情大抵如此：许多人往往甘愿舍弃较高薪酬，以换取跻身白领之列。
en: Such is 5 human nature, that a great many people are often willing to sacrifice higher pay for the privilege of becoming white-collar workers.

zh: 于是可能出现种种离奇情形；在埃尔斯米尔公司当清道夫的阿尔弗雷德·布洛格斯便是一例。
en: This can give rise to curious situations, as it did in the case of Alfred Bloggs who worked as a dustman for the Ellesmere Corporation.

zh: 结婚时，阿尔夫羞于向妻子明言自己的职业。
en: When he got married, Alf was too embarrassed to say anything to his wife about his job.

zh: 他只告诉她自己在该公司供职。
en: He simply told 10 her that he worked for the Corporation.

zh: 每晨离家时，他身着一套体面的黑色西装。
en: Every morning, he left home dressed in a smart black suit.

zh: 继而换上工装，随后八小时充当清道夫。
en: He then changed into overalls and spent the next eight hours as a dustman.

zh: 夜间归家前，他淋浴后再换回西装。
en: Before returning home at night, he took a shower and changed back into his suit.

zh: 阿尔夫如此行事逾两年，同行清道夫亦为其保密。
en: Alf did this for over two years and his fellow dustmen kept his secret.

zh: 其妻始终不知自己嫁与一名清道夫，今后亦不会知晓，因阿尔夫刚觅得新职。
en: Alf's wife has never discovered that she married a dustman and she never will, for Alf has just found another job.

zh: 他即将坐进办公室工作。
en: He will soon be working in an office.

zh: 其收入将仅及昔日之半，然他认为社会地位之提升足抵金钱之损失。
en: He will be earning only half as much as he used to, but he feels that 15 his risein status is well worth the lossof money.

zh: 自此，他将终日西装革履，他人将称其「布洛格斯先生」，
en: From now on, he will wear a suit all day and others will call him‘Mr.

zh: 而非「阿尔夫」。
en: Bloggs’, not'Alf'.

zh: 稿件送到后，编辑读完首句便拒绝刊发。
en: 5 the article arrived, the editor read the first sentence and then refused to publish it.

zh: 文章开篇写道：「数百级台阶通向环绕总统府邸的高墙。」
en: The article began:'Hundreds of steps lead to the high wall which surrounds the president's palace.

zh: 编辑当即传真记者，命其查明台阶的确切数目及围墙高度。
en: 'The editor at once sent the journalist a fax instructing him to find out the exact number of steps and the height of the wall.

zh: 记者立即着手搜集此等要目，然迟迟未将结果发回。与此同时，编辑愈感不耐，因杂志即将付印。
en: The journalist immediately set out to obtain these important facts, but he took a long time to send them. 10 Meanwhile, the editor was getting impatient, for the magazine would soon go to press.

zh: 他又发去两份传真，皆未获答复。
en: He sent the journalist two more faxes, but received no reply.

zh: 再发一电，称若再不速复，将予解雇。
en: He sent yet another fax informing the journalist that if he did not reply soon he would be fired.

zh: 记者再度沉默后，编辑不得已按原稿刊出此文。
en: When the journalist again failed to reply, the editor reluctantly published the article as it had originally been written.

zh: 一周后，编辑终于收到记者传真。
en: A week later, the editor at last received a fax from the journalist.

zh: 可怜人非但遭拘捕，且已身陷囹圄。
en: Not only had the poor man been arrested, but he had been sent to prison as well.

zh: 不过，他终于获准发出一份传真，告知编辑他在清点通往环绕总统府十五英尺高墙的一千零八十四级台阶时被捕。
en: However, he had at last been 15 allowed to send a fax in which he informed the editor that he had been arrested while counting the 1,084 steps leading to the fifteen-foot wall which surrounded the president's palace.

zh: 皮卡迪利附近一条著名拱廊街上，高档店铺甫开门营业。
en: The expensive shops in a famous arcade near Piccadilly were just opening.

zh: 清晨此时分，拱廊内几乎空无一人。
en: At this time of the morning, the arcade was almost empty.

zh: 珠宝店老板泰勒先生正欣赏新布置的橱窗。
en: Taylor, the owner of a jewellery shop was admiring a new window display.

zh: 两名店员自八时起便忙碌不停，此刻方告一段落。
en: Two of his assistants had been working busily since eight o'clock and had only just finished.

zh: 钻石项链与戒指衬以黑色天鹅绒背景，陈列得极为雅致。
en: Diamond necklaces and rings had been beautifully arranged on a background of black velvet.

zh: 泰勒先生凝视橱窗数分钟后，
en: After gazing at the display for several minutes, Mr.

zh: 返回店内。
en: Taylor went back into his shop.

zh: 一辆大灯明亮、喇叭长鸣之大型轿车突然呼啸驶入拱廊，打破了寂静。
en: The silence was suddenly broken when a large car, with its headlights on and its horn blaring, roared 10 down the arcade.

zh: 车在珠宝店门外停下。
en: It came to a stop outside the jeweller's.

zh: 一人留在驾驶座，另两人以黑色长袜蒙面，持铁棍跃出，砸碎店铺橱窗。
en: One man stayed at the wheel while two others with black stockings over their faces jumped out and smashed the window of the shop with iron bars.

zh: 事发之际，泰勒先生
en: While this was going on, Mr.

zh: 正在楼上。
en: Taylor was upstairs.

zh: 他与店员开始向窗外投掷家具。
en: He and his staff began throwing furniture out of the window.

zh: 桌椅飞入拱廊之中。
en: Chairs and tables went flying into the arcade.

zh: 一名窃贼被沉重的雕像击中，然其只顾攫取钻石，竟未觉疼痛。
en: One of the thieves was struck by a heavy statue, but he was too busy helping himself to diamonds to notice any pain.

zh: 整个劫案历时仅三分钟，众人爬回车内，汽车以惊人速度驶离。
en: The raid was all over in three minutes, for the men scrambled 15 back into the car and it moved off at a fantastic speed.

zh: 车正欲离去之际，泰勒先生
en: Just as it was leaving, Mr.

zh: 冲出门外追赶，投掷烟灰缸与花瓶，然已无法拦住窃贼。
en: Taylor rushed out and ran after it throwing ashtrays and vases, but it was impossible to stop the thieves.

zh: 他们携价值数千英镑之钻石扬长而去。
en: They had got away with thousands of pounds worth of diamonds.

zh: 出现此类失误时大可不必灰心丧气（有此经历者亦不在少数）。
en: needn't despair when they make mistakes like this(and a lot of people do)!

zh: 所幸英格兰银行设有名为「残钞淑女」的小组，专门处理有人将钱喂给机器或交给狗啃等索赔事宜。
en: Fortunately for them, the Bank of England has a team called Mutilated Ladies which deals with claims from people who fed their money to a machine or to their dog.

zh: 看来狗酷爱把钱嚼碎。
en: Dogs, it seems, love to chew up money!

zh: 近有一案涉及简·巴特林，其未婚夫约翰经营一家颇为成功的家具店。
en: A recent case concerns Jane Butlin whose fiancé, John, runs a successful furniture business.

zh: 约翰某日生意顺遂，将装有三千英镑之钱包放入微波炉以求妥善保管。
en: John had a 10 very good day and put his wallet containing£3,000 into the microwave oven for safekeeping.

zh: 随后他与简一同骑马出游。
en: Then he and Jane went horse-riding.

zh: 归家后，简用微波炉做晚餐，不经意间亦将其未婚夫之钱包一并烹调。
en: When they got home, Jane cooked their dinner in the microwave oven and without realizing it, cooked her fiancé's wallet as well.

zh: 试想二人发现一只烹调精美的钱包与化为灰烬的钞票时之沮丧。
en: Imagine their dismay when they found a beautifully-cooked wallet and notes turned to ash!

zh: 约翰往见银行经理，经理将钱包残骸与钞票送至纽卡斯尔英格兰银行之专设部门——「残钞淑女」。
en: John went to see his bank manager who sent the remains of wallet and the money to the special department of the Bank of England in Newcastle: the Mutilated Ladies!

zh: 该处检视残骸后，约翰悉数领回款项。
en: They exam- ined the remains and John got all his money back.

zh: 「只要尚有可资辨认之残迹，我方便将款项退还。」一位银行女发言人称。
en: 'So long as there's something to identify, we will give people their money back,'said a spokeswoman for the Bank.

zh: 「去年我处就两万一千宗索赔支付了一百五十万英镑。」*受损钞票。
en: 'Last year, we paid f1.5m on 21,000 claims. *Damaged bank notes.

zh: 英国纸币印有女王头像，「淑女」一词即指此。
en: The Queen's head appears on English bank notes, and 'lady'refers to this.

zh: 大圣伯纳德的
en: The Great St.

zh: 山口连接瑞士与意大利。
en: Bernard Pass connects Switzerland to Italy.

zh: 该山口海拔二千四百七十三米，为欧洲海拔最高之山隘。
en: At 2,473 metres, it is the highest mountain pass in Europe.

zh: 著名的圣伯纳
en: The famous monastery of St.

zh: 修道院建于十一世纪，距此约一英里。
en: Bernard, which was founded in the eleventh century, lies about a mile away.

zh: 数百年来，圣伯纳
en: For hundreds of years, St.

zh: 犬曾拯救无数穿越该险要山口的旅人性命。
en: Bernard 5 dogs have saved the lives of travellers crossing the dangerous Pass.

zh: 这些性情温驯之犬最初自亚洲引入，早在罗马时代便用作看门犬。
en: These friendly dogs, which were first brought from Asia, were used as watchdogs even in Roman times.

zh: 山中隧道既已贯通，山口虽较昔日安全，然每年仍有旅人遇困，届时犬只仍被遣入雪中施救。
en: Now that a tunnel has been built through the mountains, the Pass is less dangerous, but each year, the dogs are stillsent out into the snow whenever a traveller is in difficulty.

zh: 尽管有了新隧道，仍有人贸然尝试徒步穿越该山口。
en: Despite the new tunnel, there are still a few people who rashly 10 attempt to cross the Pass on foot.

zh: 夏季数月间，修道院极为繁忙，因成千上万驾车穿越山口者前来参观。
en: During the summer months, the monastery is very busy, for it is visited by thousands of people who cross the Pass in cars.

zh: 因游人众多，犬只须关入专用围栏。
en: As there are so many people about, the dogs have to be kept in a special enclosure.

zh: 冬季则不然，修道院生活颇为不同。
en: In winter, however, life at the monastery is quite different.

zh: 气温可降至零下三十摄氏度，试图穿越山口者寥寥无几。
en: The temperature drops to-30°and very few people attempt to cross the Pass.

zh: 僧侣们偏爱冬季甚于夏季，因可多得清静。
en: The monks prefer winter to summer for they have more privacy.

zh: 犬只亦更为自由，获准在围栏外游荡。
en: The dogs have greater freedom, too, for they are allowed to wander outside their enclosure.

zh: 冬季定期来访者唯滑雪团体，他们于圣诞节与复活节前往该处。
en: The only regular visitors to the monas- tery in winter are parties of skiers who go there at Christmas and Easter.

zh: 这些热爱山间宁静的年轻人，在圣伯纳
en: These young people, who love the peace of the mountains, always receive a warm welcome at St.

zh: 修道院总是受到热情款待。
en: Bernard's monastery.

zh: 大多数猫终生对人类心存戒备。
en: Most cats remain suspicious of humans all their lives.

zh: 猫最令我们着迷之处之一，便是民间相信猫有九命。
en: One of the things that fascinates us most about cats is the popular belief that they have nine lives.

zh: 看来此说颇有道理。
en: Apparently, there is a good deal of truth in this idea.

zh: 猫自高处坠落仍能生还，确有事实依据。
en: A cat's ability to survive falls is based on fact.

zh: 纽约动物医疗中心近来对一百三十二只猫进行了为期五个月的研究。此等猫皆有一共同经历：自高层建筑坠落，然其中仅八只因休克或伤重而亡。
en: Recently the New York Animal Medical Centre made a study of 132 cats over a period of five months. 10 All these cats had one experience in common: they had fallen off high buildings, yet only eight of them died from shock or injuries.

zh: 当然，纽约乃开展此类有趣研究之理想所在，因高层建筑俯拾即是。
en: Of course, New York is the idealplace for such an interesting study, because there is no shortage of tall buildings.

zh: 可供坠落的高层窗台亦为数众多。
en: There are plenty of high-rise windowsills to fall from!

zh: 一只名萨布丽娜的猫自三十二层坠下，仅折断一颗牙齿。
en: One cat, Sabrina, fell 32 storeys, yet only suffered from a broken tooth.

zh: 「猫之举止宛若训练有素之伞兵。」一位医生说道。
en: 'Cats behave like well-trained paratroopers,'a doctor said.

zh: 似乎猫坠落得愈远，愈不易伤重。
en: It seems that the further cats fall, the less they are likely to injure themselves.

zh: 长距离坠落时，其速度可达每小时六十英里乃至更高。
en: In a long drop, they reach speeds 15 of 60 miles an hour and more.

zh: 高速下落之际，坠落的猫有时间放松肢体。
en: At high speeds, falling cats have time to relax.

zh: 它们如飞鼠般伸开四肢。
en: They stretch out their legs like flying squirrels.

zh: 此举增大空气阻力，着地时撞击之冲力亦随之减轻。
en: This increases their air-resistance and reduces the shock of impact when they hit the ground.

zh: 巨轮「泰坦尼克」号于一九一二年四月十日自南安普敦驶往纽约。
en: The great ship, Titanic, sailed for New York from Southampton on April 10th,1912.

zh: 船上载有一千三百一十六名乘客及八百九十一名船员。
en: She was carrying 1,316 passengers and a crew of 891.

zh: 即以今之标准衡之，四万六千吨之「泰坦尼克」号亦属庞然巨舰。
en: Even by modern standards, the 46,000 ton Titanic was a colossal ship.

zh: 然在当时，她不仅为有史以来最大之船，且因有十六间水密舱而被视为永不沉没。
en: At that time, however, she was not only the Iargest ship that had ever been built, but was regarded as unsinkable, for she had sixteen watertight compartments.

zh: 即使其中两舱进水，她仍能漂浮。
en: Even if two of these were flooded.she would stillbe able to float.

zh: 这艘巨轮之悲惨沉没将永载史册，因她首航即告沉没，人员损失惨重。
en: The tragic sinking of this great liner will always be remembered, for she went down on her first voyage with heavy loss of life.

zh: 启航四日后，「泰坦尼克」号航行于北大西洋冰冷水域之际，瞭望员突然发现一座巨大冰山。
en: Four days after setting out, while the Titanic was sailing across the icy waters of the North Atlantic, a huge iceberg was suddenly spotted by a lookout.

zh: 警报发出后，巨轮急转船首，以避免正面相撞。
en: After the alarm had been given, the great ship turned sharply to avoid a direct collision.

zh: 「泰坦尼克」号及时转向，险险避开船旁高出水面逾百英尺之巨大冰壁。
en: The Titanic turned just in time, narrowly missing theimmense wall of ice which rose over 100 feet out of the water beside her.

zh: 忽然，船下传来轻微震颤之声，船长下舱察看。
en: Suddenly, there was a slight trembling sound from below, and the captain went down to see what had happened.

zh: 声响如此微弱，无人以为船体已受损。
en: The noise had been so faint that no one thought that the ship had been damaged.

zh: 在舱下，船长惊骇地意识到「泰坦尼克」号正迅速下沉，因十六间水密舱中已有五间进水。
en: Below, the captain realized to his horror that the Titanic was sinking rapidly, for five of her sixteen watertight compartments had already been flooded!

zh: 弃船令下，数百人跃入刺骨海水。
en: The order to abandon ship was given and hundreds of people plunged into the icy water.

zh: 因救生艇不足，一千五百人罹难。
en: As there were not enough lifeboats for everybody,1,500 lives were lost.

zh: 如今海关人员已颇为宽容，然当你经绿色通道且无可申报物品时，他们仍可拦下你。
en: Customs Officers are quite tolerant these days, but they can still stop you when you are going through the Green Channel and have nothing to declare.

zh: 即便十分诚实之人，亦常被迫心生愧疚。
en: Even really honest people are often made to feel guilty.

zh: 反之，老练的职业走私犯纵有五百只金表藏于箱中，亦绝不会为此不安。
en: The hardened professional smuggler, on the other hand, 5 is never troubled by such feelings, even if he has five hundred gold watches hidden in his suitcase.

zh: 我近日自国外归来，一名格外官僚气十足的年轻海关官员显然将我视作走私者。
en: When I returned from abroad recently, a particularly officious young Customs Officer clearly regarded me as a smuggler.

zh: 「可有何物须申报？」
en: 'Have you anything to declare?

zh: 他直视我双目问道。
en: 'he asked, looking me in the eye.

zh: 「没有。」我自信地答道。
en: 'No,'I answered confidently.

zh: 「请打开此箱，可否？」
en: 'Would you mind unlocking this suitcase please?

zh: 「当然可以。」我答道。
en: ' 'Not at all,'I answered.

zh: 该官员极为仔细地检查箱子。
en: The Officer went through the case with great care.

zh: 我精心收拾的物件很快乱作一团。
en: All the things I had packed so carefully were soon in a dreadful mess.

zh: 我深恐再也无法将箱子合上。
en: I felt sure I would never be able to close the case again.

zh: 忽然，我见该官员面露喜色。
en: Suddenly, I saw the Officer's face 15 light up.

zh: 他在箱底发现一只小瓶，欣然一把抓起。
en: He had spotted a tiny bottle at the bottom of my case and he pounced on it with delight.

zh: 「香水，嗯？」
en: 'Perfume, eh?

zh: 他讥讽地问道。
en: 'he asked sarcastically.

zh: 「您本该申报。」
en: 'You should have declared that.

zh: 香水不在进口免税之列。
en: Perfume is not exempt from import duty.

zh: 「然此非香水。」我说道。
en: ' 'But it isn't perfume,'I said.

zh: 「乃发胶。」
en: 'It's hair gel.

zh: 我又微笑补白道：「乃自制之奇特混合物。」
en: 'Then I added with a smile,'It's a strange mixture I make myself.

zh: 不出所料，他并不相信。
en: ' As I expected, he did not believe me.

zh: 「请一试。」
en: 'Try it!

zh: 我鼓励道。
en: 'I said encouragingly.

zh: 该官员旋开瓶盖，将瓶凑近鼻孔。
en: The Officer unscrewed the cap and put the bottle to his nostrils.

zh: 一股难闻之气扑鼻而来，使他确信我所言不虚。
en: He was greeted by an unpleasant smell which convinced him that I was telling the truth.

zh: 数分钟后，我行李上已画上珍贵粉笔记号，得以匆匆离去。
en: A few minutes later, I was able to hurry away with precious chalk marks on my baggage.

zh: 回答下列有关荒岛生活的问题。
en: below. desert island?

zh: 我们大多对荒岛生活抱有不切实际之想象。
en: Most of us have formed an unrealistic picture of life on a desert island.

zh: 我们有时将荒岛想象为阳光永照之某种乐园。
en: We sometimes imagine a desert island to be a sort of paradise where the sun always shines.

zh: 彼处生活简朴而美好。
en: Life there is simple and good.

zh: 熟果自树上坠落，人无须劳作。
en: Ripe fruit falls from the trees and you never have to work.

zh: 图景之另一面则截然相反。
en: The other side of the picture is quite the opposite.

zh: 荒岛生活苦不堪言。
en: Life on a desert island is wretched.

zh: 人非饿死，便如鲁滨逊般苦候一艘永不来临之船。
en: You either starve to death or live like Robinson Crusoe, waiting for a boat which never comes.

zh: 两种图景或皆含几分真实，然我们中鲜有机会一探究竟。
en: Perhaps there is an element of truth in both these pictures, but few of us have had the opportunity to find out.

zh: 两名男子近日在一座珊瑚岛上度过五日，竟恨不能多留些时日。
en: Two men who recently spent five days on a coral island wished they had stayed there longer.

zh: 他们正将一艘严重受损之船自维尔京群岛驶往迈阿密修理。
en: They were taking a badly damaged boat from the Virgin Islands to Miami to have it repaired.

zh: 航行途中，船体开始下沉。
en: During the journey, their boat began to sink.

zh: 他们迅速将食物、火柴与数罐啤酒装入小橡皮艇，划行数英里横渡加勒比海，抵达一座小小珊瑚岛。
en: They quickly loaded a small rubber dinghy with food, matches, and cans of beer and rowed for a few miles across the Caribbean until they arrived at a tiny coral island.

zh: 岛上几乎无树亦无淡水，然此并未构成问题。
en: There were hardly any trees on the island and there was no water, but this did not prove to be a problem.

zh: 二人于橡皮艇中收集雨水。
en: The men collected rainwater in the rubber dinghy.

zh: 因携有鱼枪，食物亦颇充足。
en: As they had brought a spear gun with them, they had plenty to eat.

zh: 他们每日捕得龙虾与鱼；用其中一人之言，乃「如君王般饮食」。
en: They caught lobster and fish every day, and, as one of them put it 'ate like kings'.

zh: 五日后一艘油轮经过将他们救起，二人竟真心为不得不离去而遗憾。
en: When a passing tanker rescued them five days later, both men were genuinely sorry that they had to leave.

zh: 她装扮成鬼魂模样；因前夜已做好装束，便急欲试穿。
en: 5 up as a ghost and as she had made her costume the night before, she was impatient to try it on.

zh: 尽管装束不过一床单，效果却颇佳。
en: Though the costume consisted only of a sheet, it was very effective.

zh: 理查兹夫人穿上之后，
en: After putting it on, Mrs.

zh: 走下楼去。
en: Richards went downstairs.

zh: 她想试试穿着是否舒适。
en: She wanted to find out whether it would be comfortable She tried to explain the situation to wear.

zh: 正当理查兹夫人
en: Just as Mrs.

zh: 步入餐室之际，前门传来叩门声。
en: Richards was entering the dining room, there was a knock on the front door.

zh: 她知必是面包师傅。
en: She knew that 10 it must be the baker.

zh: 她曾吩咐他：若她未能开门，便请直入，将面包置于厨房桌上。
en: She had told him to come straight in if ever she failed to open the door and to leave the bread on the kitchen table.

zh: 不愿惊吓可怜人，理查兹夫人
en: Not wanting to frighten the poor man, Mrs.

zh: 迅即躲入楼梯下小储藏室。
en: Richards quickly hid in the small storeroom under the stairs.

zh: 她听见前门开启，门厅里传来沉重脚步声。
en: She heard the front door open and heavy footsteps in the hall.

zh: 储藏室门忽然打开，一名男子走了进来。
en: Suddenly the door of the storeroom was opened and a man entered.

zh: 理查兹夫人这才发现来者必是电力公司来抄表之人。
en: Richards realized that it must be the man from the Electricity Board who had come to read the metre.

zh: 她欲说明情形，连声道「不过是我罢了」，然为时已晚。
en: She tried to explain the situation, saying 'It's only me', 15 but it was too late.

zh: 那人惊叫一声，连退数步。
en: The man let out a cry and jumped back several paces.

zh: 当理查兹夫人
en: When Mrs.

zh: 向他走去时，他夺门而逃，砰然关门。
en: Richards walked towards him, he fled, slamming the door behind him.

zh: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
en: Listen to 听 录 音 ， below.

zh: 曾几何时，芝加哥店铺与商号之东主须向歹徒缴纳巨款，以换取所谓「保护」。
en: There was a time when the owners of shops and businesses in Chicago had to pay large sums of money to gangsters in return for 'protection'.

zh: 若款项不能如期交付，歹徒便迅即捣毁其店，使其关门歇业。
en: If the money was not paid promptly, the gangsters would quickly put a man out of business by destroying his shop.

zh: 榨取“保护费”并非始自今日之罪行。
en: Obtaining ‘protection money'is not a modern crime.

zh: 早在十四世纪，英国人约翰·霍克伍德爵士便得出一项值得注意的结论：人们宁愿付出大笔金钱，也不愿毕生心血毁于歹徒之手。
en: As long ago as the fourteenth century, an Englishman, Sir John Hawkwood, made the remarkable discovery that people would rather pay large sums of money than have their life work destroyed by gangsters.

zh: 六百年前，约翰·霍克伍德爵士率领一队士兵来到意大利，在佛罗伦萨近郊定居。
en: Six hundred years ago, Sir John Hawkwood arrived in Italy with a band of soldiers and settled near 10 Florence.

zh: 他很快便声名鹊起，意大利人遂以“乔瓦尼·阿库托”之名知其人。
en: He soon made a name for himself and came to be known to the Italians as Giovanni Acuto.

zh: 每当意大利各城邦彼此交战，霍克伍德便将其士兵雇与愿出高价之君主。
en: Whenever the Italian city-states were at war with each other, Hawkwood used to hire his soldiers to princes who were willing to pay the high price he demanded.

zh: 和平时期，生意清淡时，霍克伍德与其部下便开入某一城邦，焚毁若干农庄后，提出若向其缴纳保护费，即行撤离。
en: In times of peace, when business was bad, Hawkwood and his men would march into a city-state and, after burning down a few farms, would offer to go away if protection money was paid to them.

zh: 霍克伍德以此法聚敛巨款。
en: Hawkwood made large sums of money in this way.

zh: 尽管如此，意大利人仍视其为某种英雄。
en: In spite of this, the Italians regarded him as a sort of hero.

zh: 他年届八十辞世时，佛罗伦萨人为其举行国葬，并命人绘像一幅，以志“最勇武之将士、最卓著之统帅乔瓦尼·霍克伍德阁下”之纪念。
en: When he died at the age of eighty, the Florentines gave him a state funeral and had a picture painted which was dedicated to the memory of 'the most valiant soldier and most notable leader, Signor Giovanni Haukodue'.

zh: 孩童向来珍视所得之小额赠金。
en: Children always appreciate small gifts of money.

zh: 父母自当定期供给零用钱，而叔伯姑舅等亲属则常为额外收入之来源。
en: Mum or dad, of course, provide a regular supply of pocket money, but uncles and aunts are always a source of extra income.

zh: 对有些孩子而言，少许金钱亦可大有所用。
en: With some children, small sums go a long way.

zh: 若五十便士硬币未换作糖果，便会在储钱罐中叮当数月。
en: If fifty pence pieces are not exchanged for sweets, they rattle for months inside money boxes.

zh: 唯有十分节俭之孩童方能将储钱罐装满。
en: Only very thrifty children manage to fill up a money box.

zh: 对他们大多数人来说，为一大块可口巧克力付出五十便士，代价并不算高。
en: For most of them, fifty pence is a smallprice to pay for a nice big bar of chocolate.

zh: 吾侄乔治虽有储钱罐，却总是空空如也。
en: My nephew, George, has a money box but it is always empty.

zh: 我所赠其之五十便士硬币与一镑硬币，鲜有落入该罐者。
en: Very few of the fifty pence pieces and pound coins I have given him have found their way there.

zh: 昨日我予其五十便士，并劝其储蓄。
en: I gave him fifty pence yesterday and advised him 10 to save it.

zh: 孰料彼竟以五十便士换来一身麻烦。
en: Instead he bought himself fifty pence worth of trouble.

zh: 其赴糖果店途中失落该五十便士，钱币沿人行道弹跳，旋即坠入排水沟。
en: On his way to the sweet shop, he dropped his fifty pence and it bounced along the pavement and then disappeared down a drain.

zh: 乔治脱下外衣，卷起衣袖，将右臂伸入沟盖之下。
en: George took off his jacket, rolled up his sleeves and pushed his right arm through the drain cover.

zh: 其四处摸索，终未寻得该五十便士硬币；更有甚者，手臂亦无法抽出。
en: He could not find his fifty pence piece anywhere, and what is more, he could not get his arm out.

zh: 众人围拢而至，一妇人以肥皂与黄油涂抹其臂，然乔治仍被牢牢卡住。
en: A crowd of people gathered round him and a lady rubbed his arm with soap and butter, but George was firmly stuck.

zh: 消防队奉命赶到，两名消防员以特制润滑脂方将其解救。
en: The fire brigade was called and two fire fighters freed George using a special type of grease.

zh: 乔治对此经历并不十分懊恼，因糖果店女主人闻其遭遇，遂以一大盒巧克力相酬。
en: George was not too upset by his experience because the lady who owns the sweet shop heard about his troubles and rewarded him with a large box of chocolates.

zh: 玛丽与其夫迪米特里居于希腊南部佩拉霍拉小村。
en: Mary and her husband Dimitri lived in the tiny village of Perachora in southern Greece.

zh: 玛丽心爱之物中，有其夫所赠小白羊一头。
en: One of Mary's prize possessions was a little white lamb which her husband had given her.

zh: 白日里彼将其系于田间树上，每晚往取。然某夕，小羊竟不翼而飞。
en: She kept it tied to a tree in a field during the day and went to fetch it every evening. 5 One evening, however, the lamb was missing.

zh: 绳索已被割断，故显而易见，羊系遭窃。
en: The rope had been cut, so it was obvious that the lamb had been stolen.

zh: 迪米特里自田间归来，妻遂告以经过。
en: When Dimitri came in from the fields, his wife told him what had happened.

zh: 迪米特里当即动身寻访窃贼。
en: Dimitri at once set out to find the thief.

zh: 彼知在如此小村之中，此事当不难查明。
en: He knew it would not prove difficult in such a small village.

zh: 迪米特里向数位友人述及失窃之事后，得知邻人阿列科忽获小羊一头。
en: After telling several of his friends about the theft, Dimitri found out that his neighbour, Aleko, had 10 suddenly acquired a new lamb.

zh: 迪米特里立即前往阿列科宅中，怒斥其窃羊。
en: Dimitri immediately went to Aleko's house and angrily accused him of stealing the lamb.

zh: 彼告之须将羊归还，否则将报警。
en: He told him he had better return it or he would call the police.

zh: 阿列科否认取羊，并引迪米特里至其后院。
en: Aleko denied taking it and led Dimitri into his backyard.

zh: 彼解释道，确曾新购一羊，然其羊为黑色。
en: It was true that he hadjust bought a lamb, he explained, but his lamb was black.

zh: 迪米特里因行事鲁莽而深觉惭愧，遂为无端指控向阿列科致歉。
en: Ashamed of having acted so rashly, Dimitri apologized to Aleko for having accused him.

zh: 二人交谈之际，天忽降雨，迪米特里遂留于阿列科家中，直至雨止。
en: While they were talking it began to rain and Dimitri stayed in Aleko's house until the rain stopped.

zh: 半小时后彼出门，惊见那头小黑羊几乎已呈白色。
en: When he went outside half an hour later, he was astonished to find that the little black lamb was almost white.

zh: 其毛本经染黑，竟为雨水冲刷殆尽！
en: Its wool, which had been dyed black, had been washed clean by the rain!

zh: 固然今人普遍认为韦拉扎诺乃伟大探险家，其名或将永垂不朽，盖因一九六四年十一月二十一日，世上最长之悬索桥即以彼名命名。
en: 5 means considered to be a great explorer, his name will probably remain immortal, for on November 21st,1964, the longest suspension bridge in the world was named after him.

zh: 韦拉扎诺海峡大桥由奥斯马·阿曼设计，连接布鲁克林与斯塔滕岛。
en: The Verrazano Bridge, which was designed by Othmar Ammann, joins Brooklyn to Staten Island.

zh: 其主跨四千二百六十英尺。
en: It has a span of 4,260 feet.

zh: 桥身过长，故设计者须将地球之曲率纳入考量。
en: The bridge is so long that the shape of the earth had to be taken into account by its designer.

zh: 两座巨塔承托四条粗大钢缆。
en: Two great towers support four huge cables.

zh: 塔基建于由钢与混凝土筑成之巨大水下平台之上。
en: The towers are built on immense underwater platforms made of steel and concrete.

zh: 平台深入海底逾百英尺。
en: The platforms extend to a depth of over 100 feet under the sea.

zh: 仅此一项工程即历时十六月方告竣工。
en: These alone took sixteen months to build.

zh: 水面以上，双塔耸起，高达近七百英尺。
en: Above the surface of the water, the towers rise to a height of nearly 700 feet.

zh: 彼等承托悬吊桥身之钢缆。
en: They support the cables from which the bridge has been suspended.

zh: 四条钢缆各由二万六千一百零八股钢丝组成。
en: Each of the four cables contains 26,108 lengths of wire.

zh: 据估计，即便桥上挤满汽车，其荷载亦仅及总承载能力之三分之一。
en: It has been estimated that if the bridge were packed with cars, it would still only be carrying a third of its total capacity.

zh: 然此桥之要者，非独体量与强度而已。
en: However, size and strength are not the only important things about this bridge.

zh: 尽管体量宏大，其形却简雅兼备，实现了设计者欲造“至巨之物，而线条至淡”之夙愿。
en: Despite its immensity, it is both simple and elegant, fulfilling its designer's dream to create 'an enormous object drawn as faintly as possible’.

zh: 现代雕塑已罕能再令我们惊诧。
en: Modern sculpture rarely surprises us any more.

zh: 以为现代艺术唯于博物馆方可得见，此念实误。
en: The idea that modern art can only be seen in museums is mistaken.

zh: 即便对艺术毫无兴趣者，亦难不留意公共场所陈列之现代雕塑实例。
en: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture on display in public places.

zh: 奇形之物立于园中，以及楼厦商铺之外。
en: Strange forms stand in gardens, and outside buildings and shops.

zh: 吾等对此已颇为习惯。
en: We have got quite used to them.

zh: 若干所谓“现代”之作，陈列已近八十年。
en: Some so-called ‘modern'pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years.

zh: 尽管如此，近来一场现代雕塑展仍使一些人——本人亦在其中——感到意外。
en: In spite of this, some people—including myself—were surprised by a recent exhibition of modern sculp- ture.

zh: 我步入美术馆，首见一告示，上书：“请勿触摸展品。
en: The first thing I saw when I entered the art gallery was a notice which said:'Do not touch the exhibits.

zh: 其中部分具有危险性！
en: Some of them are dangerous!

zh: ”所陈列者为活动雕塑之作。
en: 'The objects on display were pieces of moving sculpture.

zh: 自天花板悬垂、随风而动之奇形构件，于众人并不陌生。
en: Oddly shaped forms that are suspended from the ceiling and move in response to a gust of wind are quite familiar to everybody.

zh: 然此处之物却有所不同。
en: These objects, however, were different.

zh: 沿墙排列者，有细长金属丝，下端系以金属球体。
en: Lined up against the wall, there were long thin wires attached to metal spheres.

zh: 球体经磁化，彼此恒相吸引或排斥。
en: The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other all the time.

zh: 展厅中央立有若干高塔状结构，内藏彩色灯光。
en: In thee centre of the hall, there were a number of tall structures which contained coloured lights.

zh: 灯光闪烁不息，宛若失常之交通信号灯。
en: These lights flickered continuously like traffic lights which have gone mad.

zh: 小黑盒中迸出电火花，红灯怒明忽灭。
en: Sparks were emitted from small black boxes and red lamps flashed on and off angrily.

zh: 颇似一场史前电子设备之展览。
en: It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment.

zh: 这些奇特造型似不仅意在从情感上震撼观者，亦欲施以电击！
en: These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well!

zh: 绑匪素少关注动物，然近日却对
en: Kidnappers are rarely interested in animals, but they recently took considerable interest in Mrs.

zh: 埃莉诺·拉姆齐夫人之猫产生了浓厚兴趣。
en: Eleanor Ramsay's cat.

zh: 埃莉诺·拉姆齐为极富之老妇，与其猫拉斯特斯同居一寓，历有年所。
en: Eleanor Ramsay, a very wealthy old lady, has shared a flat with her cat, Rastus, for a great many years.

zh: 拉斯特斯生活颇有规律。
en: Rastus leads an orderly life.

zh: 彼通常傍晚稍事散步，七时前必已归家。
en: He 5 usually takes a short walk in the evenings and is always home by seven o'clock.

zh: 然某夕，彼竟未按时返回。
en: One evening, however, he failed to arrive.

zh: 拉姆齐夫人焦虑万分。
en: Ramsay got very worried.

zh: 彼四处寻觅，终无所获。
en: She looked everywhere for him but could not find him.

zh: 拉斯特斯失踪三日后，
en: Three days after Rastus'disappearance, Mrs.

zh: 拉姆齐夫人收到一封匿名信。
en: Ramsay received an anonymous letter.

zh: 写信人声称拉斯特斯安然无恙，若拉姆齐夫人
en: The writer stated 10 that Rastus was in safe hands and would be returned immediately if Mrs.

zh: 支付一千英镑赎金，当即予以送还。
en: Ramsay paid a ransom of f1,000.

zh: 信中命彼将钱置于纸盒内，置于门外。
en: Ramsay was instructed to place the money in a cardboard box and to leave it outside her door.

zh: 初时彼决意报警，然恐再也见不到拉斯特斯——信中言之甚明——遂改变主意。
en: At first she decided to go to the police, but fearing that she would never see Rastus again—the letter had made that quite clear—she changed her mind.

zh: 彼自银行取出千镑，依绑匪指示行事。
en: She withdrew £1,000 from her bank and followed the kidnapper's in- structions.

zh: 翌晨，纸盒已不见踪影，然拉姆齐夫人
en: The next morning, the box had disappeared but Mrs.

zh: 深信绑匪必守诺言。
en: Ramsay was sure that the kidnapper would keep his word.

zh: 果然，是夕七时，拉斯特斯准时归来。
en: Sure enough, Rastus arrived punctually at seven o'clock that evening.

zh: 彼气色甚佳，然颇觉口渴，竟饮去半瓶牛乳。
en: He looked very well, though he was rather thirsty, for he drank half a bottle of milk.

zh: 警方闻拉姆齐夫人
en: The police were astounded when Mrs.

zh: 所述经过，大为愕然。
en: Ramsay told them what she had done.

zh: 彼解释称拉斯特斯于彼极为珍贵。
en: She explained that Rastus was very dear to her.

zh: 就其付出之金额而言，此“珍贵”一语，可谓一语双关！
en: Considering the amount she paid, he was dear in more ways than one!

zh: 一九〇八年，诺思克利夫子爵悬赏一千英镑，予首位飞越英吉利海峡者。
en: In 1908 Lord Northcliffe offered a prize of £1,000 to the first man who would fly across the English Channel.

zh: 逾一年之后，方有人首次尝试。
en: Overa year passed before the first attempt was made.

zh: 一九〇九年七月十九日清晨，于贝尔·拉瑟姆驾其“安托瓦内特四号”飞机自法国海岸起飞。
en: On July 19th,1909, in the early morning, Hubert Latham took off from the French coast in his plane the ‘Antoinette IV’.

zh: 彼飞越海峡仅七英里，发动机即告失灵，被迫降于海上。
en: He had travelled only seven miles across the Channel when his engine failed and he was forced to land on the sea.

zh: “安托瓦内特”号漂浮水面，直至拉瑟姆为一艘船救起。
en: The ‘Antoinette'floated on the water until Latham was picked up by a ship.

zh: 两日后，路易·布莱里奥驾一架编号为“十一号”之飞机抵加来附近。
en: Two days later, Louis Bleriot arrived near Calais with a plane called No.

zh: 布莱里奥自一九〇五年起制造飞机，此为其最新型号。
en: Bleriot had been making planes since 1905 and this was his latest model.

zh: 一周前，彼曾完成一次成功的陆上飞行，航程二十六英里。
en: A week before, he had completed a successful overland flight during which he covered twenty-six miles.

zh: 然拉瑟姆却未轻易罢休。
en: Latham, however, did not give up easily.

zh: 彼亦于同日驾一架新“安托瓦内特”抵加来附近。
en: He, too, arrived near Calais on the same day with a new'Antoinette'.

zh: 看来一场飞越海峡之激烈竞逐在所难免。
en: It looked as if there would be an exciting race across the Channel.

zh: 两机原定于七月二十五日起飞，然拉瑟姆未能早起。
en: Both planes were going to take off on July 25th, but Latham failed to get up early enough.

zh: 布莱里奥于凌晨四时十五分作短暂试飞后，半小时后即启程。
en: After making a short test flight at 4.15 a.m., Bleriot set off half an hour later.

zh: 彼之伟大飞行历时三十七分钟。
en: His great flight lasted thirty-seven minutes.

zh: 彼在多佛附近着陆时，首位迎上前去者为当地一名警察。
en: When he landed near Dover, the first person to greet him was a local policeman.

zh: 一周之后，拉瑟姆再度尝试，距多佛不足半英里，然命运再度与彼作对。
en: Latham made 15 another attempt a week later and got within half a mile of Dover, but he was unlucky again.

zh: 发动机再次失灵，彼第二次降于海上。
en: His engine failed and he landed on the sea for the second time.

zh: 二百年前，拳击赛于英格兰极受欢迎。
en: Boxing matches were very popular in England two hundred years ago.

zh: 当时拳手徒手相搏，以争奖金。
en: In those days, boxers fought with bare fists for prize money.

zh: 因此，彼等被称为“职业拳师”。
en: Because of this, they were known as 'prizefighters'.

zh: 然拳击颇为粗野，因无规则可循，职业拳师于比赛中或受重伤，甚或殒命。
en: However, boxing was very crude, for there were no rules and a prizefighter 5 could be seriously injured or even killed during a match.

zh: 拳击史上最富传奇色彩人物之一为丹尼尔·门多萨，生于一七六四年。
en: One of the most colourful figures in boxing history was Daniel Mendoza, who was born in 1764.

zh: 拳套之使用迟至一八六〇年方引入，时昆斯伯里侯爵拟定首套规则。
en: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensberry drew up the first set of rules.

zh: 门多萨虽技术上仍为职业拳师，然于将粗野之徒手搏击化为体育运动方面贡献良多，盖彼将科学引入此项竞技。
en: Though he was technically a prizefighter, Mendoza did much to change crude prizefighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game.

zh: 在其时代，门多萨享有极高声望。
en: In his day, Mendoza enjoyed 10 tremendous popularity.

zh: 贫富皆敬慕之。
en: He was adored by rich and poor alike.

zh: 门多萨年仅十四岁，经一场拳击赛后便声名鹊起。
en: Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only fourteen years old.

zh: 此事引起理查德·汉弗莱斯之注意，彼时彼为英格兰最负盛名之拳师。
en: This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England.

zh: 彼愿训练门多萨，其年轻弟子亦领悟颇速。
en: He offered to train Mendoza and his young pupil was quick to learm.

zh: 事实上，门多萨不久即大获成功，汉弗莱斯遂与彼反目。
en: In fact, Mendoza soon became so successful that Humphries turned against him.

zh: 二人激烈争吵，显而易见，唯有以拳赛方能了结。
en: The two men quarrelled bitterly and it was clear that the argument could only be settled 15 by a fight.

zh: 比赛于斯蒂尔顿举行，二人相搏达一小时之久。
en: A match was held at Stilton, where both men fought for an hour.

zh: 公众于门多萨身上下注甚巨，然彼终败北。
en: The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza, but he was defeated.

zh: 门多萨后于拳台再遇汉弗莱斯，再度落败。
en: Mendoza met Humphries in the ring on a later occasion and he lost for a second time.

zh: 直至一七九〇年第三度交手，彼方终胜汉弗莱斯，荣膺英格兰拳王。
en: It was not until his third match in 1790 that he finally beat Humphries and became Champion of England.

zh: 其间，彼创办一所极为成功之学院，连拜伦勋爵亦曾为其弟子。
en: Meanwhile, he founded a highly successful Academy and even Lord Byron became one of his pupils.

zh: 彼赚取巨款，单次出场酬金高达一百英镑。
en: He earned enormous sums of money and was paid as much as f100 for a single appear- 20 ance.

zh: 尽管如此，彼挥霍无度，故始终负债累累。
en: Despite this, he was so extravagant that he was always in debt.

zh: 彼败于号称“绅士杰克逊”之拳手后，旋即为人淡忘。
en: After he was defeated by a boxer called Gentleman Jackson, he was quickly forgotten.

zh: 彼因无力偿债入狱，于一八三六年贫病而终。
en: He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts and died in poverty in 1836.

zh: 有些剧目大获成功，竟连演数年不绝。
en: Some plays are so successful that they run for years on end.

zh: 从多方面观之，此事于那些须夜夜重复相同台词之可怜演员而言，实属不幸。
en: In many ways, this is unfortunate for the poor actors who are required to go on repeating the same lines night after night.

zh: 人当以为彼等台词早已烂熟于心，绝无支吾之理。
en: One would expect them to know their parts by heart and never have cause to falter.

zh: 然事实并非总是如此。
en: Yet this is not always the case.

zh: 某极成功剧目中，一著名演员曾饰演一贵族，该贵族于巴士底狱中被囚二十载。
en: A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.

zh: 末幕中，狱卒必持一信登台，交予囚犯。
en: In the last act, a gaoler would always come on to the stage with a letter which he would hand to the prisoner.

zh: 虽每场均须宣读此信，该贵族却坚持信文须全文写出。
en: Even though the noble was expected to read the letter at each performance, he always insisted that it should be written out in full.

zh: 某夜，狱卒决意戏弄同僚，欲知经如此多场次之后，彼是否已将信之内容背熟。
en: One night, the gaoler decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if, after so many performances, he had managed to learn the contents of the letter by heart.

zh: 大幕升起，末幕开场，只见那贵族独坐幽暗牢房栏后。
en: The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.

zh: 恰于此时，狱卒手捧珍贵信函现身。
en: Just then, the gaoler appeared with the precious letter in his hands.

zh: 彼进入牢房，将信呈予贵族。
en: He entered the cell and presented the letter to the aristocrat.

zh: 然此次所递之抄本并未如往常般写就全文。
en: But the copy he gave him had not been written out in full as usual.

zh: 不过是一张白纸。
en: It was simply a blank sheet of paper.

zh: 狱卒热切旁观，急于知晓同台演员是否终于记熟台词。
en: The gaoler looked on eagerly, anxious to see if his fellow actor had at last learnt his lines.

zh: 贵族凝视白纸数秒。
en: The noble stared at the blank sheet of paper for a few seconds.

zh: 继而眯起双眼，说道：“光线昏暗。
en: Then, squinting his eyes, he said:"The light is dim.

zh: 请为我宣读此信。
en: Read the letter to me.

zh: ”随即将纸片递与狱卒。
en: 'And he promptly handed the sheet of paper to the gaoler.

zh: 狱卒亦一字记不得，遂答道：“陛下，光线确甚昏暗。
en: Finding that he could not remember a word of the letter either, the gaoler replied:"The light is indeed dim, sire.

zh: 须取眼镜来。
en: I must get my glasses.

zh: ”言毕，匆匆下场。
en: 'With this, he hurried off the stage.

zh: 狱卒片刻后持眼镜与惯常之信稿返回，向囚犯宣读，贵族见状大觉有趣。
en: Much to the aristocrat’s amusement, the gaoler returned a few moments later with a pair of glasses and the usual copy of the letter which he proceeded to read to the prisoner.

zh: 何物可食，何物不可食。
en: eaten and what cannot be eaten.

zh: 例如，若君居于地中海沿岸，必视章鱼为珍馐。
en: If you lived in the Mediterranean, for instance, you would consider octopus a great delicacy.

zh: 君或难以理解，何以有人视之可厌。
en: You would not be able to understand why some people find it repulsive.

zh: 反之，若以动物油脂炸土豆——此于北欧多国乃寻常做法——君或一想便觉反胃。
en: On the other hand, your stomach would turn at the idea of frying potatoes in animal fat—the normally accepted practice in many northern countries.

zh: 可悲之实情是，吾人大抵自幼被教导食用某些食物，遂终身固守此习。
en: The sad truth is that most of us have been brought up to eat certain foods and we stick to them all our lives.

zh: 世间受誉与遭贬最甚者，莫过于寻常园蜗。
en: No creature has received more praise and abuse than the common garden snail.

zh: 以酒烹之，蜗牛于世界诸多地区皆为豪奢之味。
en: Cooked in wine, snails are a great luxury in various parts of the world.

zh: 无数人自幼便习于将蜗牛与食物相联系。
en: There are countless people who, ever since their early years, have learned to associate snails with food.

zh: 吾友罗伯特居于一蔑视蜗牛之国。
en: My friend, Robert, lives in a country where snails are despised.

zh: 因其寓所在大城之中，故无自家花园。
en: As his flat is in a large town, he has no garden of his own.

zh: 多年来彼屡请吾自花园中收集蜗牛携往相赠。
en: For years he has been asking me to collect snails from my garden and take them to him.

zh: 此事于吾向来不甚诱人，然一日暴雨过后，吾偶于园中漫步，见无数蜗牛正于若干珍贵植物上爬行。
en: The idea never appealed to me very much, but one day, after a heavy shower, I happened to be walking in my garden when I noticed a huge number of snails taking a strollon some of my prize plants.

zh: 吾一时冲动，收集数十只，装入纸袋，携往罗伯特处。
en: Acting on a sudden impulse, I ollected several dozen, put them in a paper bag, and took them to Robert.

zh: 罗伯特见吾大喜，对这份小礼物亦同样满意。
en: Robert was delighted to see me and equally pleased with my little gift.

zh: 吾将纸袋留于门厅，与罗伯特入客厅交谈，历两小时之久。
en: I left the bag in the hall and Robert and I went into the living room where we talked for a couple of hours.

zh: 吾已全然忘却蜗牛一事，罗伯特忽言吾务必留下晚餐。
en: I had forgotten all about the snails when Robert suddenly said that I must stay to dinner.

zh: 蜗牛自当为主菜。
en: Snails would, of course, be the main dish.

zh: 吾对此并不向往，然仍勉强随罗伯特离室。
en: I did not fancy the idea and I reluctantly followed Robert out of the room.

zh: 令我二人惊愕者，门厅中处处皆是蜗牛：彼等自纸袋逃出，竟已占据整个门厅！
en: To our dismay, we saw that there were snails everywhere: they had escaped from the paper bag and had taken complete possession of the hall!

zh: 自那以后，吾再不能正视蜗牛。
en: I have never been able to look at a snail since then.

zh: 每户人家皆有某种可怕秘密，多年来对外人讳莫如深。
en: family has some terrible secret which has been concealed from strangers for years.

zh: 英语中有一句生动俗语，用以形容此类情形。
en: The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation.

zh: 这可怕秘密被称为“柜中骷髅”。
en: The terrible secret is called‘a skeleton in the cupboard’.

zh: 故事行至某一戏剧性时刻，可怕秘密败露，声名亦随之扫地。
en: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined.

zh: 读者读至小说末页，得知女主人公——那位向来善待众人之慈祥老妇——年轻时竟先后毒杀五任丈夫，不禁毛骨悚然。
en: The reader's hair stands on end when he reads in the final pages of the novel that the heroine, a dear old lady who had always been so kind to everybody, had, in her youth, poisoned every one of her five husbands.

zh: 此类情节出现于虚构作品中，固无不可。
en: It is all very wellfor such things to occur in fiction.

zh: 吾人或多或少皆有不愿最密友知晓之秘密，然真正藏有“柜中骷髅”者却寥寥无几。
en: To varying degrees, we all have secrets which we do not want even our closest friends to learn, but few of us have skeletons in the cupboard.

zh: 吾所知唯一藏有柜中骷髅者为乔治·卡尔顿，且彼对此颇以为傲。
en: The only person I know who has a skeleton in the cupboard is George Carlton, and he is very proud of the fact.

zh: 乔治年轻时曾习医。
en: George studied medicine in his youth.

zh: 然彼未行医，而成为成功的侦探小说作家。
en: Instead of becoming a doctor, however, he became a successful writer of detective stories.

zh: 吾曾于彼宅中度过一个局促难安、终生难忘之周末。
en: I once spent an uncomfortable weekend which I shall never forget at his house.

zh: 乔治引吾至客房，称该室罕有人居。
en: George showed me to the guestroom which, he said, was rarely used.

zh: 彼嘱吾安顿行李后下楼用餐。
en: He told me to unpack my things and then come down to dinner.

zh: 吾将衬衣与内衣叠入两只空抽屉后，决意将随身两套西装之一挂入柜中。
en: After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard.

zh: 吾拉开柜门，呆立柜前，如遭雷击。
en: I opened the cupboard door and then stood in front of it petrified.

zh: 一具骷髅悬于眼前。
en: A skeleton was dangling before my eyes.

zh: 柜门骤动，骷髅微微摇晃，令吾几以为其将扑面跃出。
en: The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me.

zh: 吾掷下西装，飞奔下楼告知乔治。
en: Dropping my suit, I dashed downstairs to tell George.

zh: 此较诸“可怕秘密”更甚——此乃货真价实之骷髅！
en: This was worse than ‘a terrible secret'; this was a real skeleton!

zh: 然乔治却无动于衷。
en: But George was unsympathetic.

zh: “哦，那个呀，”彼微笑而言，宛若谈论一老友。
en: "Oh, that,'he said with a smile as if he were talking about an old friend.

zh: “那是塞巴斯蒂安。
en: "That's Sebastian.

zh: 君忘矣，吾亦曾为医科学生。
en: You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.

zh: 十九世纪最著名帆船之一“卡蒂萨克号”，今日仍可见于格林尼治。
en: One of the most famous sailing ships of the nineteenth century, the Cutty Sark, can still be seen at Greenwich.

zh: 其立于旱地之上，每年有成千上万游人前来参观。
en: She stands on dry land and is visited by thousands of people each year.

zh: 其令人肃然想起昔日巨舰之辉煌。
en: She serves as an impressive reminder of the great ships of the past.

zh: 在蒸汽船取代帆船之前，诸如“卡蒂萨克号”之帆船曾用于自中国运茶、自澳大利亚运羊毛。
en: Before they were replaced by steamships, sailing vessels like the Cutty Sark were used to carry tea from China and wool from Australia.

zh: “卡蒂萨克号”为有史以来建造之最快帆船之一。
en: The Cutty Sark was one of the fastest sailing ships that has ever been built.

zh: 唯一能与之匹敌者为“瑟莫皮莱号”。
en: The only other ship to match her was the Thermopylae.

zh: 一八七二年六月十八日，两船同自上海启航，展开一场激动人心的竞速，驶往英格兰。
en: Both these ships set out from Shanghai on June 18th,1872 on an exciting race to England.

zh: 此赛历时整整四月，乃此类竞赛之绝唱。
en: This race, which went on for exactly four months, was the last of its kind.

zh: 它标志着大帆船伟大传统之终结，亦开启一崭新时代。
en: It marked the end of the great 10 tradition of ships with sails and the beginning of a new era.

zh: 开赛之后，率先抵爪哇者为“瑟莫皮莱号”，然在印度洋上，“卡蒂萨克号”反超领先。
en: The first of the two ships to reach Java after the race had begun was the Thermopylae, but on the Indian Ocean, the Cutty Sark took the lead.

zh: 看来彼必为首先抵家之船，然赛程中厄运频仍。
en: It seemed certain that she would be the first ship home, but during the race she had a lot of bad luck.

zh: 八月间，巨风暴袭船，舵叶竟被撕脱。
en: In August, she was struck by a very heavy storm during which her rudder was torn away.

zh: “卡蒂萨克号”左右颠簸，已无法操舵。
en: The Cutty Sark rolled from side to side and it became impossible to steer her.

zh: 船员以备用木板临时制成一舵，安装时极为艰难。
en: A temporary rudder was made on board from spare planks and it was fitted with great difficulty.

zh: 此举大减船速，盖若行速过快，此临时舵亦有被撕脱之虞。
en: This greatly reduced the speed of the ship, for there was a danger that if she travelled too quickly, this rudder would be torn away as well.

zh: 因此，“卡蒂萨克号”失去领先优势。
en: Because of this, the Cutty Sark lost her lead.

zh: 越过赤道后，船长入港换装新舵，然此时“瑟莫皮莱号”已领先逾五百英里。
en: After crossing the Equator, the captain called in at a port to have a new rudder fitted, but by now the Thermopylae was over five hundred miles ahead.

zh: 虽新舵以惊人速度装妥，“卡蒂萨克号”已绝无胜算。
en: Though the new rudder was fitted at tremendous speed, it was impossible for the Cutty Sark to win.

zh: 她在“塞莫皮莱”号抵达英国一周之后也到了英国。
en: She arrived in England a week after the Thermopylae.

zh: 即便如此亦属难得，须知她途中屡遭耽搁。
en: Even this was remarkable, considering that she had had so many delays.

zh: 毋庸置疑，倘若她不曾失落舵板，本可轻而易举赢得比赛。
en: There is no doubt that if she had not lost her rudder she would have won the race easily.

zh: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
en: Listen to the tape then answer the question below.

zh: 无人能免于广告之影响。
en: No one can avoid being influenced by advertisements.

zh: 尽管我们或许以鉴赏力自诩，却已不能自由择取所欲之物，盖因广告于我们施以潜移默化之作用。
en: Much as we may pride ourselves on our good taste, we are no longer free to choose the things we want, for advertising exerts a subtle influence on us.

zh: 为劝诱我等购置此物或彼物，广告商悉心探究人性，并将我等种种细微弱点分门别类。
en: In their efforts to persuade us to buy this or that product, advertisers have made a close study of human nature and have classified all our little weaknesses.

zh: 广告商多年前即已发现，世人皆喜不劳而获。
en: Advertisers discovered years ago that all of us love to get something for nothing.

zh: 以“免费”这一神奇字眼开篇之广告，鲜有失手。
en: An advertisement which begins with the magic word FREE can rarely go wrong.

zh: 而今广告商不仅馈赠试用品，更有汽车、住宅乃至环球旅行之“免费”厚礼。
en: These days, advertisers not only offer free samples, but free cars, free houses, and free trips round the world as well.

zh: 彼等设计数以百计之竞赛，使我等得以赢取巨款。
en: They devise hundreds of competitions which will enable us to win huge sums of money.

zh: 无线电与电视使广告商得以借此攫取千万人之注意。
en: Radio and television have made it possible for advertisers to capture the attention of millions of people in this way.

zh: 某次广播节目中，一家饼干制造商曾请听众烤制饼干并寄往其厂。
en: During a radio programme, a company of biscuit manufacturers once asked listeners to bake biscuits and send them to their factory.

zh: 彼等愿以每磅十美元之价，收购听众所制最大之饼干。
en: They offered to pay $10 a pound for the biggest biscuit baked by a listener.

zh: 此次竞赛反响极为热烈。
en: The response to this competition was tremendous.

zh: 未几，形形色色、大小不一之饼干便陆续运抵工厂。
en: Beforé long, biscuits of all shapes and sizes began arriving at the factory.

zh: 一位女士竟以独轮车推来一块饼干。
en: One lady brought in a biscuit on a wheelbarrow.

zh: 其重近五百磅。
en: It weighed nearly 500 pounds.

zh: 稍后，又有一男子驱车而至，所携饼干占满车尾行李箱。
en: A little later, a man came along with a biscuit which occupied the whole boot of his car.

zh: 所有寄来之饼干皆经仔细称量。
en: All the biscuits that were sent were carefully weighed.

zh: 最大者重七百一十三磅。
en: The largest was 713 pounds.

zh: 似已笃定可获头奖。
en: It seemed certain that this would win the prize.

zh: 然竞赛即将截止之际，一辆卡车驶入工厂，载来一块重达二千四百磅之巨饼，当真骇人。
en: But just before the competition closed, a lorry arrived at the factory with a truly colossal biscuit which weighed 2,400 pounds.

zh: 此饼出自一名大学生之手，彼用面粉逾千磅、糖八百磅、油脂二百磅，另加各类配料四百磅。
en: It had been baked by a college student who had used over 1,000 pounds of flour,800 pounds of sugar,200 pounds of fat, and 400 pounds of various other ingredients.

zh: 其沉重至须动用起重机方能自车上卸下。
en: It was so heavy that a crane had to be used to remove it from the lorry.

zh: 制造商所付之款超出原先预料，盖因彼等自该生处购得此饼，出价二万四千美元。
en: The manufacturers had to pay more money than they had anticipated, for they bought the biscuit from the student for $24,000.

zh: 常言道，人皆赖出售某物以谋生。
en: It has been said that everyone lives by selling something.

zh: 据此言之，教师以兜售知识为生，哲人以兜售智慧为生，牧师则以兜售心灵慰藉为生。
en: In the light of this statement, teachers live by selling knowledge, philosophers by selling wisdom and priests by selling spiritual comfort.

zh: 物质财货之价值或可折算为金钱，然欲估量他人为我等所尽效劳之真正价值，则极为困难。
en: Though it may be possible to measure the value of material goods in terms of money, it is extremely difficult to estimate the true value of the services which people perform for us.

zh: 有时我等愿倾尽所有以换性命，却于外科医师提供恰是此项效劳而索高价时，心有不甘。
en: There are times when we would willingly give everything we possess to save our lives, yet we might grudge paying a surgeon a high fee for offering us precisely this service.

zh: 社会情状如此，技艺之酬须如店铺购物般给付。
en: The conditions of society are such that skills have to be paid for in the same way that goods are paid for at a shop.

zh: 人皆有物可售。
en: Everyone has something to sell.

zh: 流浪者似为此通则之唯一例外。
en: Tramps seem to be the only exception to this general rule.

zh: 乞丐几以自身为人货而售，以激起路人怜悯。
en: Beggars almost sell themselves as human beings to arouse the pity of passers-by.

zh: 然真正之流浪者并非乞丐。
en: But real tramps are not beggars.

zh: 彼等无所可售，亦不求于人。
en: They have nothing to sell and require nothing from others.

zh: 追求独立之际，彼等并不牺牲人格尊严。
en: In seeking independence, they do not sacrifice their human dignity.

zh: 流浪者或向汝乞钱，却绝不会求汝怜悯彼。
en: A tramp may ask you for money, but he will never ask you to feel sorry for him.

zh: 彼乃刻意择取此种生活，且深知其后果。
en: He has deliberately chosen to lead the life he 15 leads and is fully aware of the consequences.

zh: 彼或难料下一餐从何而来，却免于困扰众人万千焦虑。
en: He may never be sure where the next meal is coming from, but he is free from the thousands of anxieties which afflict other people.

zh: 其随身物甚少，故得轻松辗转各地。
en: His few material possessions make it possible for him to move from place to place with ease.

zh: 因须露宿旷野，彼较我等大多数人更为亲近自然。
en: By having to sleep in the open, he gets far closer to the world of nature than most of us ever do.

zh: 彼或偶以狩猎、乞讨或偷窃维生；在真正困窘之时，或亦稍事劳作；然绝不牺牲自由。
en: He may hunt, beg, or steal occasionally to keep himself alive; he may even, in times of real need, do a little work; but he will never sacrifice his freedom.

zh: 我等常轻蔑谈及流浪者，将其与乞丐混为一谈，然试问有几人能坦言，未尝对其简朴生活与无忧无虑稍生艳羡？
en: We often speak of 20 tramps with contempt and put them in the same class as beggars, but how many of us can honestly say that we have not felt a little envious of their simple way of life and their freedom from care?

zh: 当巨轮驶入港湾之际，满载货物之小艇疾驶而至。
en: Small boats loaded with wares sped to the great liner as she was entering the harbour.

zh: 巨轮尚未下锚，小艇上之人已攀上甲板，顷刻间甲板便铺满波斯之绚丽地毯、印度之丝绸、铜制咖啡壶与精美手工银器。
en: Before she had anchored, the men from the boats had climbed on board and the decks were soon covered with colourful rugs from Persia, silks from India, copper coffee pots, and beautiful handmade silverware.

zh: 欲不动心殊非易事。
en: It was difficult not to be tempted.

zh: 船上诸多游客已与商贩讨价还价，然吾决意待离船后再行购买。甫一下船，便有一人上前纠缠，欲向吾兜售钻戒。
en: Many of the tourists on board had begun bargaining with the tradesmen, but I decided not to buy anything until I had disembarked. colourful rugs from Persia I had no sooner got off the ship than I was assailed by a man who wanted to sell me a diamond ring.

zh: 吾本无购买之意，然难掩对钻石硕大之印象。
en: I had 10 no intention of buying one, but I could not conceal the fact that I was impressed by the size of the diamonds.

zh: 其中数颗大如弹珠。
en: Some of them were as big as marbles.

zh: 此人费尽周章以证钻石为真。
en: The man went to great lengths to prove that the diamonds were real.

zh: 途经一店时，彼将一钻石紧抵橱窗，于玻璃上压出深痕。
en: As we were walking past a shop, he held a diamond firmly against the window and made a deep impression in the glass.

zh: 吾费时逾半时辰方摆脱彼。
en: It took me over half an hour to get rid of him.

zh: 次一上前者兜售名贵钢笔与手表。
en: The next man to approach me was selling expensive pens and watches.

zh: 吾细察其中一支钢笔。
en: I examined one of the pens closely.

zh: 其外观确似真品。
en: It certainly looked genuine.

zh: 于金笔帽底部，镌有“美国制造”字样。
en: At the base of the gold cap, the words 'made in the U.

zh: 字迹工整清晰。
en: 'had been neatly inscribed.

zh: 此人称该笔值五十英镑，然为特别关照，愿以三十英镑售予吾。
en: The man said that the pen was worth f50, but as a special favour, he would let me have it for f30.

zh: 吾摇头，竖起五指，示愿付五英镑。
en: I shook my head and held up five fingers indicating that I was willing to pay f5.

zh: 此人手势夸张，佯装怒不可遏，终将价降至十英镑。
en: Gesticulating wildly, the man acted as if he found my offer outrageous, but he eventually reduced the price to f10.

zh: 吾耸肩欲去，片刻之后彼追将上来，将笔塞入吾手。
en: Shrug- ging my shoulders, I began to walk away when, a moment later, he ran after me and thrust the pen into my hands.

zh: 虽仍频频摊臂作绝望状，彼却欣然收下吾所付之五英镑。
en: Though he kept throwing up his arms in despair, he readily accepted the f5I gave him.

zh: 吾于这笔绝妙买卖颇感得意——直至返回船上。
en: I felt espe- cially pleased with my wonderful bargain—until I got back to the ship.

zh: 无论吾如何用力，此精美之笔竟无法灌入墨水，迄今一字未书！
en: No matter how hard I tried, it was impossible to fill this beautiful pen with ink and to this day it has never written a single word!

zh: 一则笑话是否可笑，大抵取决于我等成长之地。
en: Whether we find a joke funny or not largely depends on where we have been brought up.

zh: 幽默感与民族性格神秘地交织一处。
en: The sense of humour is mysteriously bound up with national characteristics.

zh: 例如，法国人或觉俄国笑话难以发噱。
en: A Frenchman, for instance, might find it hard to laugh at a Russian joke.

zh: 同理，俄国人或于一则足令英国人笑出眼泪之笑话中，不见丝毫趣味。
en: In the same way, a Russian might fail to see anything amusing in a joke which would make an Englishman laugh to tears.

zh: 多数滑稽故事皆基于喜剧性情境。
en: Most funny stories are based on comic situations.

zh: 尽管民族各异，某些滑稽情境仍具普遍之吸引力。
en: In spite of national differences, certain funny situations have a universal appeal.

zh: 无论居处何方，欲不对卓别林早期影片之类忍俊不禁，殆非易事。
en: No matter where you live, you would find it difficult not to laugh at, say, Charlie Chaplin's early films.

zh: 然而，一种新型幽默，其主要发源于
en: However, a new type of humour, which stems largely from the U.

zh: 美国，近来已蔚为风尚。
en: S., has recently come into fashion.

zh: 人称“病态幽默”。
en: It is called 'sick humour'.

zh: 喜剧演员以暴亡、严重事故等悲剧性情境为笑料。
en: Comedians base their jokes on tragic situations like violent death or serious accidents.

zh: 多人觉此类笑话令人反感。
en: Many people find this sort of joke distasteful.

zh: 下举“病态幽默”之一例，供君自行判断。
en: The following example of 'sick humour'will enable you to judge for yourself.

zh: 一男子摔断右腿，于圣诞节前数周被送往医院。
en: A man who had broken his right leg was taken to hospital a few weeks before Christmas.

zh: 自入院起，彼便不断纠缠医师，询问何时可归家。
en: From the moment he arrived there, he kept on pestering his doctor to tell him when he would be able to go home.

zh: 彼极惧须在院中度过圣诞。
en: He dreaded having to spend Christmas in hospital.

zh: 虽医师尽心竭力，病人康复仍缓。
en: Though the doctor did his best, the patient's recovery was slow.

zh: 圣诞日，该男子右腿仍裹石膏。
en: On Christmas Day, the man stillhad his right leg in plaster.

zh: 彼卧床度日，郁郁寡欢，念及所失诸般乐趣。
en: He spent a miserable day in bed thinking of all the fun he was missing.

zh: 然翌日医师慰之曰，彼及时出院以迎新岁庆典之望颇佳。
en: The following day, however, the doctor consoled him by telling him that his chances of being able to leave hospital in time for New Year celebrations were good.

zh: 男子乃振起精神，果如其言，除夕彼竟能蹒跚赴宴。
en: The man took heart and, sure enough, on New Year's Eve he was able to hobble along to a party.

zh: 为弥补住院之不快经历，彼饮酒稍过量。
en: To compensate for his unpleas- ant experiences in hospital, the man drank a little more than was good for him.

zh: 席间彼尽兴畅怀，且不断向众人宣称自己如何憎恶医院。
en: In the process, he enjoyed himself thoroughly and kept telling everybody how much he hated hospitals.

zh: 宴会将终，彼仍喃喃自语医院之事，忽踩冰块滑倒，摔断左腿。
en: He was still mumbling some- thing about hospitals at the end of the party when he slipped on a piece of ice and broke his left leg.

zh: 多年来，村民皆信恩德利农场闹鬼。
en: For years, villagers believed that Endley Farm was haunted.

zh: 该农场为乔·考克斯与鲍勃·考克斯兄弟二人所共有。
en: The farm was owned bytwo brothers, Joe and Bob Cox.

zh: 彼等雇有数名雇工，然无人愿久留彼处。
en: They employed a few farmhands, but no one was willing to work there long.

zh: 每有工人辞工，所述故事如出一辙。
en: Every time a worker gave up his job, he told the same story.

zh: 农场劳工称，彼等每于醒来时发现夜间活计已毕。
en: Farm labourers said that they always woke up to find that work had been done overnight.

zh: 干草已割，牛棚已扫。
en: Hay had been cut and cowsheds had been cleaned.

zh: 一雇工彻夜未眠，自称曾见月下有人影刈麦。
en: A farm worker, who stayed up allnight, claimed to have seen a figure cutting corn in the moonlight.

zh: 久而久之，考克斯兄弟雇有一尽职鬼魂代其劳作大半，竟成公认之事。
en: In time, it became an accepted fact that the Cox brothers employed a conscientious ghost that did most of their work for them.

zh: 无人疑农场尚有从未露面者。
en: No one suspected that there might be someone else on the farm who had never been seen.

zh: 事实确系如此。
en: This was indeed the case.

zh: 不久前，村民惊闻恩德利之“鬼魂”已逝。
en: A short time ago, villagers were astonished to learn that the ghost of Endley had died.

zh: 众人皆往送葬，因该“鬼魂”非他，乃埃里克·考克斯——本被认为少年时即已夭折之第三弟。
en: Everyone went to the funeral, for the ‘ghost'was none other than Eric Cox, a third brother who was supposed to have died as a young man.

zh: 葬礼之后，乔与鲍勃吐露一项保守逾五十年之秘密。
en: After the funeral, Joe and Bob revealed a secret which they had kept for over fifty years.

zh: 埃里克乃家中长子，较二弟年长甚多。
en: Eric had been the eldest son of the family, very much older than his two brothers.

zh: 第二次世界大战期间彼被迫从军。
en: He had been obliged to join the army during the Second World War.

zh: 因憎恶军旅生活，彼决意逃离部队。
en: As he hated army life, he decided to desert his regiment.

zh: 当得知将被遣往海外，彼返归农场，其父将其藏匿直至战争结束。
en: When he learnt that he would be sent abroad, he returned to the farm and his father hid him until the end of the war.

zh: 因畏惧当局，战后埃里克仍隐而不出。
en: Fearing the authorities, Eric remained in hiding after the war as well.

zh: 其父告众人埃里克已阵亡。
en: His father told everybody that Eric had been killed in action.

zh: 知此秘密者唯乔与鲍勃。
en: The only other people who knew the secret were Joe and Bob.

zh: 彼等甚至未告知各自之妻。
en: They did not even tell 20 their wives.

zh: 其父去世时，二人以为有责任继续将埃里克藏匿。
en: When their father died, they thought it their duty to keep Eric in hiding.

zh: 历年来，埃里克过着隐士般之生活。
en: All these years, Eric had lived as a recluse.

zh: 彼惯于昼寝夜作，竟不自知已成恩德利之“鬼魂”。
en: He used to sleep during the day and work at night, quite unaware of the fact that he had become the ghost of Endley.

zh: 然彼既殁，兄弟二人终觉无法再守此秘密。
en: When he died, however, his brothers found it impossible to keep the secret any longer.

zh: 真正之怪人从不刻意引人注目。
en: True eccentrics never deliberately set out to draw attention to themselves.

zh: 彼等漠视社会习俗，却不自觉行有何异常。
en: They disregard social conventions without being conscious that they are doing anything extraordinary.

zh: 此点恒使彼等赢得他人敬爱，盖因彼等为平淡日常添一抹色彩。
en: This invariably wins them the love and respect of others, for they add colour to the dull routine of everyday life.

zh: 直至去世，理查德·科尔森一直为本镇最显赫人物之一。
en: Up to the time of his death, Richard Colson was one of the most notable figures in our town.

zh: 彼为精明富有之商人，然镇中多数人对其作为商人之一面几无所知。
en: He was a shrewd and wealthy businessman, but most people in the town hardly knew anything about this side of his life.

zh: 众人皆称彼为迪基，其怪癖在彼去世很久以前便已传为传奇。
en: He was known to us all as Dickie and his eccentricity had become legendary long before he died.

zh: 迪基深恶势利之徒。
en: Dickie disliked snobs intensely.

zh: 虽拥有大汽车，彼几从不驾驶，总喜徒步而行。
en: Though he owned a large car, he hardly ever used it, preferring always to go on foot.

zh: 即便大雨滂沱，彼亦拒持伞。
en: Even when it was raining heavily, he refused to carry an umbrella.

zh: 一日，彼遭暴雨淋透，步入一家高档商店。
en: One day, he walked into an expensive shop after having been caught in a particularly heavy shower.

zh: 彼欲为妻子购一块三百英镑之表，然衣衫褴褛，店员竟拒为彼服务。
en: He wanted to buy a f300 watch for his wife, but he was in such a bedraggled condition that an assistant refused to serve him.

zh: 迪基一言不发离去，旋即携一大布袋返回。
en: Dickie left the shop without a word and returned carrying a large cloth bag.

zh: 因其沉重异常，彼将袋置于柜台之上。
en: As it was extremely heavy, he dumped it on the counter.

zh: 店员请其离开，迪基置若罔闻，要求见经理。
en: The assistant asked him to leave, but Dickie paid no attention to him and requested to see the manager.

zh: 经理认出顾客身份，连连致歉，并严斥该店员。
en: Recognizing who the customer was, the manager was most apologetic and reprimanded the assis- tant severely.

zh: 迪基得表之后，将布袋呈予店员。
en: When Dickie was given the watch, he presented the assistant with the cloth bag.

zh: 内装三百英镑之便士。
en: It contained f300 in pennies.

zh: 彼坚持须由店员点清款项方肯离去——共计三万枚便士！
en: He insisted on the assistant's counting the money before he left—30,000 pennies in all!

zh: 又有一次，彼邀数位重要艺评家前来观赏其私人收藏之现代绘画。
en: On another occasion, he invited a number of important critics to see his private collection of modern paintings.

zh: 此展于报界颇受瞩目，盖因画作虽托名名家手笔，实则皆出自迪基。
en: This exhibition received a great deal of attention in the press, for though the pictures were supposed to be the work of famous artists, they had in fact been painted by Dickie.

zh: 彼费时四年布置此精巧恶作剧，仅为证明艺评家未必真知其所言。
en: It took him four years to stage this elaborate joke simply to prove that critics do not always know what they are talking about.

zh: 打捞作业彻底失败。
en: The salvage operation had been a complete failure.

zh: 小型船只“埃尔科”号于巴伦支海搜寻数周，正返航归港。
en: The small ship, Elkor, which had been searching the Barents Sea for weeks, was on its way home.

zh: 船长收到来自本土之无线电报，命其放弃搜寻。
en: A radio message from the mainland had been received by the ship's captain instructing him to give up the search.

zh: 船长知日后必将再度尝试，盖因彼欲寻之沉船曾载有贵重金条。
en: The captain knew that another attempt would be made later, for the sunken ship he was trying to find had been carrying a precious cargo of gold bullion.

zh: 尽管有此电文，“埃尔科”号船长仍决意再试一次。
en: Despite the message, the captain of the Elkor decided to try once more.

zh: 海底以巨网搜刮，当一箱自海底吊起时，船上兴奋达于极点。
en: The sea bed was scoured with powerful nets and there was tremendous excitement on board when a chest was raised from the bottom.

zh: 船员初以为失踪之船已得发现，然箱内之物证明彼等判断有误。
en: Though the crew were at first under the impression that the lost ship had been found, the contents of the chest proved them wrong.

zh: 彼等实乃发现一艘多年前即已沉没之船。
en: What they had in fact found was a ship which had been sunk many years before.

zh: 箱中乃水手艾伦·菲尔丁之私人物品。
en: The chest contained the personal belongings of a seaman, Alan Fielding.

zh: 有书籍、衣物与照片，并附其妻昔日寄彼之书信。
en: There were books, clothing and photographs, together with letters which the seaman had once received from his wife.

zh: “埃尔科”号船长命船员尽量自沉船打捞遗物。
en: The captain of the Elkor ordered his men to salvage as much as possible from the wreck.

zh: 未发现值钱之物，然捞上之众多物件却饶有兴味。
en: Nothing of value was found, but the numerous items which were brought to the surface proved to be of great interest.

zh: 自捞起之重炮，船长推断该船必为巡洋舰。
en: From a heavy gun that was raised, the captain realized that the ship must have been a cruiser.

zh: 另一箱内为某军官遗物，中有未完成之信，写于一九四三年三月十四日。
en: In another chest, which contained the belongings of a ship's officer, there was an unfinished letter which had been written on March 14th,1943.

zh: 船长由信中得知失踪船只之名曰“卡伦”号。
en: The captain learnt from the letter that the name of the lost ship was the Karen.

zh: 最珍贵之发现乃该船航海日志，其中部分尚可辨认。
en: The most valuable find of all was the ship's log book, parts of which it was still possible to read.

zh: 据此船长得以将已显露之种种讯息缀合完整。
en: From this the captain was able to piece together all the information that had come to light.

zh: “卡伦”号于护航赴俄途中遭敌潜艇鱼雷击沉。
en: The Karen had been sailing in a convoy to Russia when she was torpedoed by an enemy submarine.

zh: “埃尔科”号归港后，国防部一海军官员证实此事。
en: This was later confirmed by a naval official at the Ministry of Defence after the Elkor had returned home.

zh: 所有寻获之物皆送交战争博物馆。
en: All the items that were found were sent to the War Museum.

zh: 我等皆有诸事不顺之日。
en: We have all experienced days when everything goes wrong.

zh: 一日或开端尚佳，然骤然间万事似皆失控。
en: A day may begin well enough, but suddenly everything seems to get out of control.

zh: 常见之况乃大量事情恰于同一时刻一齐出错。
en: What invariably happens is that a great number of things choose to go wrong at precisely the same moment.

zh: 仿佛一微末之事触发连锁反应。
en: It is as if a single unimportant event set up a chain of reactions.

zh: 假设汝正备餐，同时尚须照看婴儿。
en: Let us suppose that you are preparing a meal and keeping an eye on the baby at the same time.

zh: 电话铃响，此即一连串意外灾祸之序曲。
en: The telephone rings and this marks the prelude to an unforeseen series of catastrophes.

zh: 汝接听电话之际，婴儿扯落桌布，砸碎半数精美瓷器，并因而割伤自己。
en: While you are on the phone, the baby pulls the tablecloth off the table, smashing half your best crockery and cutting himself in the process.

zh: 汝匆匆挂断电话，赶去照料婴儿、收拾瓷器等。
en: You hang up hurriedly and attend to 10 baby, crockery, etc.

zh: 与此同时，饭菜烧焦。
en: Meanwhile, the meal gets burnt.

zh: 若仅此尚不足以令汝落泪，汝夫忽归，竟携三位客人前来用膳。
en: As if this were not enough to reduce you to tears, your husband arrives, unexpectedly bringing three guests to dinner.

zh: 事情亦可大规模失控，悉尼郊区帕拉马塔近日即有众人亲历此事。
en: Things can go wrong on a big scale, as a number of people recently discovered in Parramatta, a suburb of Sydney.

zh: 某晚交通高峰时段，两车相撞，双方司机争执起来。
en: During the rush hour one evening two cars collided and both drivers began to argue.

zh: 紧随两车之后之女子恰为学车者。
en: The woman immediately behind the two cars Happened to be a learner.

zh: 彼女骤然惊慌，停车不前。其后之司机遂猛踩刹车。
en: She suddenly got into a panic and stopped her car. 15 This made the driver following her brake hard.

zh: 其妻坐于身侧，手捧大蛋糕。
en: His wife was sitting beside him holding a large cake.

zh: 彼女向前一冲，蛋糕穿过挡风玻璃，落于路面。
en: As she was thrown forward, the cake went right through the windscreen and landed on the road.

zh: 一卡车司机见蛋糕凌空飞过，正驶近该车，猛然刹住。
en: Seeing a cake flying through the air, a lorry driver who was drawing up alongside the car, pulled up all of a sudden.

zh: 卡车载有空啤酒瓶，数百只自车尾滑落路面。
en: The lorry was loaded with empty beer bottles and hundreds of them slid off the back of the vehicle and on to the road.

zh: 于是又起一场激烈争吵。
en: This led to yet another angry argument.

zh: 与此同时，后方车辆排成长龙。
en: Meanwhile, the traffic piled up behind.

zh: 警方费时近一小时方使交通恢复流动。
en: It took the police 20 nearly an hour to get the traffic on the move again.

zh: 在此期间，卡车司机须清扫数百破碎酒瓶。
en: In the meantime, the lorry driver had to sweep up hundreds of broken bottles.

zh: 唯两只流浪狗于此混乱中得利，贪婪吞食蛋糕之余屑。
en: Only two stray dogs benefited from all this confusion, for they greedily devoured what was left of the cake.

zh: 真可谓祸不单行之日！
en: It was just one of those days!

zh: 古董店对甚多人施以独特之魅惑。
en: Antique shops exert a peculiar fascination on a great many people.

zh: 高档古董店将稀世之物陈列于玻璃柜中以避尘埃，往往令人望而生畏。
en: The more expensive kind of antique shop where rare objects are beautifully displayed in glass cases to keep them free from dust is usually a forbidding place.

zh: 然步入一家不甚张扬之古董店，则无须鼓足勇气。
en: But no one has to muster up courage to enter a less pretentious antique shop.

zh: 人总怀希望：于其霉味弥漫、幽暗杂乱、如迷宫之斗室中，于满地杂陈旧货堆里，或可觅得真罕物。
en: There is always hope that in its labyrinth of musty, dark, disordered rooms a real rarity willbe found amongst the piles of assorted junk that litter the floors.

zh: 无人能凭偶然发现罕物。
en: No one discovers a rarity by chance.

zh: 真正执着之淘便宜货者须有耐心，尤须具一眼识破价值之眼光。
en: A truly dedicated bargain hunter must have patience, and above all, the ability to recognize the worth of something when he sees it.

zh: 欲臻此境，其学识至少须与店主相当。
en: Todo this, he must be at least as knowledgeable as the dealer.

zh: 如矢志发现之科学家，彼必怀希望：有朝一日终获厚报。
en: Like a scientist bent on making a discovery, he must cherish the hope that one day he will be amply rewarded.

zh: 吾老友弗兰克·哈利迪正属此类人物。
en: My old friend, Frank Halliday, is just such a person.

zh: 彼常向吾讲述如何仅以五十英镑购得一幅杰作。
en: He has often described to me how he picked up a masterpiece for a mere £50.

zh: 某周六上午，弗兰克造访吾邻一家古董店。
en: One Saturday morning, Frank visited an antique shop in my neighbourhood.

zh: 因从未到过彼处，彼觉颇多可资玩味之物。
en: As he had never been there before, he found a great deal to interest him.

zh: 上午倏忽而逝，弗兰克正欲离去，忽见地上横卧一大木箱。
en: The morning passed rapidly and Frank was about to leave when he noticed a large packing case lying on the floor.

zh: 店主告彼货甫到，然懒于开箱。
en: The dealer told him that it had just come in, but that he could not be bothered to open it.

zh: 弗兰克恳求开箱，店主方勉强撬开。
en: Frank begged him to do so and the dealer reluc- tantly prised it open.

zh: 内物令人失望。
en: The contents were disappointing.

zh: 除一柄外观有趣之雕花匕首外，箱中尽是瓷器，多已破损。
en: Apart from an interesting-looking carved dagger, the box was full of crockery, much of it broken.

zh: 弗兰克轻轻取出瓷器，忽于箱底见一帧袖珍画。
en: Frank gently lifted the crockery out of the box and suddenly noticed a miniature painting at the bottoin of the packing case.

zh: 其构图与线条令彼想起所熟知之一幅意大利画，遂决意购下。
en: As its composition and line reminded him of an Italian painting he knew well, he decided to buy it.

zh: 店主略瞥一眼，告彼该画值五十英镑。
en: Glancing at it briefly, the dealer told him that it was worth f50.

zh: 弗兰克几难掩兴奋，因知自己确有所获。
en: Frank could hardly conceal his excitement, for he knew that he had made a real discovery.

zh: 此小幅画作后经鉴定为科雷乔之未知杰作，价值数十万英镑。
en: The tiny painting proved to be an unknown masterpiece by Correggio and was worth hundreds of thousands of pounds.

zh: “正义”一词通常与法庭相联系。
en: The word justice is usually associated with courts of law.

zh: 当一人之无辜或有罪已获确凿证明，我等或可谓正义得伸。
en: We might say that justice has been done when a man's innocence or guilt has been proved beyond doubt.

zh: 正义乃法律繁复机制之一部分。
en: Justice is part of the complex machinery of the law.

zh: 求之者踏上艰辛旅程，永难确知终能觅得与否。
en: Those who seek it undertake an arduous journey and can never be sure that they will find it.

zh: 法官无论何等睿智显赫，终为人，难免失误。
en: Judges, however wise or eminent, are human and can make mistakes.

zh: 亦有罕见之时，正义几不复为抽象概念。
en: There are rare instances when justice almost ceases to be an abstract concept.

zh: 赏罚之施，全然不假人力干预。
en: Reward or punishment are meted out quite independent of human interference.

zh: 于此类时刻，正义犹如一种活的力量。
en: At such times, justice acts like a living force.

zh: 当我等使用“罪有应得”一类措辞时，于某种程度上实乃承认：某种情势已使正义自行运作。
en: When we use a phrase like ‘it serves him right’, we are, in part, admitting that a certain set of circumstances has 10 enabled justice to act of its own accord.

zh: 某晨一窃贼于一家大型珠宝店内被擒，店员想必忍不住要说“罪有应得”。
en: When a thief was caught on the premises of a large jewellery store one morning, the shop assistants must have found it impossible to resist the temptation to say ‘it serves him right'.

zh: 该店由旧宅改建，有诸多宽大废置之壁炉与高耸狭窄之烟囱。
en: The shop was an old converted house with many large, disused fireplaces and tall, narrow chimneys.

zh: 近正午时，一女郎闻墙后传来闷哑之呼喊。
en: Towards midday, a girl heard a muffled cry coming from behind one of the walls.

zh: 呼喊再三，彼女奔告经理，经理立即致电消防队。
en: As the cry was repeated several times, she ran to tell the manager 15 who promptly rang up the fire brigade.

zh: 呼喊确自某一烟囱传来，然烟囱众多，消防队员一时难定何者。
en: The cry had certainly come from one of the chimneys, but as there were so many of them, the fire fighters could not be certain which one it was.

zh: 彼等敲击墙壁，倾听男子哭喊，方定位正确之烟囱。
en: Theylocated the right chimney by tapping at the walls and listening for the man's cries.

zh: 凿穿厚达十八英寸之墙壁后，发现一男子被困于烟囱内。
en: After chipping through a wallwhich was eighteen inches thick, they found that a man had been trapped in the chimney.

zh: 因烟囱极狭，男子无法动弹，消防队员终在墙上凿一大洞方将其救出。
en: As it was extremely narrow, the man was unable to move, but the fire fighters were eventually able to free him by cutting a huge hole in the wall.

zh: 现身者面目乌黑、形容狼狈，当即承认夜间曾欲破店而入，却卡在烟囱中。
en: The sorry-looking, blackened figure that emerged, admited at once that he had tried to break into the shop during the night but had got stuck in the chimney.

zh: 彼被困已近十小时。
en: He had been there for nearly ten hours.

zh: 在那名男子被移交警方之前，正义其实早已得到伸张。
en: Justice had been done even before the man was handed over to the police.

zh: 小说家惯于以一连串巧合来收束故事——其中大多荒诞不经，令人难以置信。
en: a novelist would bring his story to a conclusion by presenting his readers with a series of coincidences —most of them wildly improbable.

zh: 读者也乐于接受这样一个事实：一位默默无闻的女仆原来竟是主人公的母亲。
en: Readers happily accepted the fact that an obscure maidservant was really the hero's mother.

zh: 一位久已失踪、被认为早已亡故的兄弟其实一直活着，并且居心险恶地密谋使主人公身败名裂。
en: A long-lost brother, who was presumed dead, was really alive all the time and wickedly plotting to bring about the hero's downfall.

zh: 如此等等。
en: And so on.

zh: 现代读者会觉得这种幼稚的收场完全无法接受。
en: Modern readers would find such naive solutions totally unacceptable.

zh: 然而，在现实生活中，环境有时确会凑巧促成种种巧合；除了十九世纪的小说家之外，任何人都会觉得难以置信。
en: Yet, in real life, circumstances do sometimes conspire to bring about coincidences which anyone but a nineteenth century novelist would find incredible.

zh: 我小时候，祖父曾告诉我，德国出租车司机弗朗茨·布斯曼如何找到了一位被认为已在二十年前阵亡的兄弟。
en: When I was a boy, my grandfather told me how a German taxi driver, Franz Bussman, found a brother who was thought to have been killed twenty years before.

zh: 他与妻子徒步旅行时，停下来与一名工人攀谈。
en: While on a walking tour with his wife, he stopped to talk to a workman.

zh: 他们继续前行之后，布斯曼夫人
en: After they had gone on, Mrs.

zh: 评论那名工人与她丈夫相貌极为相似，甚至暗示他可能就是她丈夫的兄弟。
en: Bussman commented on the workman's close resemblance to her husband and even suggested that he might be his brother.

zh: 弗朗茨对此嗤之以鼻，指出他的兄弟已在战争中阵亡。
en: Franz poured scorn on the idea, pointing out that his brother had been killed in action during the war.

zh: 尽管布斯曼夫人
en: Though Mrs.

zh: 对此事始末了如指掌，她仍认为自己万一说中的可能性虽微乎其微，却并非绝无可能。
en: Bussman was fully acquainted with this story, she thought that there was a chance in a million that she might be right.

zh: 几天之后，她派一名男孩去问那名工人，他是否叫汉斯·布斯曼。
en: A few days later, she sent a boy to the workman to ask him if his name was Hans Bussman.

zh: 不言而喻，那人正是汉斯·布斯曼，他也确实是弗朗茨失散多年的兄弟。
en: Needless to say, the man's name was Hans Bussman and he really was Franz's long-lost brother.

zh: 兄弟重逢时，汉斯说明了自己何以仍活在人间。
en: When the brothers were reunited, Hans explained how it was that he was still alive.

zh: 战争临近结束时他负了伤，被送进医院，因而与部队失散。
en: After having been wounded towards the end of the war, he had been sent to hospital and was separated from his unit.

zh: 医院遭到轰炸，汉斯徒步返回西德。
en: The hospital had been bombed and Hans had made his way back into Western Germany on foot.

zh: 与此同时，他所在的部队下落不明，有关他的一切档案也尽遭焚毁。
en: Meanwhile, his unit was lost and all records of him had been destroyed.

zh: 汉斯回到老家，但房屋已被炸毁，邻里无人知晓住户下落如何。
en: Hans returned to his family home, but the house had been bombed and no one in the neighbourhood knew what had become of the inhabitants.

zh: 他以为全家已在空袭中遇难，便在一个五十英里外的村庄定居下来，从此一直住在那里。
en: Assuming that his family had been killed during an air raid, Hans settled down in a village fifty miles away where he had remained ever since.

zh: 唯有遭遇罕见的暴雪，铁路运输才可能暂时陷入紊乱。
en: Only an exceptionally heavy snowfall might temporarily dislocate railway services.

zh: 一旦出事，人们总惯于归咎于铁路当局，这也未免太过轻易。
en: It is all too easy to blame the railway authorities when something does go wrong.

zh: 事实是，若出现差错，更可能是我们自身的失误，而非他们的责任。
