en: The truth is that when mistakes occur, they are more likely to be ours than theirs.

zh: 我查阅列车时刻表后，满意地发现有一趟开往韦斯特海文的快车。
en: After consulting my railway timetable, I noted with satisfaction that there was an express train to Westhaven.

zh: 它从本站直达，全程仅需一小时十七分。
en: It went direct from my local station and the journey lasted a mere hour and seventeen minutes.

zh: 上车后，我禁不住注意到许多本地乘客也一同登车。
en: When I boarded the train, I could not help noticing that a great many local people got on as well.

zh: 当时，我并未觉得有何异样。
en: At the time, this did not strike me as odd.

zh: 我心想，除我之外，必定还有许多人想利用这一优良班次。
en: I reflected that there must be a great many people besides myself who wished to take advantage of this excellent service.

zh: 列车在威德利——沿线数英里外的一座小站——停靠时，我也并不感到意外。
en: Neither was I surprised when the train stopped at Widley, a tiny station a few miles along the line.

zh: 即便气势恢宏的快车，也可能因信号而受阻。
en: Even a mighty express train can be held up by signals.

zh: 可是，当列车一站又一站地磨蹭不前时，我不禁起了疑心。
en: But when the train dawdled at station after station, I began to wonder.

zh: 我突然明白，这趟快车并非以每小时九十英里的速度沿铁路飞驰，而只是以每小时三十英里勉强爬行。
en: It suddenly dawned on me that this express was not roaring down the line at ninety miles an hour, but barely chugging along at thirty.

zh: 一小时十七分钟过去了，我们连一半路程都尚未走完。
en: One hour and seventeen minutes passed and we had not even covered half the distance.

zh: 我问一位乘客这是否为开往韦斯特海文的快车，他却连听都未曾听说过。
en: I asked a passenger if this was the Westhaven Express, but he had not even heard of it.

zh: 我决意一到站便提出投诉。
en: I determined to lodge a complaint as soon as we arrived.

zh: 两小时之后，我正怒气冲冲地与韦斯特海文站长交涉。
en: Two hours later, I was talking angrily to the station master at Westhaven.

zh: 当他否认存在这样一趟列车时，我借来他的时刻表一阅。
en: When he denied the train's existence, I 20 borrowed his copy of the timetable.

zh: 当我告诉他白纸黑字写得清清楚楚时，我的语气中带着几分得意。
en: There was a note of triumph in my voice when I told him that it was there in black and white.

zh: 他匆匆瞥了一眼，却叫我再看一遍。
en: Glancing at it briefly, he told me to look again.

zh: 一个小小的星号把我引到页末的一条脚注。
en: A tiny asterisk conducted me to a footnote at the bottom of the page.

zh: 脚注写道：「本班次已暂停运营。」
en: It said:'This service has been suspended.

zh: 未来的史学家在记述我们这个时代的历史时，将处于独一无二的地位。
en: Future historians will be in a unique position when they come to record the history of our own times.

zh: 面对不断累积的大量史料，他们几乎难以判断应选取哪些事实。
en: They will hardly know which facts to select from the great mass of evidence that steadily accumulates.

zh: 更有甚者，他们不必仅仅依赖文字记载。
en: What is more, they will not have to rely solely on the 5 written word.

zh: 电影、录像、光盘及只读光盘等，不过是他们所能掌握的海量信息中的一部分。
en: Films, videos, CDs and CD-ROMs are just some of the bewildering amount of information they will have.

zh: 他们可以说是在亲眼目睹、亲耳聆听我们的一举一动。
en: They will be able, as it were, to see and hear us in action.

zh: 但是，试图重建遥远过去的史学家始终面临着艰巨的任务。
en: But the historian attempting to reconstruct the distant past is always faced with a difficult task.

zh: 他必须从寥寥可数的线索中尽量推究出真相。
en: He has to deduce what he can from the few scanty clues available.

zh: 即便看似微不足道的遗存，也能为早期人类的历史提供饶有兴味的启示。
en: Even seemingly insignificant remains can shed interesting light on the history of early man.

zh: 迄今为止，史学家一直认为历法随农业的出现而产生，因为那时人类确有了解季节变化的实际需要。
en: Up to now, historians have assumed that calendars came into being with the advent of agriculture, for then man was faced with a real need to understand something about the seasons.

zh: 近期的科学证据似乎表明，这一假设并不正确。
en: Recent scientific evidence seems to indicate that this assumption is incorrect.

zh: 长久以来，刻在墙壁、骨骼以及猛犸象牙上的点、线与符号一直令史学家困惑不解。
en: Historians have long been puzzled by dots, lines and symbols which have been engraved on walls, bones, and the ivory tusks of mammoths.

zh: 留下这些记号的游牧民生活于最后一次冰河期，以狩猎和捕鱼为生；该时期约始于公元前三万五千年，
en: The nomads who made these markings lived by hunting and 15 fishing during the last Ice Age which began about 35,000 B.

zh: 约终于公元前一万年。
en: C.and ended about 10,000 B.

zh: 通过比对世界各地留下的记号，史学家终于读懂了这一难解的符号体系。
en: By correlating markings made in various parts of the world, historians have been able to read this difficult code.

zh: 他们发现，它与昼夜更替及月相变化有关。
en: They have found that it is connected with the passage of days and the phases of the moon.

zh: 事实上，这是一种原始的历法。
en: It is, in fact, a primitive type of calendar.

zh: 人们早已知道，绘于壁上的狩猎场景并非单纯的艺术表现。
en: It has long been known that the hunting scenes depicted on walls were not simply a form of artistic expression.

zh: 它们具有明确含义，因为那是早期人类所能做到的最接近文字的东西。
en: They had a definite meaning, for they were as near as early man could get to writing.

zh: 这些绘画与有时伴随其旁的记号之间，或许存在某种确定的关系。
en: It is possible that there is a definite relation between these paintings and the markings that sometimes accompany them.

zh: 看来，人类力图理解季节的努力，要比人们原先所认为的早两万年。
en: It seems that man was making a real effort to understand the seasons 20,000 years earlier than has been supposed.

zh: 穿越平原的崎岖道路很快变得难以通行，我们试图劝布鲁斯掉头驶回我们出发的村庄。
en: The rough road across the plain soon became so bad that we tried to get Bruce to drive back to the village we had come from.

zh: 尽管路上遍布巨石、坑洼累累，布鲁斯却丝毫不为所动。
en: Even though the road was littered with boulders and pitted with holes, Bruce was not in the least perturbed.

zh: 他瞥了一眼地图，告诉我们下一个村庄不过二十英里之遥。
en: Glancing at his map, he 5 informed us that the next village was a mere twenty miles away.

zh: 倒不是说布鲁斯总是低估困难。
en: It was not that Bruce always underestimated difficulties.

zh: 他简直毫无危险意识。
en: He simply had no sense of danger at all.

zh: 无论路况如何，他都认为汽车应尽可能开得快。
en: No matter what the conditions were, he believed that a car should be driven as fast as it could possibly go.

zh: 我们在尘土飞扬的小路上颠簸前行，为避开大块岩石而频频急转。
en: As we bumped over the dusty track, we swerved to avoid large boulders.

zh: 车轮卷起石子，在车身下砰砰作响，令人不安。
en: The wheels scooped up stones 10 which hammered ominously under the car.

zh: 我们深信，迟早会有石子划破油箱或损坏发动机。
en: We felt sure that sooner or later a stone would rip a hole in our petrol tank or damage the engine.

zh: 因此，我们不断回头张望，担心身后是否留下一道油迹。
en: Because of this, we kept looking back, wondering if we were leaving a trail of oil and petrol behind us. fissure.

zh: 在我们再次恳求之下，布鲁斯终于停车。
en: In response to renewed pleadings, Bruce stopped.

zh: 我们全都下车察看那道地裂，他却仍坐在车里。
en: Though we all got out to examine the fissure, hee remained in the car.

zh: 我们告诉他，地裂延伸五十码，宽两英尺，深四英尺。
en: We informed him that the fissure extended for fifty yards and was two feet wide and four feet deep.

zh: 即便如此，仍无济于事。
en: Even this had no effect.

zh: 布鲁斯挂入低速挡，以令人胆寒的速度前进，前轮跨骑裂缝，沿着其曲折走向行驶。
en: Bruce went into a low gear and drove at a terrifying speed, keeping the front wheels astride the crack as he followed its zigzag course.

zh: 我们尚未来得及担忧将会发生何事，便已重新回到平原之上。
en: Before we had time to worry about what might happen, we were back on the plain again.

zh: 布鲁斯再次查看地图，告诉我们下一个村庄现在只有十五英里远了。
en: Bruce consulted the map once more and told us that the 20 village was now only fifteen miles away.

zh: 我们遇到的下一道障碍是一片浅水池，宽约半英里。
en: Our next obstacle was a shallow pool of water about half a mile across.

zh: 布鲁斯驱车直冲过去，可车到池心却戛然停住。
en: Bruce charged at it, but in the middle, the car came to a grinding halt.

zh: 仪表盘上一盏黄灯愤怒地闪烁，布鲁斯却兴高采烈地宣布：发动机里已经没有机油了！
en: A yellow light on the dash- board flashed angrily and Bruce cheerfully announced that there was no oil in the engine!

zh: 何以大学生似乎比任何人都更热衷于搞恶作剧，向来无人说得清。
en: It has never been explained why university students seem to enjoy practical jokes more than anyone else.

zh: 大学生尤其擅长一种恶作剧：骗局。
en: Students specialize in a particular type of practical joke: the hoax.

zh: 请消防队来扑灭一场子虚乌有的大火，是一种粗鄙的欺骗；稍有自尊的学生绝不会为之。
en: Inviting the fire brigade to put out a nonexistent fire is a crude form of deception which no self-respecting student woud ever indulge in.

zh: 学生常常制造出滑稽场面，除受害者外，人人觉得好笑。
en: Students often create amusing situations which are funny to everyone except the victims.

zh: 一名学生近日见两名工人在校外使用风钻，便立即致电警方，称有两名学生扮成工人，正用风钻掘路。
en: When a student recently saw two workmen using a pneumatic drill outside his university, he immediately telephoned the police and informed them that two students dressed up as workmen were tearing up the road with a pneumatic drill.

zh: 他挂断电话后，走到工人跟前，告诉他们若有警察命他们离开，不必当真。
en: As soon as he had hung up, he went 10 over to the workmen and told them that if a policeman ordered them to go away, they were not to take him seriously.

zh: 他又补充说，有一名学生扮成警察，正四处对人开各种无聊的玩笑。
en: He added that a student had dressed up as a policeman and was playing all sorts of silly jokes on people.

zh: 警方与工人都因这一事先通报而对那名学生心存感激。
en: Both the police and the workmen were grateful to the student for this piece of advance information.

zh: 那名学生躲在附近的拱门里，可以看清并听见现场发生的一切。
en: The student hid in an archway nearby where he could watch and hear everything that went on.

zh: 果然，一名警察到场，彬彬有礼地请工人离开。
en: Sure enough, a policeman arrived on the scene and politely asked the workmen to go away.

zh: 当他遭到一名工人极为粗鲁的答复时，便威胁要以武力将他们驱离。
en: When he received a 15 very rude reply from one of the workmen, he threatened to remove them by force.

zh: 工人叫他悉听尊便，警察便打电话请求支援。
en: The workmen told him to do as he pleased and the policeman telephoned for help.

zh: 不久，又有四名警察赶到，对工人加以规劝。
en: Shortly afterwards, four more policemen arrived and remonstrated with the workmen.

zh: 由于工人拒绝停工，警察试图夺下风钻。
en: As the men refused to stop working, the police attempted to seize the pneumatic drill.

zh: 工人奋力反抗，其中一人动了怒。
en: The workmen struggled fiercely and one of them lost his temper.

zh: 他威胁要报警。
en: He threatened to call the police.

zh: 闻听此言，警察不无讽刺地指出，这恐怕大可不必，因为工人们其实已被逮捕。
en: At this, the police pointed out ironically that this would hardly be necessary as the men were already 20 under arrest.

zh: 一名工人故作严肃，问在被带往警局之前是否可以打一个电话。
en: Pretending to speak seriously, one of the workmen asked if he might make a telephone call before being taken to the station.

zh: 获准之后，一名警察陪他走到公用电话旁。
en: Permission was granted and a policeman accompanied him to a pay phone.

zh: 直到看见那人当真在拨打报警电话，他才意识到他们全都成了骗局的牺牲品。
en: Only when he saw that the man was actually telephoning the police did he realize that they had all been the victims of a hoax.

zh: 宁静的乡村生活从未吸引过我。
en: The quiet life of the country has never appealed to me.

zh: 我生于城市、长于城市，一向把乡村视为隔窗而望的风景，或是周末偶尔造访之处。
en: City born and city bred, I have always regarded the country as something you look at through a train window, or something you occasionally visit during the weekend.

zh: 我的大多数朋友住在城里，然而只要一提到乡村，他们便赞不绝口。
en: Most of my friends live in the city, yet they 5 always go into raptures at the mere mention of the country.

zh: 尽管他们盛赞宁静生活的种种优点，其中却只有一人真正迁居乡下，而且不到半年便又回城。
en: Though they extol the virtues ofthe peaceful life, only one of them has ever gone to live in the country and he was back in town within six months.

zh: 即便他至今仍抱着一种错觉，以为乡村生活在某种意义上优于城市生活。
en: Even he still lives under the illusion that country life is somehow superior to town life.

zh: 他喋喋不休地谈论友好的乡民、清新的空气、亲近自然以及舒缓的生活节奏。
en: He is forever talking about the friendly people, the clean atmosphere, the closeness to nature and the gentle pace of living.

zh: 他坚称，没有什么能与第一声鸡鸣、黎明时分的鸟语啁啾、旭日映照林木与牧场的景象相提并论。
en: Nothing can be compared, he maintains, 10 with the first cockcrow, the twittering of birds at dawn, the sight of the rising sun glinting on the trees and pastures.

zh: 这幅田园诗般的牧歌景象不过是全貌之一隅。
en: This idyllic pastoral scene is only part of the picture.

zh: 我的朋友却绝口不提那些漫长而孤寂的冬夜——只能守着电视机，那几乎是唯一的消遣。
en: My friend fails to mention the long and friendless winter evenings in front of the TV—virtually the only form of entertainment.

zh: 他也不谈商店里货物品种之贫乏，更不谈那些每日不得不从乡下进城上班的不幸者。
en: He says nothing about the poor selection of goods in the shops, or about those unfortunate people who have to travel from the country to the city every day to get to work.

zh: 人们何以甘愿每日忍受四小时车程，只为换取那可疑的「居于乡间」的特权，我实在无法理解。
en: Why people are prepared to tolerate a four-hour journey each 15 day for the dubious privilege of living in the country is beyond me.

zh: 若他们选择住在本该属于他们的城市里，本可省去许多痛苦与开销。
en: They could be saved so much misery and expense if they chose to live in the city where they rightly belong.

zh: 若能割舍乡间那少许牧歌式的乐趣，你会发现城市能为你提供人生所能给予的最好的一切。
en: If you can do without the few pastoral pleasures of the country, you willfind the city can provide you with the best that life can offer.

zh: 你无须跋涉数英里方能访友。
en: You never have to travel miles to see your friends.

zh: 他们几乎总住在近旁，随时可来一番随意的闲聊或共度一晚。
en: They invariably live nearby and are always available for an informal chat or an evening's entertainment.

zh: 我在乡下的一些熟人，每年进城一两次看戏，权当特别的享受。
en: Some of my 20 acquaintances in the country come up to town once or twice a year to visit the theatre as a special treat.

zh: 对他们来说，这无异于一项重大行动，需要煞费周章地筹划。
en: For them this is a major operation which involves considerable planning.

zh: 戏近尾声时，他们便担心能否赶得上末班火车回家。
en: As the play draws to its close, they wonder whether they will ever catch that last train home.

zh: 城市居民却从不经历此类焦虑。
en: The city dweller never experiences anxieties of this sort.

zh: 最新的展览、电影或戏剧，乘短途公共汽车即可抵达。
en: The latest exhibitions, films, or plays are only a short bus ride away.

zh: 购物亦始终是一种乐趣。
en: Shopping, too, is always a plea- sure.

zh: 商品琳琅满目，你从来不必退而求其次。
en: There is so much variety that you never have to make do with second best.

zh: 乡下人进城购物往往如脱缰之马，回家时满载异国风味的各色商品，步履蹒跚，能拿多少便拿多少。
en: Country people run wild 25 when they go shopping in the city and stagger home loaded with as many of the exotic items as they can carry.

zh: 城市也并非没有动人时刻。
en: Nor is the city without its moments of beauty.

zh: 在阴冷潮湿的冬夜，广告牌投下的暖光令人倍感慰藉。
en: There is something comforting about the warm glow shed by advertisements on cold wet winter nights.

zh: 周末时分，成千上万每日进城上班的人已回到乡居家中，空旷的市街笼罩在一片静谧之中，几乎没有比这更令人印象深刻的景象。
en: Few things could be more impressive than the peace that descends on deserted city streets at weekends when the thousands that travel to work every day are tucked awayin their homes in the country.

zh: 我始终不解：那些明明欣赏这一切的城市居民，为何却固执地装作宁愿住在乡下。
en: It has always been a mystery to me why city dwellers, who appreciate all 30 these things, obstinately pretend that they would prefer to live in the country.

zh: 第三单元 第四十一课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 41

zh: 洞穴探险，或称「探洞」，作为一种运动出现较晚。
en: Cave exploration, or pot-holing, as it has come to be known, is a relatively new sport.

zh: 也许是渴望独处，也许是期待意外发现，诱使人们深入地下。
en: Perhaps it is the desire for solitude or the chance of making an unexpected discovery that lures people down to the depths ofthe earth.

zh: 要对探洞者的动机作出令人满意的解释，是不可能的。
en: It is impossible to give a satisfactory explanation for a pot-holer's motives.

zh: 对他而言，洞穴具有某种奇特的魅力，正如高山之于登山者。
en: For him, caves have the same peculiar fascination which high mountains have for the climber.

zh: 它们唤起某种只能隐约体认的本能。
en: They arouse instincts which can only be dimly understood.

zh: 探索真正的深洞，绝非周日午后散步者所能胜任。
en: Exploring really deep caves is not a task for the Sunday afternoon rambler.

zh: 此类事业需要如军事行动般精密的筹划与远见。
en: Such undertakings require the precise planning and foresight of military operations.

zh: 在进入极深的洞穴之前，往往要花长达八天的时间架设绳梯并建立补给基地。
en: It can take as long as eight days to rig up rope 10 ladders and to establish supply bases before a descent can be made into a very deep cave.

zh: 采取这类防范措施实属必要，因为无法预知探洞者将遭遇何种具体困难。
en: Precautions of this sort are necessary, for it is impossible to foretell the exact nature of the difficulties which will confront the pot-holer.

zh: 已知世界上最深的洞穴是格勒诺布尔附近的古弗尔·贝尔热洞。
en: The deepest known cave in the world is the Gouffre Berger near Grenoble.

zh: 其深度达三千七百二十三英尺。
en: It extends to a depth of 3,723 feet.

zh: 这道巨大的裂隙由一条地下溪流冲刷而成，溪流沿岩石裂隙穿凿出一条通道。
en: This immense chasm has been formed by an underground stream which has tunnelled a course through a flaw in the rocks.

zh: 洞口位于多菲内阿尔卑斯山区的一处高原上。
en: The entrance to the caveis on a plateau in the Dauphiné Alps.

zh: 因入口仅宽六英尺，几乎难以察觉。
en: As it is only six 15 feet across, it is barely noticeable.

zh: 若非法国著名探洞者贝尔热发现洞口，此洞或许永远不会为人所知。
en: The cave might never have been discovered had not the entrance been spotted by the distinguished French pot-holer, Berger.

zh: 自发现以来，它已成为探洞者心目中的「珠穆朗玛」。
en: Since its discovery, it has become a sort of pot- holers'Everest.

zh: 虽已多次下洞，其中大部分区域仍有待探索。
en: Though a number of descents have been made, much of it still remains to be explored.

zh: 一队探洞者近日下到了古弗尔·贝尔热洞。
en: A team of pot-holers recently went down the Gouffre Berger.

zh: 他们穿过高原上狭窄的裂口进入洞内，沿陡峭的洞壁下行，直至来到一条狭窄的通道。
en: After entering the narow gap on the pla- teau, they climbed down the steep sides of the cave until they came to a narrow corridor.

zh: 他们只得侧身沿通道挪动，时而蹚过浅溪，时而游过深潭。
en: They had to edge 20 their way along this, sometimes wading across shallow streams, or swimming across deep pools.

zh: 忽然，他们来到一道瀑布，瀑布泻入洞底的地下湖。
en: Suddenly they came to a waterfall which dropped into an underground lake at the bottom of the cave.

zh: 他们跃入湖中，把装备搬上一艘充气橡皮艇后，任水流将他们载向对岸。
en: They plunged into the lake, and after loading their gear on aninflatable rubber dinghy, let the current carry them to the other side.

zh: 为抵御刺骨的湖水，他们不得不穿上特制的橡胶潜水服。
en: To protect themselves from the icy water, they had to wear special rubber suits.

zh: 在湖的远端，他们见到被水流冲积而成的大堆碎石。
en: At the far end of the lake, they came to huge piles of rubble which had been washed up by the water.

zh: 在洞穴的这一部分，他们听到一阵持续不断的轰鸣；后来发现，那是从洞顶射入水潭的小水柱所致。
en: In this part of the cave, 25 they could hear an insistent booming sound which they found was caused by a small waterspout shooting down into a pool from the roof of the cave.

zh: 探洞者从岩石裂隙中挤过，来到一个巨大的洞厅，其大小堪比一座大型音乐厅。
en: Squeczing through a cleft in the rocks, the pot-holers arrived at an enormous cavern, the size of a huge concert all.

zh: 打开强弧光灯后，他们看见巨大的石笋——有的高达四十余英尺——如树干般向上耸立，与悬垂洞顶的石钟乳相接。
en: After switching on powerful arc lights, they saw great stalagmites—some of them over forty feet high—rising up like tree-trunks to met the stalactites suspended from the roof.

zh: 四周，石灰岩堆在灯光下闪烁，呈现出彩虹般的各色光泽。
en: Round about, piles of limestone glistened in all the colours of the rainbow.

zh: 在洞厅阴森寂静之中，唯一能听见的是水从他们头顶高高的穹顶不断滴落的声音。
en: In the eerie silence 30 of the cavern, the only sound that could be heard was made by water which dripped continuously from the high dome above them.

zh: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
en: Listen to the tape then answer the question below.

zh: 保险公司通常愿意承保任何事物。
en: Insurance companies are normally willing to insure anything.

zh: 为公私财产投保，在世界上大多数国家都是通行做法。
en: Insuring public or private property is a standard practice in most countries in the world.

zh: 然而，若你举办露天游园会或义卖会，同样可以为恶劣天气投保。
en: If, however, you were holding an open air garden party or a fete it would be equally possible to insure yourself in the event of bad weather.

zh: 不言而喻，保险公司承担的风险越大，你所需支付的保费便越高。
en: Needless to say, the bigger the risk an insurance company takes, the higher the premium you will have to pay.

zh: 听说某航运公司为打捞沉船的费用提出索赔，这并不罕见。
en: It is not uncommon to hear that a shipping company has made a claim for the cost of salvaging a sunken ship.

zh: 但地方政府为追回打捞一只沉没馅饼盘的费用而提出索赔，想必绝无仅有。
en: But the claim made by a local authority to recover the cost of salvaging a sunken pie dish must surely be unique.

zh: 诚然，那是一只非同寻常的馅饼盘，长十八英尺，宽六英尺。
en: Admittedly it was an unusual pie dish, for it was eighteen feet long and six feet wide.

zh: 地方政府购入此盘，以便在一年一度的集市上烘烤一只巨型馅饼。
en: It had been pur- chased by a local authority so that an enormous pie could be baked for an annual fair.

zh: 馅饼委员会认定，经运河运输此盘最为稳妥，于是为此次行程投保。
en: The pie committee decided that the best way to transport the dish would be by canal, so they insured it for the trip.

zh: 盘子下水后不久，馅饼委员会便到当地小酒馆庆祝。
en: Shortly after it was launched, the pie committee went to a local inn to celebrate.

zh: 与此同时，一群少年爬上盘子，也自办了一个小聚会。
en: At the same time, a number of teenagers climbed on to the dish and held a little party of their own.

zh: 跳舞超出了盘子所能承受的限度，聚会中盘子倾覆，沉入七英尺深的水中。
en: Dancing proved to be more than the dish could bear, for during the party it capsized and sank in seven feet of water.

zh: 馅饼委员会打电话给当地一家修车厂老板，他开着救援车赶来打捞馅饼盘。
en: The pie committee telephoned a local garage owner who arrived in a recovery truck to salvage the pie dish.

zh: 少年们湿衣瑟瑟发抖，在一旁观望，三名男子反复潜入水中寻找盘子。
en: Shivering in their wet clothes, the teenagers looked on while three men dived repeatedly into the water to locate the dish.

zh: 他们找到盘子并不困难，但将其拖出水面却成了一大难题。
en: They had little difficulty in finding it, but hauling it out of the water proved to be a serious problem.

zh: 盘壁过于光滑，要在不损坏盘缘的情况下系上缆绳与铁链几乎不可能。
en: The sides of the dish were so smooth that it was almost impossible to attach hawsers and chains to the rim without damaging it.

zh: 最终，铁链系在盘子一端，一台大功率绞车开始运转。
en: Eventually chains were fixed to one end of the dish and a powerful winch was put into operation.

zh: 盘子浮出水面，被缓缓拖向运河岸。
en: The dish rose to the surface and was gently drawn towards the canal bank.

zh: 有一刹那，盘子危险地搁在运河岸上，却忽然失衡，又滑回水中。
en: For one agonizing moment, the dish was perched precariously on the bank of the canal, but it suddenly overbalanced and slid back into the water.

zh: 众人只得再次尝试。
en: The men were now obliged to try once more.

zh: 这次他们在盘子两侧装上沉重的金属夹具，以便固定铁链。
en: This time they fixed heavy metal clamps to both sides of the dish so that they could fasten the chains.

zh: 盘子须垂直吊起，因其一边抵在运河壁上。
en: The dish now had to be lifted vertically because one edge was resting against the side of the canal.

zh: 绞车再次开动，其中一人发动了卡车。
en: The winch was again put into operation and one of the men started up the truck.

zh: 数分钟后，盘子终于被成功拖出水面。
en: Several minutes later, the dish was successfully hauled above the surface of the water.

zh: 水如洪流般从盘边倾泻而下，力道之大，在运河中激起巨浪。
en: Water streamed in torrents over its sides with such force that it set up a huge wave in the canal.

zh: 存在这样的危险：巨浪可能从对岸反弹回来，再次将盘子掀入水中。
en: There was a danger that the wave would rebound off the other side of the bank and send the dish plunging into the water again.

zh: 众人以惊人的速度作业，终于在巨浪回涌之前将盘子拖上了岸。
en: By working at tremendous speed, the men managed to get the dish on to dry land before the wave returned.

zh: 你怎么看？
en: think?

zh: 长途旅行的人常常必须决定，究竟愿意取道陆路、海路还是空路。
en: People travelling long distances frequently have to decide whether they would prefer to go by land, sea, or air.

zh: 几乎没有人能在火车上安坐数小时之后仍感到真正愉快。
en: Hardly anyone can positively enjoy sitting in a train for more than a few hours.

zh: 车厢很快变得拥挤而闷塞。
en: Train compartments soon get cramped and stuffy.

zh: 要想不去想这段旅程，几乎是不可能的。
en: It is almost impossible 5 to take your mind off the journey.

zh: 阅读只是部分解脱，因为车轮敲击铁轨的单调节奏很快便会催人入睡。
en: Reading is only a partial solution, for the monotonous rhythm of the wheels clicking on the rails soon lulls you to sleep.

zh: 白天，睡眠断断续续。
en: During the day, sleep comes in snatches.

zh: 到了夜晚，当你真正想睡时，却往往难以成眠。
en: At night, when you really wish to go to sleep, you rarely manage to do so.

zh: 若你有幸订到卧铺，你会半夜盯着天花板上的小蓝灯，或摸索着找车票以备查票。
en: If you are lucky enough to get a sleeper, you spend half the night staring at the small blue light in the ceiling, or fumbling to find your ticket for inspection.

zh: 到头来，你抵达目的地时几乎精疲力竭。
en: Inevitably you arrive at your destination almost exhausted.

zh: 长途驾车更不惬意，因为连阅读都绝无可能。
en: Long car journeys are even less pleasant, for it is quite impossible even to read.

zh: 在高速公路上，你至少可以较安全地高速行驶，但多半时候，旅程的大部分都耗在服务站稀少、车辆拥挤的普通道路上。
en: On motorways you can, at least, travel fairly safely at high speeds, but more often than not, the greater part of the journey is spent on roads with few service stations and too much traffic.

zh: 相比之下，渡轮航行或邮轮旅行能提供种种文明而舒适的享受。
en: By comparison, ferry trips or cruises offer a great variety of civilized comforts.

zh: 你可以在宽敞的甲板上舒展双腿，做游戏，结识有趣的人，享用美食——当然，这要以海面平静为前提。
en: You can stretch your legs on the spacious decks, play games, meet interesting people and enjoy good food—always assuming, of course, that the sea is calm.

zh: 若海面并不平静，而你又容易晕船，则没有哪种交通方式比这更糟糕。
en: If it 15 is not, and you are likely to get seasick, no form of transport could be worse.

zh: 即便天气理想，海上航行也颇为耗时。
en: Even if you travel in ideal weather, sea journeys take a long time.

zh: 愿意为乘海船的乐趣而牺牲假期时间的人相对较少。
en: Relatively few people are prepared to sacrifice holiday time for the pleasure of travelling by sea.

zh: 飞机素有危险之名，即便久经旅途的人也常为之胆寒。
en: Aeroplanes have the reputation of being dangerous and even hardened travellers are intimidated by them.

zh: 它们还有费用高昂这一缺点。
en: They also have the disadvantage of being an expensive form of transport.

zh: 但在速度与舒适方面，没有什么能与飞机相比。
en: But nothing can match them 20 for speed and comfort.

zh: 在三万英尺高空，远在云层之上，以每小时逾五百英里的速度飞行，是一种令人振奋的体验。
en: Travelling at a height of 30,000 feet, far above the clouds, and at over 500 miles an hour is an exhilarating experience.

zh: 你不必想方设法转移对旅程的注意力，因为飞机会迅速将你送达目的地。
en: You do not have to devise ways of taking your mind off the journey, for an aeroplane gets you to your destination rapidly.

zh: 在数小时里，你深坐于宽大的扶手椅中，悠然享受飞行。
en: For a few hours, you settle back in a deep armchair to enjoy the flight.

zh: 真正的逃避现实者可在某些航班上观看电影、啜饮香槟。
en: The real escapist can watch a film and sip champagne on some services.

zh: 但即便没有这些雅致的享受，也有许多事可让你忙碌。
en: But even when such refinements are not available, there is plenty to keep you occupied.

zh: 飞机会为你呈现一幅非凡而惊心动魄的世界图景。
en: An aeroplane offers you an unusual 25 and breathtaking view of the world.

zh: 你轻松飞越高山深谷。
en: You soar effortlessly over high mountains and deep valleys.

zh: 你真正看清了地貌的轮廓。
en: You really see the shape of the land.

zh: 若地面景色隐没不见，你仍可欣赏那奇景：绵延数英里的云层如平原般铺展在脚下，晴空万里，阳光灿烂。旅途如此平稳，你尽可阅读或入睡。
en: If the landscape is hidden from view, you can enjoy the extraordinary sight of unbroken cloud plains that stretch out for miles before you, while the sun shines brilliantly in a clear sky The journey is so smooth that there is nothing to prevent you from reading or sleeping.

zh: 无论你打算如何打发时间，有一点是肯定的：你会神清气爽、衣衫平整地抵达目的地。
en: However you decide to spend your time, one thing is certain: you will rrive at your destination fresh and uncrumpled.

zh: 你不必再花数日时光，从漫长而艰辛的旅程中恢复过来。
en: You will 30 not have to spend the next few days recovering from a long and arduous journey.

zh: 在民主国家，任何限制新闻自由的企图都会受到正当的谴责。
en: In democratic countries any efforts to restrict the freedom of the press are rightly condemned.

zh: 然而，此种自由极易遭到滥用。
en: However, this freedom can easily be abused.

zh: 关于常人的报道往往较政治事件更能吸引公众关注。
en: Stories about people often attract far more public attention than political events.

zh: 我们虽或乐于披阅他人生平，但是否同样乐于披阅关于自己的文字，却极可怀疑。
en: Though we may enjoy reading about the lives of others, it is extremely doubtful whether we would equally enjoy reading about ourselves.

zh: 新闻记者秉持“事实神圣”之见，若发表有关私人生活之细节，便可能给个人带来难以估量之痛苦。
en: Acting on the contention that facts are sacred, reporters can cause untold suffering to individuals by publishing details about their private lives.

zh: 报纸影响如此巨大，不仅能改变普通民众之生活，甚至能推翻政府。
en: Newspapers exert such tremendous influence that they can not only bring about major changes to the lives of ordinary people but can even overthrow a government.

zh: 一户贫寒之家一朝成名致富之故事，生动地说明了新闻界之力量。
en: The story of a poor family that acquired fame and fortune overnight, dramatically illustrates the power of the press.

zh: 该家居于南达科他州阿伯丁，乃人口两万三千之小镇。
en: The family lived in Aberdeen, a small town of 23,000 inhabitants in South Dakota.

zh: 父母育有五子，生计与贫穷之争无休无止。
en: As the parents had five children, life was a perpetual struggle against poverty.

zh: 他们正待第六胎降生，经济困窘愈形紧迫。
en: They were expecting their sixth child and were faced with even more pressing economic problems.

zh: 若仅多一子，此事或无人留意。
en: If they had only had one more child, the fact would have passed unnoticed.

zh: 他们本将继续与逆境相搏，默默无闻以终。
en: They would have continued to struggle against economic odds and would have lived in obscurity.

zh: 然他们忽为五胞胎之父母——四女一男——此事彻底改变了其生活。
en: But they suddenly became the parents of quintuplets, four girls and a boy, an event which radically changed their lives.

zh: 五婴降生之次日，一飞机抵阿伯丁，载来记者与摄影师六十人。
en: The day after the birth of the five children, an aeroplane arrived in Aberdeen bringing sixty reporters and photographers.

zh: 成名之速，令人瞠目。
en: The rise to fame was swift.

zh: 电视镜头与报纸将消息传遍全国。
en: Television cameras and newspapers carried the news to everyone in the country.

zh: 报章杂志愿出巨资，以购独家报道与照片之权。
en: Newspapers and magazines offered the family huge sums for the exclusive rights to publish stories and photographs.

zh: 馈赠纷至沓来，既有素不相识者，亦有婴儿食品与肥皂厂商，欲借之广告其货。
en: Gifts poured in not only from unknown people, but from baby food and soap manufactur- ers who wished to advertise their products.

zh: 其旧农舍将易以价值五十万美元之新居。
en: The old farmhouse the family lived in was to be replaced by a new $500,000 home.

zh: 记者屡索采访，遂须聘律师于记者会上代为发言。
en: Reporters kept pressing for interviews so lawyers had to be employed to act as spokes- men for the family at press conferences.

zh: 五婴尚在医院育婴室氧气帐中安睡，其父母却已为成名付出代价。
en: While the five babies were stil quietly sleeping in oxygen tents in a hospital nursery, their parents were paying the price for fame.

zh: 彼等再难望过寻常日子。
en: It would never again be possible for them to lead normal lives.

zh: 彼等沦为商业化之牺牲品，盖其姓名已具市场价值。
en: They had become the victims of commercialization, for their names had acquired a market value.

zh: 此五子非但未为家中新丁，反立刻沦为商品。
en: Instead of being five new family members, these children had immediately become a commodity.

zh: 吾人热衷于凡事躬亲，其情如此炽烈，以致对专门分工之依赖日益减少。
en: So great is our passion for doing things for ourselves, that we are becoming increasingly less dependent on specialized labour.

zh: 今已无人能托辞不知，盖自学类出版物多不胜数。
en: No one can plead ignorance of a subject any longer, for there are countless do-it-yourself publications.

zh: 新婚夫妇既得相宜之器具与材料，便欣然着手装点新居。
en: Armed with the right tools 5 and materials, newlyweds gaily embark on the task of decorating their own homes.

zh: 男子尤喜以闲暇安装壁炉、布置园圃、搭建车房、制作家具。
en: Men, particularly, spend hours of their leisure time installing their own fireplaces, laying out their own gardens; building garages and making furniture.

zh: 有热衷者甚至自组电脑。
en: Some really keen enthusiasts go so far as to build their own computers.

zh: 商店迎合此风，不仅为初学者设咨询之务，亦售零部件供人于家中组装。
en: Shops cater for the do-it-yourselff craze not only by running special advisory services for novices, but by offering consumers bits and pieces 10 which they can assemble at home.

zh: 此类活动足为郁积之创造力辟一出路，然非人皆生而善工。
en: Such things provide an excellent outlet for pent up creative energy, but unfortunately not all of us are born handymen.

zh: 有妇人常以为其夫无所不能，无物不可修。
en: Some wives tend to believe that their husbands are infinitely resourceful and can fix anything.

zh: 即连钉子亦钉不直者，亦被视作天生之电工、木匠、水管工与机修工。
en: Even men who can hardly drive a nail in straight are supposed to be born electricians, carpenters, plumbers and me- chanics.

zh: 电灯保险丝烧断、家具摇晃、管道堵塞，或吸尘器失灵，有些妇女便以为丈夫总能设法修好。
en: When lights fuse, furniture gets rickety, pipes get clogged, or vacuum cleaners fail to operate, some 15 women assume that their husbands will somehow put things right.

zh: 躬亲其事之最糟处在于，有时男子亦沉溺于无所不能之妄念，虽屡经证谬而不悟。
en: The worst thing about the do-it-yourself game is that sometimes even men live under the delusion that they can do anything, even when they have repeatedly been proved wrong.

zh: 此事关乎颜面者多矣。
en: It is a question of pride as much as anything else.

zh: 去年春天，妻子劝我请人检查一下割草机。
en: Last spring my wife suggested that I call in a man to look at our lawn mower.

zh: 割草机于去岁夏即已损坏，我虽许诺修理，迄未得暇。
en: It had broken down the previous summer, and though I promised to repair it, I had never got round to it.

zh: 我不纳其议，谓必亲自动手。
en: I would not hear of the 20 suggestion and said that I would fix it myself.

zh: 某周六午后，我将机器拖至园中细察。
en: One Saturday afternoon, I hauled the machineinto the garden and had a close look at it.

zh: 据我观之，仅需微调：此处拧一螺钉，彼处略紧，滴油数滴，便可如新。
en: As far as I could see, it needed only a minor adjustment: a turn of a screw here, a little tightening up there, a drop of oil and it would be as good as new.

zh: 不消说，修理并不如此简单。
en: Inevitably the repair job was not quite so simple.

zh: 割草机拒不工作，我遂决意拆之。
en: The mower firmly refused to mow, so I decided to dismantle it.

zh: 未几，园中遍布曾为割草机之金属碎块。
en: The garden was soon littered with chunks of metal which had once made up a lawn mower.

zh: 然我颇以自得。
en: But I was extremely pleased with myself.

zh: 我已查明故障之由。
en: I had 25 traced the cause of the trouble.

zh: 驱动车轮之链条中有一环断裂。
en: One of the links in the chain that drives the wheels had snapped.

zh: 购得新链后，又须面对将此一团乱麻重新拼合之艰巨任务。
en: After buying a new chain I was faced with the insurmountable task of puting the confusing jigsaw puzzle together again.

zh: 重组后机器仍不运转，我亦不以为异——盖尚余数片形状怪异之金属，无处安顿。
en: I was not surprised to find that the machine stillrefused to work after I had reassembled it, for the simple reason that I was left with several curiously shaped bits of metal which did not seem to fit anywhere.

zh: 我绝望罢手。
en: I gave up in despair.

zh: 数周既过，野草渐长。
en: The weeks passed and the grass grew.

zh: 妻子一再催我处理，我说要么买一台新的割草机，要么就任凭野草生长。
en: When my wife nagged me to do something about it, I 30 told her that either I would have to buy a new mower or let the grass grow.

zh: 毋庸赘言，舍外已为丛林所围。
en: Needless to say our house is now surrounded by a jungle.

zh: 深草之中，有一生锈之割草机，我曾许他日修之。
en: Buried somewhere in deep grass there is a rusting lawn mower which I have prom- ised to repair one day.

zh: 第三单元 第四十六课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 46

zh: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
en: Listen to the tape then answer the question below.

zh: 污染乃吾人为人口过剩、过度工业化所付之代价。
en: Pollution is the price we pay for an overpopulated, over- industrialized planet.

zh: 细思之，处置垃圾不过四途：倾倒、焚烧、化废为用，或力求少产。
en: When you come to think about it, there are only four ways you can deal with rubbish: dump it, burn it, turn it into something you can use again, attempt to produce less of it.

zh: 吾人屡试四法，然全球垃圾之巨，几欲淹没吾人。
en: We keep trying all four methods, but the sheer volume of rubbish we produce worldwide threatens to overwhelm us.

zh: 然垃圾仅为污染地球问题之一端。
en: Rubbish, however, is only part of the problem of polluting our planet.

zh: 欲增产廉价食品之需求，又生另一类污染。
en: The need to produce ever- increasing quantities of cheap food leads to a different kind of pollution.

zh: 工业化耕作产廉价之肉：牛、猪、鸡。
en: Industrialized farming methods produce cheap meat products: beef, pork and chicken.

zh: 杀虫剂与化肥之使用，使谷物蔬菜价格低廉。
en: The use of pesticides and fertilizers produces cheap grain and vegetables.

zh: 廉价食品之代价或已过高：牛之疯牛病（BSE）、鸡卵之沙门氏菌、乳品之李斯特菌。
en: The price we pay for cheap food may be already too high: Mad Cow Disease(BSE)in cattle, salmonella in chicken and eggs, and listeria in dairy products.

zh: 若谓戒肉茹素即可免祸，则非购昂贵之有机菜蔬，即于自以为啖新鲜色拉菜蔬、或仅饮清水之际，恒摄农药。
en: And if you think you'll abandon meat and become a vegetarian, you have the choice of very expensive organically-grown vegetables or a steady diet of pesticides every time you think you're eating fresh salads and vegetables, or just having an innocent glass of water!

zh: 然犹有一种更为阴毒之污染，尤侵城镇、渗入日常，即噪声。
en: However, there is an even more insidious kind of pollution that particularly affects urban areas and invades our daily lives, and that is noise.

zh: 防盗警报昼夜作响，徒惹路人，反助盗贼。
en: Burglar alarms going off at any time of the day or night serve only to annoy passers-by and actually assist burglars to burgle.

zh: 汽车警报于街头厉鸣，令人深以为苦。
en: Car alarms constantly scream at us in the street and are a source of profound iritation.

zh: 近期一项噪声影响之调查揭示（可异乎？）：夜间犬吠不已，于一分至七分之量表上，列为最重之噪声污染。
en: A recent survey of the effects of noise revealed(surprisingly?)that dogs barking incessantly in the night rated the highest form of noise pollution on a scale ranging from 1 to 7.

zh: 调查又列诸多吾人深恶之噪声源。
en: The survey revealed a large number of sources of noise that we really dislike.

zh: 夏日割草机之嗡鸣、公寓深夜之宴聚、喧邻、各类车辆——尤巨箱货车穿静村而过者——、头顶之飞机直升机、公共场所大开音量之收音机。
en: Lawn mowers whining on a summer's day, late-night parties in apartment blocks, noisy neighbours, vehicles of all kinds, especially large container trucks thundering through quiet villages, planes and helicopters flying overhead, large radios carried round in public places and played at maximum volume.

zh: 新技术亦为噪声推波助澜。
en: New technology has also made its own contribution to noise.

zh: 多人厌移动电话，尤恶其用于餐馆或公共交通。
en: A lot of people object to mobile phones, especially when they are used in public places like restaurants or on public transport.

zh: 高声通话扰人思绪，或打断与友静谈之雅。
en: Loud conversations on mobile phones invade our thoughts or interrupt the pleasure of meeting friends for a quiet chat.

zh: 噪声调查又揭一颇出意料、或亦堪一笑之陈旧噪声源。
en: The noise pollution survey revealed a rather surprising and possibly amusing old- fashioned source of noise.

zh: 乃打鼾。
en: It turned out to be snoring!

zh: 男子为尤甚者。
en: Men were found to be the worst offenders.

zh: 调查显示，三十五岁许之男子，百分之二十打鼾。
en: It was revealed that 20%of men in their mid-thirties snore.

zh: 六十岁男子中，此数升至百分之六十，令人震惊。
en: This figure rises to a staggering 60%of men in their sixties.

zh: 相较之下，仅百分之五之妇人常鼾，余者则为鼾声如雷之伴侣所扰，不得安眠。
en: Against these figures, it was found that only 5%of women snore regularly, while the rest are constantly woken or kept awake by their trumpeting partners.

zh: 不论噪声何来，有一点可定：宁静似已成珍贵之回忆。
en: Whatever the source of noise, one thing is certain: silence, it seems, has become a golden memory.

zh: 于此足迹遍及之世，犹有万千去处，游人难至。
en: In this much-travelled world, there are still thousands of places which are inaccessible to tourists.

zh: 吾人总谓偏远村民友善好客。
en: We always assume that villagers in remote places are friendly and hospitable.

zh: 然若人不仅与外国游客隔绝，且与本国同胞亦隔绝，便可能对旅人怀敌意。
en: But people who are cut off not only from foreign tourists, but even from their own countrymen can be hostile to travellers.

zh: 造访真正偏远之村落，罕能愉快——拙荆与余游巴尔干时深有体会。
en: Visits to really remote villages are seldom enjoyable —as my wife and I discovered during a tour through the Balkans.

zh: 吾等于一小城盘桓数日，并谒附近多座古教堂。
en: We had spent several days in a smalltown and visited a number of old churches in the vicinity.

zh: 教堂吸引众多游客，盖其不仅建筑可贵，且藏大量保存完好之壁画。
en: These attracted many visitors, for they were not only of great architectural interest, but contained a large number of 10 beautifully preserved frescoes as well.

zh: 启程前一日，数大巴载游客涌入该城。
en: On the day before our departure, several bus loads of tourists de- scended on the town.

zh: 吾等不堪其扰，遂决以末日探乡村。
en: This was more than we could bear, so we decided to spend our last day exploring the countryside.

zh: 循一出城小径，穿数畦，至密林。
en: Taking a path which led out of the town, we crossed a few fields until we came to a dense wood.

zh: 吾等以为路将尽，然见其蜿蜒穿林。
en: We expected the path to end abruptly, but we found that it traced its way through the trees.

zh: 吾等于林中跋涉逾二时，至一深水溪。
en: We tramped through the wood for over two hours until we arrived at a deep stream.

zh: 可见对岸仍有路，然不知如何渡溪。
en: We could see that the path continued 15 on the other side, but we had no idea how we could get across the stream.

zh: 忽而拙荆见岸边系一小舟。
en: Suddenly my wife spotted a boat moored to the bank.

zh: 舟中一艄公正酣睡。
en: In it there was a boatman fast asleep.

zh: 吾等轻唤之，请渡至对岸。
en: We gently woke him up and asked him to ferry us to the other side.

zh: 彼初不愿，终为吾等说服。
en: Though he was reluctant to do so at first, we eventually persuaded him to take us.

zh: 小径通至陡坡上一小村。
en: The path led to a tiny village perched on the steep sides of a mountain.

zh: 村中唯有一条蜿蜒土路，两旁小屋林立。
en: The place consisted of a strag- gling unmade road which was lined on either side by small houses.

zh: 即晴空之下，村子亦显阴森，盖屋皆灰泥砖所筑。
en: Even under a clear blue sky, the village 20 looked forbidding, as all the houses were built of grey mud bricks.

zh: 村子似已荒废，唯一生气乃近处田中树上短绳所系一貌丑黑山羊。
en: The village seemed deserted, the only sign of life being an ugly-looking black goat on a short length of rope tied to a tree in a field nearby.

zh: 吾等于田边破木栅坐定，启两听沙丁鱼，野餐而食。
en: Sitting down on a dilapidated wooden fence near the field, we opened a couple oftins of sardines and had a picnic lunch.

zh: 忽觉拙荆似满怀惊惧。
en: All at once, I noticed that my wife seemed to be filled with alarm.

zh: 抬头则见一群衣衫褴褛之童子默围吾等，观吾等进食。
en: Looking up I saw that we were surrounded by children in rags who were looking at us silently as we ate.

zh: 吾等馈之以食，温言相慰，彼等仍呆立不动。
en: We offered them food and spoke to 25 them kindly, but they remained motionless.

zh: 我断其仅羞于见生人。
en: I concluded that they were simply shy of strangers.

zh: 后吾等沿村主街行，身后默随一队童子。
en: When we later walked down the main street of the village, we were followed by a silent procession of children.

zh: 似已荒废之村，霎时活络起来。
en: The village which had seemed deserted, immediately came to life.

zh: 窗间现出面庞。
en: Faces appeared at windows.

zh: 着衬衫之男子立于屋外，怒目而视。
en: Men in shirt sleeves stood outside their houses and glared at us.

zh: 披黑披肩之老妪于门隙窥视。
en: Old women in black shawls peered at us from doorways.

zh: 最可怖者，乃四周寂然无声。
en: The most frightening thing of all was that not a sound could be heard.

zh: 毫无疑问，我们是不受欢迎的客人。
en: There was no doubt that we were 30 unwelcome visitors.

zh: 我们不需要更多的警告。
en: We needed no further warning.

zh: 我们转身沿着大街往回走，加快脚步，匆匆向小溪赶去，希望船夫还在那里等候。
en: Turning back down the main street, we quickened our pace and made our way rapidly towards the stream where we hoped the boatman was waiting.

zh: 第三单元 第四十八课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 48

zh: 幸而哈里特姑妈多年前已谢世。
en: It is a good thing my aunt Harriet died years ago.

zh: 若其今日犹存，便无法就其最喜谈论之题——家政仆役——大发议论。
en: If she were alive today she would not be able to air her views on her favourite topic of conversation: domestic servants.

zh: 哈里特姑妈生于从容之世，彼时雇人理家乃寻常事。
en: Aunt Harriet lived in that leisurely age when servants were employed to do housework.

zh: 彼有一座庞大零乱之乡间宅第，名曰“山墙庄”。
en: She had a huge, rambling country house called 'The Gables'.

zh: 彼对此宅眷恋甚深；虽于彼过大，夫亡后仍久居不去。
en: She was sentimentally attached to this house, for even though it was far too big for her needs, she persisted in living there long after her husband’s death.

zh: 未老之前，哈里特姑妈常盛宴宾朋。
en: Before she grew old, Aunt Harriet used to entertain lavishly.

zh: 幼时常往山墙庄做客。
en: I often visited The Gables when I was a boy.

zh: 无论来客几何，大宅恒一尘不染。
en: No matter how many guests were present, the great house was always immaculate.

zh: 镶木地板光可鉴人；擦净之银器陈于明亮玻璃柜；即姑丈大批藏书，亦奇迹般无尘。
en: The parquet floors shone like mirrors; highly polished silver was displayed in gleaming glass cabinets; even my uncle's huge collection of books was kept miraculously free from dust.

zh: 哈里特姑妈统率一支无形仆役大军，不停洗刷擦拭。
en: Aunt Harriet presided over an invisible army of servants that continuously scrubbed, cleaned, and polished.

zh: 彼恒称之为“流动人口”，盖其来去太频，我竟未能识其名。
en: She always referred to them as 'the shifting population', for they came and went with such frequency that I never even got a chance to learn their names.

zh: 虽姑妈当时政策颇为开明，从不令仆役日劳逾八时，然极难取悦。
en: Though my aunt pursued what was, in those days, an enlightened policy, in that she never allowed her domestic staff to work more than eight hours a day, she was extremely difficult to please.

zh: 彼一面常讥人性无常，一面至死犹觅理想仆役，即于贝茜令其大失所望之后亦然。
en: While she always criticized the fickleness of human nature, she carried on an unrelenting search for the ideal servant to the end of her days, even after she had been sadly disillusioned by Bessie.

zh: 贝茜事哈里特姑妈三年。
en: Bessie worked for Aunt Harriet for three years.

zh: 其间深得姑妈信任，受命总管家事仆役。
en: During that time she so gained my aunt's confidence, that she was put in charge of the domestic staff.

zh: 哈里特姑妈赞贝茜勤劳干练，辞不胜赞。
en: Aunt Harriet could not find words to praise Bessie's indus- triousness and efficiency.

zh: 除诸般长处，贝茜亦精烹调。
en: In addition to all her other qualifications, Bessie was an expert cook.

zh: 彼扮演完美仆役凡三年，直至姑妈察其“小疵”。
en: She acted the role of the perfect servant for three years before Aunt Harriet discovered her 'little weakness'.

zh: 离山墙庄一周后，一日午后姑妈忽携众客归，命贝茜备晚宴。
en: After being absent from The Gables for a week, my aunt unexpectedly returned one afternoon with a party of guests and instructed Bessie to prepare dinner.

zh: 菜肴不仅远逊常格，贝茜步履亦似不稳。
en: Not only was the meal well below the usual standard, but Bessie seemed unable to walk steadily.

zh: 彼频频触家具，口中喃喃，皆关客人。
en: She bumped into the furniture and kept mumbling about the guests.

zh: 彼端末道大布丁入厅时，绊于地毯，布丁腾空飞出，几中姑妈，猛击餐桌。
en: When she camee in with the last course—a huge pudding--she tripped on the carpet and the pudding went flying through the air, narrowly missed my aunt, and crashed on the dining table with considerable force.

zh: 宾客虽哄堂，哈里特姑妈则骇极。
en: Though this caused great mirth among the guests, Aunt Harriet was horrified.

zh: 彼不得已认定贝茜已醉。
en: She reluctantly came to the conclusion that Bessie was drunk.

zh: 宾客自贝茜开门迎客便知，惨剧未至之前，久已强忍笑意。
en: The guests had, of course, realized this from the moment Bessie opened the door for them and, long before the final catastrophe, had had a difficult time trying to conceal their amusement.

zh: 可怜女子当即被遣。
en: The poor girl was dismissed instantly.

zh: 贝茜去后，姑妈于彼旧衣柜中发现成堆空瓶，形色各异，叠放齐整。
en: After her departure, Aunt Harriet discovered that there were piles of empty wine bottles of all shapes and sizes neatly stacked in what had once been Bessie's wardrobe.

zh: 瓶竟自酒窖神不知鬼不觉至此！
en: They had mysteriously found their way there from the wine cellar!

zh: 新年乃立志之时。
en: The New Year is a time for resolutions.

zh: 至少在心理上，吾人多能列一长串“当为”与“勿为”。
en: Mentally, at least, most of us could compile formidable lists of 'dos'and ‘don'ts'.

zh: 年年岁岁，总不外乎那几项，单调重复。
en: The same old favourites recur year in year out with monotonous regularity.

zh: 吾人立志早起、节食、多陪孩子、理家中千端万绪、善待所恶之人、谨慎驾车、日遛狗。
en: We resolve to get up earlier each morning, eat less, find more time 5 to play with the children, do a thousand and one jobs about the house, be nice to people we don't like, drive carefully, and take the dog for a walk every day.

zh: 往事告吾人，有若干目标本不可达。
en: Past experience has taught us that certain accomplishments are beyond attainment.

zh: 若吾人烟瘾难戒，乃因屡尝失败之沮丧。
en: If we remain inveterate smokers, it is only because we have so often experienced the frustration that results from failure.

zh: 吾人自我完善多败，因计划过奢，永无时间践行。
en: Most of us fail in our efforts at self-improvement 10 because our schemes are too ambitious and we never have time to carry them out.

zh: 吾人又犯根本之误：向众人宣之于口，故态复萌时愈显可笑。
en: We also make the fundamental error of announcing our resolutions to everybody so that we look even more foolish when we slip back into our bad old ways.

zh: 深知此等陷阱，今年吾力图秘而不宣。
en: Aware of these pitfalls, this year I attempted to keep my resolutions to myself.

zh: 吾仅立二不甚奢之愿：每晨锻炼，晚间多读书。
en: I limited myself to two modest ambitions: to do physical exercises every morning and to read more of an evening.

zh: 除夕通宵之宴，为吾岁首不行此二愿得绝佳借口；至次日，吾乃专心从事。
en: An all-night party on New Year's Eve provided me with a good excuse for not carrying out 15 either of these new resolutions on the first day of the year, but on the second, I applied myself assiduously to the task.

zh: 每日锻炼仅十一分，吾拟于清晨众人未起前为之。
en: The daily exercises lasted only eleven minutes and I proposed to do them early in the morning before anyone had got up.

zh: 较平日早十一分起床所需之自律，殊非小可。
en: The self-discipline required to drag myself out of bed eleven minutes earlier than usual was considerable.

zh: 然吾仍潜至起居室二日，始为人觉。
en: Nevertheless, I managed to creep down into the living room for two days before anyone 20 found me out.

zh: 于地毯上跳跃、扭身成种种不适姿势后，吾疲惫不堪就早餐席。
en: After jumping about on the carpet and twisting the human frame into uncomfortable posi- tions, I sat down at the breakfast table in an exhausted condition.

zh: 正此露了马脚。
en: It was this that betrayed me.

zh: 翌晨全家结队来观此“戏”。
en: The next morning the whole family trooped in to watch the performance.

zh: 诚然令人不安，然吾和颜拒之嘲谑，未几众人亦习以为常。
en: That was really unsettling, but I fended off the taunts and jibes of the family good-humouredly and soon everybody got used to the idea.

zh: 然吾热情渐消。
en: However, my enthusiasm waned.

zh: 用于锻炼之时日递减。
en: The time I spent at exercises gradually diminished.

zh: 十一分一点一点减至零。
en: Little by little the eleven minutes fell 25 to zero.

zh: 至一月十日，吾复归起点。
en: By January 10th, Iwas back to where I had started from.

zh: 吾辩曰：若晨间少耗气力于锻炼，则下班后脑筋清爽，宜于读书。
en: I argued that ifI spent less time exhausting myself at exercises in the morning, I would keep my mind fresh for reading when I got home from work.

zh: 吾拒电视之催眠，数夕坐于房中，目不转睛读书。
en: Resisting the hypnotizing effect of television, I sat in my room for a few evenings with myeyes glued to a book.

zh: 然一夕觉冷且寂，吾下楼坐电视前，佯作披阅。
en: One night, however, feeling cold and lonely, I went downstairs and sat in front of the television pretending to read.

zh: 此遂败吾事，因吾旋复旧习，于屏前打盹。
en: That proved to be my undoing, for I soon got back to my old bad habit of dozing off in 30 front of the screen.

zh: 吾犹未弃多读书之志。
en: I still haven't given up my resolution to do more reading.

zh: 实则吾刚购一书，题曰《如何一分钟读一千词》。
en: In fact, I have just bought a book entitled How to Read a Thousand Words a Minute.

zh: 或能解吾困，然吾偏无暇读之！
en: Perhaps it will solve my problem, but I just haven't had time to read it!

zh: 第三单元 第五十课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 50

zh: 预言未来素称难事。
en: Predicting the future is notoriously difficult.

zh: 例如二十世纪七十年代中期，谁能料二十世纪末计算机会如电视机一般入千家万户？
en: Who could have imagined, in the mid 1970s, for example, that by the end of the 20th century, computers would be as common in people's homes as TV sets?

zh: 七十年代计算机已颇常见，然仅在大企业、政府部门与大型机构。
en: In the 1970s, computers were common enough, but only in big business, government departments, and large organizations.

zh: 此即所谓主机。
en: These were the so-called mainframe machines.

zh: 主机体积确甚庞大，常占满整间空调机房，雇专职技师，运行专撰之软件。
en: Mainframe computers were very large indeed, often occupying whole air-conditioned rooms, employing full-time technicians and run on specially-written software.

zh: 虽此类大机犹存，其多功能已为小巧强劲之个人电脑——通称PC——所取代。
en: Though these large machines still exist, many of their functions have been taken over by smallpowerful personal computers, commonly known as PCs.

zh: 一九七五年，美国推出一原始机器，名曰Altair。
en: In 1975, a primitive machine called the Altair, was launched in the USA.

zh: 其可恰如其分称为首台“家用电脑”，并昭示未来方向。
en: It can properly be described as thefirst 'home computer'and it pointed the way to the future.

zh: 七十年代末，继有名为Apple之机器。
en: This was followed, at the end of the 1970s, by a machine called an Apple.

zh: 八十年代初，计算机巨擘IBM推出世界首台个人电脑。
en: In the early 1980s, the computer giant, IBM produced the world's first Personal Computer.

zh: 其运行于名曰DOS之操作系统，该系统由当时尚小之微软公司所制。IBM个人电脑广为仿造。
en: This ran on an 'operating system’called DOS, produced by a then small company named Microsoft The IBM Personal Computer was widely copied.

zh: 自彼卑微开端，遂有今日通用之易用家用电脑与多媒体机。
en: From those humble beginnings, we have seen the develop- 15 ment of the user-friendly home computers and multimedia machines which are in common use today.

zh: 此等进展何其新近，更令人惊叹者，早在二十世纪六十年代，英国人莱昂·巴格里特已能预见计算机之若干当今用途。
en: Considering how recent these developments are, it is even more remarkable that as long ago as the 1960s, an Englishman, Leon Bagrit, was able to predict some of the uses of computers which we know today.

zh: 巴格里特斥计算机会自学“思考”、将“统治世界”之说——当时人乐信之。
en: Bagrit dismissed the idea that computers would learn to 'think’for themselves and would 'rule the world’, which people liked to believe in those days.

zh: 巴格里特预见计算机会小至可握掌中，能示交通拥堵并建议改道，能于医院助医诊病，能替职员与会计脱却枯燥重复之文牍。
en: Bagrit foresaw a time when computers would be small 20 enough to hold in the hand, when they would be capable of providing information about traffic jams and suggesting alternative routes, when they would be used in hospitals to help doctors to diagnose illnesses, when they would relieve office workers and accountants of dull, repetitive clerical work.

zh: 凡此计算机之用，今已司空见惯。
en: Allthese computer uses have become commonplace.

zh: 然莱昂·巴格里特断不能预见互联网——此一全球体系，使吾人借与电话网相连之计算机，可与世界任何角落之人即时通讯。
en: Of course, Leon Bagrit could not possibly have foreseen the development of the Internet, the worldwide system that enables us to communicate instantly with anyone in any part of 25 the world by using computers linked to telephone networks.

zh: 彼亦不能预见吾人如何借互联网获取已知一切主题之资讯，于家中屏上披阅，欲时亦可打印。
en: Nor could he have foresecn how we could use the Internet to obtain information on every known subject, so we can read it on a screen in our homes and even print it as well if we want to.

zh: 计算机愈小愈强，亦愈廉。
en: Computers have become smaller and smaller, more and more powerful and cheaper and cheaper.

zh: 此正使莱昂·巴格里特之预见尤为非凡。
en: This is what makes Leon Bagrit's predictions particularly remarkable.

zh: 若彼或类彼者今日犹存，或能告吾人未来五十年将如何。
en: If he, or someone like him, were alive today, he might be able to tell us what to expect in the next fifty years.

zh: 表兄哈里于其书房永久陈列一形状奇诡之大瓶。
en: My cousin, Harry, keeps a large curiously-shaped bottle on permanent display in his study.

zh: 瓶虽染淡雅绿色，细心之访客不久即觉其中盛满看似浓稠灰白之物。若问哈里瓶中何物，彼谓乃带香之泥。
en: Despite the fact that the bottle is tinted a delicate shade of green, an observant visitor would soon notice that it is filled with what looks like a thick, greyish substance. 5 If you were to ask Harry what was in the bottle, he would tell you that it contained perfumed mud.

zh: 倘若你表示怀疑或惊讶，他便会请你闻一闻，再取少许涂在皮肤上。
en: If you expressed doubt or surprise, he would immediately invite you to smell it and then to rub some into your skin.

zh: 这一简单的试验足以打消你可能尚存的一切疑虑。
en: This brief experiment would dispel any further doubts you might have.

zh: 瓶中确含带香之泥。
en: The bottle really does contain perfumed mud.

zh: 哈里如何得此异物，有一段有趣故事，彼喜为人述之。
en: How Harry came into the possession of this outlandish stuff makes an interesting 10 story which he is fond of relating.

zh: 且得此瓶亦使彼戒除多年所染之恶习。
en: Furthermore, the acquisition of this botle cured him of a bad habit he had been developing for years.

zh: 哈里昔以为乐：入昂贵化妆品店，索不存在之货。
en: Harry used to consider it a great joke to go into expensive cosmetic shops and make outrageous requests for goods that do not exist.

zh: 彼当场捏造离奇之名。
en: He would invent fanciful names on the spot.

zh: 进店便索新香水曰“香影”，或“不溶浴块”。
en: On entering a shop, he would ask for a new perfume called 'Scented Shadow'or for ‘insoluble bath cubes'.

zh: 若店员谓未闻其名，彼即佯怒。
en: If a shop assistant told him she had 15 not heard of it, he would pretend to be considerably put out.

zh: 彼最爱闻称其虚构之货暂缺，并郑重约他日再来，然从不践约。
en: He loved to be told that one of his imaginary products was temporarily out of stock and he would faithfully promise to call again at some future date, but of course he never did.

zh: 哈里于此等表演中如何板脸，吾百思不解。
en: How Harry managed to keep a straight face during these performances is quite beyond me.

zh: 哈里无需人催，便会述其如何购得此瓶珍贵之泥。
en: Harry does not need to be prompted to explain how he bought his precious bottle of mud.

zh: 一日，彼至伦敦一高档店，索“Myrolite”。
en: One day, he 20 went to an exclusive shop in London and asked for 'Myrolite'.

zh: 店员茫然，哈里乃缓声重述，一字一顿。
en: The shop assistant looked puzzled and Harry repeated the word, slowly stressing each syllable.

zh: 妇人困惑摇头时，哈里又释“myrolite”为坚硬琥珀状之物，可去雀斑。
en: When the woman shook her head in bewilderment, Harry went on to explain that‘myrolite'was a hard, amber-like substance which could be used to remove freckles.

zh: 此释显然使妇人有所领会，乃层层架搜寻。
en: This explanation evidently conveyed something to the woman who searched shelf after shelf.

zh: 彼取出种种古怪调和之物，无一合哈里之意。
en: She produced all sorts of weird concoctions, but none of them met with Harry's requirements.

zh: 哈里微愠作态时，店员允为订购。
en: When Harry put on his act 25 of being mildly annoyed, the assistant promised to order some for him.

zh: 成功使彼陶醉。
en: Intoxicated by his success.

zh: 哈里继索带香之泥。
en: Harry then asked for perfumed mud.

zh: 彼原以为店员将瞠目结舌。
en: He expected the assistant to look at him in blank astonishment.

zh: 然轮到彼吃惊：妇人双目顿亮，取数瓶置柜上供哈里检视。
en: However, it was his turn to be surprised, for the woman's eyes immediately lit up and she fetched several bottles which she placed on the counter for Harry to inspect.

zh: 此番哈里只得认输。
en: For once, Harry had to admit defeat.

zh: 彼取看似最小之一瓶，低声问价。
en: He picked up what seemed to be the smallest bottle and discreetly asked the price.

zh: 他庆幸自己只花了区区二十英镑便得以脱身，于是匆匆离去，将那只珍贵的瓶子紧夹在腋下。
en: He was glad to get away with a mere twenty 30 pounds and he beat a hasty retreat, clutching the precious bottle under his arm.

zh: 从那时起，哈里便断定，他发明的这套小把戏也许会代价不菲。
en: From then on, Harry decided that this little game he had invented might prove to be expensive.

zh: 那只奇异的瓶子如今陈列在他书房的书架上，是他首次亦是最后一次购置名贵化妆品的经历。
en: The curious bottle, which now adorns the bookcase in his study, was his first and last purchase of rare cosmetics.

zh: 第三册 第五十二课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 52

zh: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
en: Listen to the tape then answer the question below.

zh: 斯堪的纳维亚各国因其开明的社会政策而备受世人称道。
en: The Scandinavian countries are much admired all over the world for their enlightened social policies.

zh: 瑞典已经形成了一套完善的制度，用以保护普通公民免受专横或无能的公职人员之害。
en: Sweden has evolved an excellent system for protecting the individual citizen from high- handed or incompetent public officers.

zh: 该制度行之有效，因而亦为其他国家所采纳。
en: The system has worked so well, that it has been adopted in other countries too.

zh: 瑞典人最先认识到，诸如公务员、警官、卫生稽查员或税务人员等公职人员亦可能失误，或出于自以为在为民效劳的信念而行为过当。
en: The Swedes were the first to recognize that public officials like civil servants, police officers, health inspectors or tax-collectors can make mistakes or act over-zealously in the belief that they are serving the public.

zh: 早在1809年，瑞典议会即推行一项方案，以维护个人权益。
en: As long ago as 1809, the Swedish Parliament introduced a scheme to safeguard the interest of the individual.

zh: 一个代表各政党的议会委员会任命一名适格人士，负责调查个人对国家的不满情事。
en: A parliamentary committee representing all political parties appoints a person who is suitably qualified to investigate private grievances against the State.

zh: 该职员的正式称谓为「Justitieombudsman」（司法专员），但瑞典人通常称其为「J.」。
en: The official title of the person is 'Justiteombudsman³, but the Swedes commonly refer to him as the 'J.

zh: 或称「申诉专员」（Ombudsman）。
en: 'or 'Ombudsman'.

zh: 申诉专员不受政治压力左右。
en: The Ombudsman is not subject to political pressure.

zh: 他调查来自社会各阶层、大大小小纷至沓来的投诉。
en: He investigates complaints large and small that come to him from all levels of society.

zh: 因投诉须以书面提出，申诉专员每年平均收到约一千二百封信函。
en: As complaints must be made in writing, the Ombudsman receives an average of 1,200 letters a year.

zh: 他有八名律师助理协助，并逐一详审每一封信。
en: He has eight lawyer assistants to help him and he examines every single letter in detail.

zh: 专员的公干并无秘密可言，因为其往来函件均可接受公众查阅。
en: There is nothing secretive about the Ombudsman's work, for his correspondence is open to public inspection.

zh: 若公民投诉有理，专员将代其采取行动。
en: If a citizen's complaint is justified, the Ombudsman will act on his behalf.

zh: 所采取的措施视投诉性质而定。
en: The action he takes varies according to the nature of the complaint.

zh: 他或委婉申斥有关官员，甚至建议议会修改法律。
en: He may gently reprimand an official or even suggest to parliament that a law be altered.

zh: 下列案例即可典型地说明专员的职责范围。
en: The following case is a typical example of the Ombudsman's work.

zh: 一名侨居瑞典某村庄的外国人致函专员，声称只因自己是外国人而遭警察虐待。
en: A foreigner living in a Swedish village wrote to the Ombudsman complaining that he had been ill-treated by the police, simply because he was a foreigner.

zh: 专员当即致函该地警察局长，索阅该案记录。
en: The Ombudsman immediately wrote to the Chief of Police in the district asking him to send a record of the case.

zh: 记录中并无材料表明该外国人的投诉有据，警察局长亦断然否认指控。
en: There was nothing in the record to show that the foreigner's complaint was justified and the Chief of Police strongly denied the accusation.

zh: 专员虽无法采取行动，但当同村另一名外国人提出类似投诉时，他立即派遣一名律师前往调查。
en: It was impossible for the Ombudsman to take action, but when hé received a similar complaint from another foreigner in the same village, he immediately sent one of his lawyers to investigate the matter.

zh: 律师查明确有一名警察曾数次粗暴对待外国人。
en: The lawyer ascertained that a policeman had indeed dealt roughly with foreigners on several occasions.

zh: 该警察对外国人抱有成见这一事实无法记入官方档案。
en: The fact that the policeman was prejudiced against foreigners could not be recorded in the official files.

zh: 专员唯有派出代表核实情况，方能查明此事。
en: It was only possible for the Ombudsman to find this out by sending one of his representatives to check the facts.

zh: 有关警察受到严厉申饬，并被告知若再有人投诉，将对其提出检控。
en: The policeman in question was severely reprimanded and was informed that if any further complaints were lodged against him, he would be prosecuted.

zh: 专员的迅速行动立即使一种本来可能无人过问的不当做法戛然而止。
en: The Ombudsman's prompt action at once put an end to an unpleasant practice which might have gone unnoticed.

zh: 我们自幼便耳濡目染，对昆虫心存畏惧。
en: We have been brought up to fear insects.

zh: 我们将它们视为有害无益的多余生物。
en: We regard them as unnecessary creatures that do more harm than good.

zh: 我们对它们不断宣战，因为它们玷污食物、传播疾病，或啃食庄稼。
en: We continually wage war on them, for they contaminate our food, carry diseases, or devour our crops.

zh: 它们无故蜇人或叮咬；夏日的夜晚，它们不请自来飞进我们的房间，或扑撞亮着的窗玻璃。
en: They sting or bite without provocation; they 5 fly uninvited into our rooms on summer nights, or beat against our lighted windows.

zh: 我们不仅惧怕蜘蛛、黄蜂之类令人不快的昆虫，就连蛾子等并无害处的昆虫亦心怀恐惧。
en: We live in dread not only of unpleasant insects like spiders or wasps, but ofquite harmless ones like moths.

zh: 阅读有关昆虫的著述虽能增进了解，却不足以消除我们的畏惧。
en: Reading about them increases our understanding without dispelling our fears.

zh: 我们明知勤劳的蚂蚁生活在组织严密的社会之中，但当发现成群蚂蚁爬满精心准备的野餐午餐时，仍不免心生厌恶。
en: Knowing that the industrious ant lives in a highly organized society does nothing to prevent us from being filled with revulsion when we find 10 hordes of them crawling over a carefully prepared picnic lunch.

zh: 无论我们多喜爱蜂蜜，或读过多少关于蜜蜂方向感异乎寻常的文章，我们仍害怕被蜇。
en: No matter how much we like honey, or how much we have read about the uncanny sense of direction which bees possess, we have a horror of being stung.

zh: 我们的恐惧大多毫无道理，却难以磨灭。
en: Most of our fears are unreasonable; but they are impossible to erase.

zh: 然而与此同时，昆虫又奇异地引人入胜。
en: At the same time, however, insects are strangely fascinating.

zh: 我们喜读有关它们的文字，尤其当得知它们——譬如螳螂——过着何等骇人的生活时。
en: We enjoy reading about them, especially when we find that, like the praying mantis, they lead perfectly horrible lives.

zh: 我们喜欢凝视它们劳作，沉醉其间，但愿它们并未察觉我们的存在。
en: We enjoy staring at them, entranced as they go about their business, 15 unaware(we hope)of our presence.

zh: 谁不曾为蜘蛛扑向苍蝇、或蚁群凯旋搬回一只硕大死甲虫而肃然驻足？
en: Who has not stood in awe at the sight of a spider pouncing on a fly, or a column of ants triumphantly bearing home an enormous dead beetle?

zh: 去年夏天，我在花园中连日观察成千上万只蚂蚁沿我心爱的桃树树干向上攀爬。
en: Last summer I spent days in the garden watching thousands of ants crawling up the trunk of my prize peach tree.

zh: 那棵树依房屋向阳一面的暖墙而生。
en: The tree has grown against a warm wall on a sheltered side of the house.

zh: 我尤为自豪，不仅因它熬过数个严冬，亦因它偶尔结出甘美多汁的桃子。
en: I am especially proud of it, not only because it has survived several severe winters, but because it occasionally produces luscious 20 peaches.

zh: 夏季里，我注意到树叶开始枯萎。
en: During the summer, I noticed that the leaves of the tree were beginning to wither.

zh: 叶片背面可见成簇的微小昆虫，名曰蚜虫。
en: Clusters of tiny insects called aphids were to be found on the underside of the leaves.

zh: 一支庞大的蚁群前来造访，从蚜虫身上汲取一种蜜汁。
en: They were visited by a large colony of ants which obtained a sort of honey from them.

zh: 我当即着手一项试验；尽管未能赶走蚂蚁，却令我连续二十四小时乐此不疲。
en: I immediately embarked on an experiment which, even though it failed to get rid of the ants, kept me fascinated for twenty-four hours.

zh: 我用胶带缠住树干基部，使蚂蚁无法接近蚜虫。
en: I bound the base of the tree .with sticky tape, making it impossible for the ants to reach the aphids.

zh: 胶带黏性极强，它们不敢逾越。
en: The tape was so sticky that they did 25 not dare to cross it.

zh: 良久，我见它们在树下茫然乱窜。
en: For a long time, I watched them scurrying around the base of the tree in bewilderment.

zh: 我甚至午夜持手电筒外出，满意而诧异地见蚂蚁仍在胶带周围蜂拥，却无可奈何。
en: Ieven went out at midnight with a torch and noted with satisfaction(and surprise)that the ants were still swarming around the sticky tape without being able to do anything about it.

zh: 翌日清晨我早早起身，指望蚂蚁已绝望放弃。
en: I got up early next morning hoping to find that the ants had given up in despair.

zh: 不料它们竟另辟蹊径。
en: Instead, I saw that they had discovered a new route.

zh: 它们沿屋墙攀上，再爬到树叶之上。
en: They were climbing up the wallof the house and then on to the leaves of the tree.

zh: 我悲哀地意识到，自己已完全被它们的机智所战胜。
en: I realized sadly that I had 30 been completely defeated by their ingenuity.

zh: 蚂蚁迅即找到了对策，回击我那全然不科学的手法！
en: The ants had been quick to find an answer to my thoroughly unscientific methods!

zh: 第三册 第五十四课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 54

zh: 天文学近年来的进展使我们有能力在本银河系及其他星系中探测行星。
en: Recent developments in astronomy have made it possible to detect planets in our own Milky Way and in other galaxies.

zh: 这是一项重大成就，因为行星体积相对微小，且自身不发光。
en: This is a major achievement because, in relative terms, planets are very small and do not emit light.

zh: 发现行星已属不易，而在其上发现生命必将难上加难。
en: Finding planets is proving hardenough, but finding 5 life on them will prove infinitely more difficult.

zh: 首要问题是：行星是否确能维持生命。
en: The first question to answer is whether a planet can actually support life.

zh: 例如在本太阳系中，金星酷热难耐，火星严寒刺骨，皆不宜生命存活。
en: In our own solar system, for example, Venus is far too hot and Mars is far too cold to support life.

zh: 唯地球提供理想条件；即便在此，植物与动物经过逾四十亿年方演进而成。
en: Only the Earth provides ideal conditions, and even here it has taken more than four billion years for plant and animal life to evolve.

zh: 行星能否维持生命，取决于其恒星的体积与亮度，亦即其「太阳」。
en: Whether a planet can support life depends on the size and brightness of its star, that is its‘sun’.

zh: 设想一颗恒星，其体积、亮度与热度可达我们太阳的二倍、十倍乃至二十倍。
en: Imagine a star up to twenty times larger, brighter and hotter than our own sun.

zh: 行星须距其极远方能维持生命。
en: A planet would have to be a very long way from it to be capable of supporting life.

zh: 反之，若恒星较小，则宜于维持生命的行星须以较近轨道绕行，并须具备生命形态演化的完美条件。
en: Alternatively, if the star were small, the life-supporting planet would have to have a close orbit round it and also provide the perfect conditions for life forms to develop.

zh: 但我们又如何寻觅这样的行星呢？
en: But how would we find such a planet?

zh: 目前尚不存在能够探测生命迹象的望远镜。
en: At present, there is no telescope in existence that is capable of 15 detecting the presence of life.

zh: 研制此类望远镜将成为二十一世纪伟大的天文学工程之一。
en: The development of such a telescope willbe one of the great astronomical projects of the twenty-first century.

zh: 使用地面望远镜在另一行星上寻找生命是不可能的。
en: It is impossible to look for life on another planet using earth-based telescopes.

zh: 我们温暖的大气层与望远镜自身产生的热量，使我们无法观测到行星这般微小的天体。
en: Our own warm atmosphere and the heat generated by the telescope would make it impossible to detect objects as smallas planets.

zh: 即便如成就卓著的哈勃望远镜那样环绕地球运行的望远镜亦不相宜，盖因太阳系中尘埃微粒之故。
en: Even a telescope in orbit round the earth, like the very successful Hubble telescope, would not be suitable because 20 of the dust particles in our solar system.

zh: 欲在外层空间寻找生命，望远镜须置于与木星相距之远，因越向我们太阳系外缘行进，尘埃便越稀薄。
en: A telescope would have to be as far away as the planet Jupiter to look for life in outer space, because the dust becomes thinner the further we travel towards the outer edges of our own solar system.

zh: 一旦探测到行星，须设法遮蔽其恒星之光，方能「看清」该行星并分析其大气。
en: Once we detected a planet, we would have to find a way of blotting out the light from its star, so that we would be able to 'see'the planet properly and analyse its atmosphere.

zh: 首先我们要寻找的是植物生命，而非「小绿人」。
en: In the first instance, we would be looking for plant life, rather than 'little green men'.

zh: 行星上最可能演化的生命形态当属细菌。
en: The life forms most likely to 25 develop on a planet would be bacteria.

zh: 正是细菌产生了我们在地球上所呼吸的氧气。
en: It is bacteria that have generated the oxygen we breathe on earth.

zh: 在地球历史的大部分时间里，它们曾是地球上唯一的生命形式。
en: For most of the earth's history they have been the only form of life on our planet.

zh: 作为地球上的居民，我们总怀着被小绿人造访并能与之交流的希望。
en: As Earth-dwellers, we always cherish the hope that we willbe visited by little green men and that we willbe able to communicate with them.

zh: 但此种希望始终仅存于科幻领域。
en: But this hope is always in the realms of science fiction.

zh: 若能在另一行星发现细菌等低级生命，将彻底改变我们对自己的看法。
en: If we were able to discover lowly forms of life like bacteria on another planet, it would completely change our view of ourselves.

zh: 正如美国国家航空航天局的丹尼尔·戈尔丁所言：「在别处发现生命将改变一切。」
en: As Daniel Goldin of 30 NASA observed,'Finding life elsewhere would change everything.

zh: 人类的任何努力与思想都不会不受影响。
en: No human endeavour or thought would be unchanged by it.

zh: 第三册 第五十五课
en: ' Unit 3 Lesson 55

zh: 构成我们农场东界的那条河流，向来在我们的生活中举足轻重。
en: The river which forms the eastern boundary of our farm has always played an important part in our lives.

zh: 若无此河，我们便无法谋生。
en: Without it we could not make a living.

zh: 泉水仅足以供应宅院，故农田用水须从河中抽水。
en: There is only enough spring water to supply the needs of the house, so we have to pump from the river for farm use.

zh: 我们向这条河倾诉一切秘密。
en: We tell 5 the river all our secrets.

zh: 我们凭直觉便知，正如同养蜂人对蜜蜂一般——若人生大事不向它禀告，灾祸或将来临。
en: We know instinctively, just as beekeepers with their bees, that misfortune might overtake us if the important events of our lives were not related to it.

zh: 夏日里我们特地在河边举办生日聚会。
en: We have special river birthday parties in the summer.

zh: 有时我们溯流而上，到一处钟爱的回水湾；有时在船屋聚会——那是农场上一任主人在草地上所建，毗邻最深、可供游泳跳水的水潭。
en: Sometimes we go upstream to a favourite backwater, sometimes we have our party at the boathouse, which a predecessor of ours at the farm built in the meadow 10 hard by the deepest pool for swimming and diving.

zh: 酷暑时节我们选中在午夜举行生日聚会，那最是激动人心。
en: In a heat wave we choose a midnight birthday party and that is the most exciting of all.

zh: 我们在河边迎送四季：春日给最年幼的女孩戴上花冠，仲夏节前夜举行夏节庆典，秋日感恩收获，冬日则将冬青花环投入激流。
en: We welcome the seasons by the riverside, crowning the youngest girl with flowers in the spring, holding a summer festival on Midsummer Eve, giving thanks for the harvest in the autumn, and throwing a holly wreath into the current in the winter.

zh: 久雨之后，河水或溢出堤岸。
en: After a long period of rain the river may overflow its banks.

zh: 此事罕见，因我们这里的气候难得走向极端。
en: This is a rare occurrence as our climate 15 seldom goes to extremes.

zh: 所幸唯有地势较低的田地——占农场很小一部分——会受淹；其他农场位置较差，洪水有时会给其主带来灾害。
en: We are lucky in that only the lower fields, which make up a very small proportion of our farm, are affected by flooding, but other farms are less favourably sited, and flooding can sometimes spell disaster for their owners.

zh: 有一个严冬，我们眼见河水漫上较低的草地。
en: One bad winter we watched the river creep up the lower meadows.

zh: 全部牲口已移入厩中，我们损失的余地不大。
en: All the cattle had been moved into stalls and we stood to lose little.

zh: 但我们为近邻担忧：他们的农场地势低洼，且是初来本地的新户。
en: We were, however, worried about our nearest neighbours, whose farm was 20 low lying and who were newcomers to the district.

zh: 洪水使电话中断，我们无法得知他们境况如何。
en: As the floods had put the telephone out of order, we could not find out how they were managing.

zh: 从阁楼窗户可纵览两农场交界处的那段河面；在危急关头我们轮流守望该处。
en: From an attic window we could get a sweeping view of the river where their land joined ours, and at the most critical juncture we took turns in watching that point.

zh: 灾难的第一个征兆是一只死羊漂下。
en: The first sign of disaster was a dead sheep floating down.

zh: 继而一匹马勇敢游来，但我们担心急流会使它在筋疲力竭前无法靠岸。
en: Next came a horse, swimming bravely, but we were afraid that the strength of the current would prevent its landing anywhere before it became exhausted.

zh: 忽然出现一只木筏，颇似挪亚方舟，载着全家、几只母鸡、狗、猫及笼中之鸟。
en: Suddenly a 25 raft appeared, looking rather like Noah's ark, carrying the whole family, a few hens, the dogs, a cat, and a bird in a cage.

zh: 我们明白他们定是为上涨的洪水过度惊恐，因其屋宇地基牢固，即便几乎没顶亦能屹立。
en: We realized that they must have become unduly frightened by the rising flood, for their house, which had sound foundations, would have stood stoutly even if it had been almost submerged.

zh: 我家男丁手持船篙，涉过被淹的草地，指望能钩住木筏一角，将其从急流中拖向岸这边。
en: The men of our family waded down through our flooded meadows with boathooks, in the hope of being able to grapple a corner of the raft and pull it out of the current towards our bank.

zh: 他们竟能成功，我们至今仍以为近乎奇迹。
en: We still think it a miracle that they were 30 able to do so.

zh: 第三册 第五十六课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 56

zh: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。
en: Listen to the tape then answer the question below.

zh: 我停车让汽车冷却下来，并察看地图。
en: I stopped to let the car cool offand to study the map.

zh: 我原以为此刻应已接近目的地，但眼前一切仍觉陌生。
en: I had expected to be near my objective by now, but everything still seemed alien to me.

zh: 父亲带我出国时我才五岁，距今已十八年。
en: I was only five when my father had taken me abroad, and that was eighteen years ago.

zh: 母亲在一次不幸事故中英年早逝后，他久久未能从震惊与孤寂中恢复。
en: When my mother had died after a tragic accident, he did not quickly recover from the shock and loneliness.

zh: 周遭处处遗有她的气息，不断撕裂旧创。
en: Everything around him was full of her presence, continually reopening the wound.

zh: 于是他决定移居国外。
en: So he decided to emigrate.

zh: 在新国度他全身心为我们父子俩经营新生活，悲痛遂渐渐淡去。
en: In the new country he became absorbed in making a new life for the two of us, so that he gradually ceased to grieve.

zh: 他未再娶，我在没有女性照料下长大；但我无所缺憾，因他身兼父母。
en: He did not marry again and I was brought up without a woman's care; but I lacked for nothing, for he was both father and mother to me.

zh: 他总想有朝一日回国，却无意长驻。
en: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay.

zh: 他与我之根已深深扎在这片新土地。
en: His roots and mine had become too firmly embedded in the new land.

zh: 但他想再见故旧，并祭扫母亲之墓。
en: But he wanted to see the old folk again and to visit my mother's grave.

zh: 我们原定动身前数月他病危不起；得知自己将不久于人世，他要我答应独自前往。
en: He became mortally ill a few months before we had planned to go and, when he knew that he was dying, he made me promise to go on my own.

zh: 上岸次日我租了一辆车，买了一本详尽的地图册，越野途中颇得力，末段原以为不必再用。
en: I hired a car the day after landing and bought a comprehensive book of maps, which I found most helpful on the cross-country journey, but which I did not think I should need on the last stage.

zh: 我其实并不记得任何事。
en: It was not that I actually remembered anything at all.

zh: 但父亲一再描述离开最近城镇后每个路标处应见何景，故我确信能认出那片熟悉的乡土。
en: But my father had described over and over again what we should see at every milestone, after leaving the nearest town, so that I was positive I should recognize it as familiar territory.

zh: 然而我错了——我已迷路。
en: Well, I had been wrong, for I was now lost.

zh: 我查看地图，又看里程表。
en: I looked at the map and then at the milometer.

zh: 离城已行十英里，按父亲所言，此处应见山谷中的农庄与村舍，远处露出我们村庄教堂的尖顶。
en: I had come ten miles since leaving the town, and at this point, according to my father, I should be looking at farms and cottages in a valley, with the spire of the church of our village showing in the far distance.

zh: 我却不见山谷、农庄、村舍与教堂尖顶——唯有一片湖。
en: I could see no valley, no farms, no cottages and no church spire —only a lake.

zh: 我断定自己某处拐错了弯。
en: I decided that I must have taken a wrong turning somewhere.

zh: 于是我折返城中，沿原路重来，不时瞥一眼地图。
en: So I drove back to the town and began to retrace the route, taking frequent glances at the map.

zh: 却又回到同一街角。
en: I landed up at the same corner.

zh: 奇怪的是地图上竟未标出那片湖。
en: The curious thing was that the lake was not marked on the map.

zh: 我仿佛误入梦魇之境，正如人在梦中有时所遇。
en: I felt as if I had stumbled into a nightmare country, as you sometimes do in dreams.

zh: 亦如噩梦，举目无人可求助。
en: And, as in a nightmare, there was nobody in sight to help me.

zh: 幸而正当我寻思下一步如何时，地平线上出现一名骑马朝我而来的人。
en: Fortunately for me, as I was wondering what to do next, there appeared on the horizon a man on horseback, riding in my direction.

zh: 我等其走近，继而打听去我们老村的路径。
en: I waited till he came near, then I asked him the way to our old village.

zh: 他说如今已无村庄。
en: He said that there was now no village.

zh: 我以为他误解，便重复村名。
en: I thought he must have misunderstood me, so I repeated its name.

zh: 这次他指向那片湖。
en: This time he pointed to the lake.

zh: 村庄已不复存在，因它与整个山谷均被淹没。
en: The village no longer existed because it had been submerged, and all the valley too.

zh: 该湖并非天然湖泊，而是人工水库。
en: The lake was not a natural one, but a man-made reservoir.

zh: 第三册 第五十七课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 57

zh: 她正想着翌晨须因这般严重的疏忽而斥责家中帮佣，忽又记起帮佣走后她曾外出购物，且确知两把钥匙都已锁好。
en: She was thinking that she must reprimand her home help the next morning for such a monstrous piece of negligence, when she remembered that she had gone shopping after the home help had left and she knew that she had turned both keys in their locks.

zh: 她缓步走进门厅，立刻发现各房门洞开，而她出门前依惯例明明都已关上。
en: She walked slowly into the hall and at once noticed that all the room doors were open, yet following her regular practice she had shut them before going out.

zh: 朝客厅望去，只见书桌旁一片狼藉。
en: Looking into the drawing room, she saw a scene of confusion over by her writing desk.

zh: 彼时一望便知，她外出时有贼强行闯入。
en: It was as clear as daylight then that burglars had forced an entry during her absence.

zh: 她第一反应是逐室搜寻窃贼，继而转念，以她这般年纪，或宜有人相伴更为稳妥，便下楼去地下室找门房。
en: Her first impulse was to go round all the rooms looking for the thieves, but then she decided that at her age it might be more prudent to have someone with her, so she went to fetch the porter from his basement.

zh: 此刻她双腿发颤，便坐下接过一杯浓茶，门房则打电话报警。
en: By this time her legs were beginning to tremble, so she sat down and accepted a cup of very strong tea, while he telephoned the police.

zh: 她恢复镇定后，在门房搀扶下准备搜寻寓所，看是否尚有潜藏者。
en: Then, her composure regained, she was ready to set off with the porter's assistance to search for any intruders who might still be lurking in her flat.

zh: 二人穿室而行，小心不触碰物品，以免妨碍警方搜寻指纹。
en: They went through the rooms, being careful to touch nothing, as they did not want to hinder the police in their search for fingerprints.

zh: 凌乱不堪，难以想象。
en: The chaos was inconceivable.

zh: 她在此寓所居住三十年，攒物成癖，犹如喜鹊；仿佛其所有家当尽被倒出、反复翻转。
en: She had lived in the flat for thirty years and was a veritable magpie at hoarding; and it seemed as though everything she possessed had been tossed out and turned over and over.

zh: 至少多年前早该丢弃之物，如今反倒便于分拣。
en: At least sorting out the things she should have discarded years ago was now being made easier for her.

zh: 随即一名巡官带着警员赶到，她告知他们发现寓所遭洗劫。
en: Then a police inspector arrived with a constable and she told them of her discovery of the ransacked flat.

zh: 巡官开始提取指纹，警员则检查前门锁是否曾被撬，从而证明窃贼或用了万能钥匙，或自阳台攀入。
en: The inspector began to look for fingerprints, while the constable checked that the front door locks had not been forced, thereby proving that the burglars had either used skeleton keys or entered over the balcony.

zh: 未见指纹痕迹，但巡官发现一个肮脏的红包裹，内装珠宝，老妇称非己有。
en: There was no trace of fingerprints, but the inspector found a dirty red bundle that con- tained jewellery which the old lady said was not hers.

zh: 足见闯入此寓所并非他们当日首次作案，必曾被打断。
en: So their entry into this flat was apparently not the burglars'first job that day and they must have been disturbed.

zh: 巡官请老妇翌日尽量清点失物，并劝她数夜勿独宿寓中。
en: The inspector then asked the old lady to try to check what was missing by the next day and advised her not to stay alone in the flat for a few nights.

zh: 老妇心想此人未免多事，但门房亦以为然，她便致电女儿，请其相助，只说是点小麻烦。
en: The old lady thought he was afussy creature, but since the porter agreed with him, she rang up her daughter and asked for her help in what she described as a little spot of bother.

zh: 人们往往积聚财物，有时甚至浑然不觉。
en: People tend to amass possessions, sometimes without being aware of doing so.

zh: 的确，当他们发现某件有用之物而他们并不知自己曾拥有时，常会惊喜。
en: Indeed they can have a delightful surprise when they find something useful which they did not know they owned.

zh: 从不需搬迁的人往往不加辨别地收藏只能称之为杂物的东西。
en: Those who never have to move house become indiscriminate collectors of what can only be described as clutter.

zh: 他们把不用的物件留在抽屉、橱柜与阁楼经年累月，以为有朝一日或许会用到正是那些东西。
en: They leave unwanted objects in drawers, cupboards and attics for years, in the belief that they may one day need just those very things.

zh: 人上了年纪还会因另外两种原因囤积物品：一是缺乏整理丢弃所需的体力与心力，二是恋旧。
en: As they grow old, people also accumulate belongings for two other reasons, lack of physical and mental energy, both of which are essential in turning out and throwing away, and sentiment.

zh: 久藏之物满载对过去的联想，或许与已故亲人相关，因而渐渐获得超出其实际价值的意味。
en: Things owned for a long time are full of associations with the past, perhaps with relatives who are dead, and so they gradually acquire a value beyond their true worth.

zh: 有些物件在家中刻意收藏，意在避免浪费。
en: Some things are collected deliberately in the home in an attempt to avoid waste.

zh: 其中我要列举绳与牛皮纸：节俭之人拆开包裹后将其保留，免得再买这两种必需品。
en: Among these I would list string and brown paper, kept by thrifty people when a parcel has been opened, to save buying these two requisites.

zh: 收藏小物件极易演变为癖好。
en: Collecting small items can easily become a mania.

zh: 我认识一位女士，总从报纸上剪下时装式样图，若有钱她愿买那些衣服。
