en: Iknow someone who always cuts sketches out from newspapers of model clothes that she would like to buy if she had the money.

zh: 她并不富裕，能买得起那些东西的机会渺茫；她却总不够果决，无法戒除此习。
en: As she is not rich, the chances that she willever be able to afford such purchases are remote; but she is never sufficiently strong- minded to be able to stop the practice.

zh: 这是无害的习惯，却把书桌堆得狼藉不堪，每次打开抽屉，纸片便四处飞散。
en: It is a harmless habit, but it litters up her desk to such an extent that every time she opens it, loose bits of paper fall out in every direction.

zh: 把收藏当作严肃爱好则大不相同，且有许多益处。
en: Collecting as a serious hobby is quite different and has many advantages.

zh: 它能在闲暇时提供消遣，因仅观赏所藏亦为乐事。
en: It provides relaxation for leisure hours, as just looking at one's treasures is always a joy.

zh: 不必外出寻娱乐，因藏品就在家中。
en: One does not have to go outside for amuse- ment, since the collection is housed at home.

zh: 无论收藏何物——邮票、唱片、书籍初版本、瓷器、玻璃器、古家具、画片、汽车模型、剥制鸟类、玩具动物——总有事可做：或为新得寻合适位置，或在参考书里核对事实。
en: Whatever it consists of, stamps, records, first editions of books, china, glass, antique furniture, pictures, model cars, stuffed birds, toy animals, there is always something to do in connection with it, from finding the right place for the latest addition, to verifying facts in reference books.

zh: 这一爱好使人不仅受教于所选专题，亦涉猎与之相关的常识。
en: This hobby educates one not only in the chosen subject, but also in general matters which have some bearing on it.

zh: 此外还有别的好处。
en: There are also other benefits.

zh: 人愿结交志趣相投的藏家，请教、交流心得、互换藏品、炫耀最新收获。
en: One wants to meet like-minded collectors, to get advice, to compare notes, to exchange articles, to show off the latest find.

zh: 于是朋友圈子扩大。
en: So one's circle of friends grows.

zh: 不久爱好或引出走，或赴他城聚会，或为寻访稀见标本而远涉异国，因藏家并不囿于一国。
en: Soon the hobby leads to travel, perhaps to a meeting in another town, possibly a trip abroad in search of a rare specimen, for collectors are not confined to any one country.

zh: 经年累月，人或可成为某一收藏领域的权威，会先被邀向小范围聚会作非正式讲演，若受欢迎再面向更多听众。
en: Over the years, one may well become an authority on one's hobby and will very probably be asked to give informal talks to litle gatherings and then, if successful, to larger audiences.

zh: 如此自信心增强：先因精通一题，继而能侃侃而谈。
en: In this way self-confidence grows, first from mastering a subject, then from being able to talk about it.

zh: 收藏以如此有建设性的方式占用余暇，使人知足，无暇无聊。
en: Collecting, by occupying spare time so constructively, makes a person contented, with no time for boredom.

zh: 第三册 第五十九课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 59

zh: 守时是一切文明社会公共事务中不可或缺的习性。
en: Punctuality is a necessary habit in all public affairs in civilized society.

zh: 若无守时，任何事皆难善始善终；一切将陷于混乱。
en: Without it, nothing could ever be brought to a conclusion; everything would be in a state of chaos.

zh: 唯有在人口稀疏的乡村社区方可稍忽此节。
en: Only in a sparsely-populated rural community is it possible to disregard it.

zh: 寻常生活中，对迟到尚可稍加宽宥。
en: In ordinary living, there can be some tolerance of unpunctuality.

zh: 从事艰深问题的知识分子，为手边课题已将诸事协调安排停当。
en: The intellectual, who is working on some abstruse problem, has everything coordinated and organized for the matter in hand.

zh: 故而若晚宴迟到可得谅解。
en: He is therefore forgiven if late for a dinner party.

zh: 但人们往往因不准时而受责，其实过错仅在时间安排过紧。
en: But people are often reproached for unpunctuality when their only fault is cutting things fine.

zh: 精力旺盛、头脑敏捷者不忍虚掷光阴，故常欲在赴约前了结手头工作。
en: It is hard for energetic, quick-minded people to waste time, so they are often tempted to finish a 10 job before setting out to keep an appointment.

zh: 若途中无爆胎、改道、浓雾骤降等意外，他们便能准时。
en: If no accidents occur on the way, like punctured tyres, diversions of traffic, sudden descent of fog, they will be on time.

zh: 他们常较从不迟到者更为勤勉有用。
en: They are often more industrious, useful citizens than those who are never late.

zh: 过分早到与不准时同样令人生厌。
en: The over-punctual can be as much a trial to others as the unpunctual.

zh: 提前半小时抵达的客人最为讨厌。
en: The guest who arrives half an hour too soon is the greatest nuisance.

zh: 我家有些亲友曾有此等恼人习惯。
en: Some friends of my family had this iritating habit.

zh: 唯一办法是请他们比其他客人晚到半小时。
en: The only thing to do was ask them to come half an hour later than the other guests.

zh: 于是他们恰在我们希望之时到来。
en: Then they arrived 15 just when we wanted them.

zh: 若要赶火车，宁可宽裕地早到，切勿迟到分秒。
en: If you are catching a train, it is always better to be comfortably early than even a fraction of a minute too late.

zh: 虽早到或略费时间，却远胜于误车而候车逾一时以上；亦可免却在列车正驶离站台之际赶到却无法登车的懊丧。
en: Although being early may mean wasting a little time, this will be less than if you miss the train and have to wait an hour or more for the next one; and you avoid the frustration of arriving at the very moment when the train is drawing out of the station and being unable to get on it.

zh: 更难堪的是准时赶到站台，却仍眼见列车开行而自己未能上车。
en: An even harder situation is to be on the 20 platform in good time for a train and still to see it go off without you.

zh: 一位少女首次独行便遭遇此种经历。
en: Such an experience befell a certain young girl the first time she was travelling alone.

zh: 她提前二十分钟进站，因父母再三强调误车不可原谅，会使她将要寄宿的朋友为接她而跑两趟。
en: She entered the station twenty minutes before the train was due, since her parents had impressed upon her that it would be unforgivable to mis it and cause the friends with whom she was going to stay to make two journeys to meet her.

zh: 她将行李交予搬运工并出示车票。
en: She gave her luggage to a porter and showed him her ticket.

zh: 令她惊骇的是，对方说她早到了两个钟头。
en: To her horror he said 25 that she was two hours too soon.

zh: 她在手袋里翻出父亲写下全程细节的纸条交给搬运工。
en: She felt in her handbag for the piece of paper on which her father had written down all the details of the journey and gave it to the porter.

zh: 对方承认确有列车在纸上所列时刻进站并会停靠，但仅为接取邮件，不载客。
en: He agreed that a train did come into the station at the time on the paper and that it did stop, but only to take on mail, not passengers.

zh: 女孩要求看时刻表，确信父亲不会犯下如此错误。
en: The girl asked to see a timetable, feling sure that her father could not have made such a mistake.

zh: 搬运工取来时刻表，站长亦随同而至，他郑重取出时刻表，指出列车抵达本站时间旁有一个微不可辨的「o」；此小「o」表示列车仅停邮。
en: The porter went to fetch one and arrived back with the station master, who produced it with a flourish and pointed out a microscopic 30 ‘o'beside the time of the arrival of the train at his station; this little'o'indicated that the train only stopped for mail.

zh: 恰恰此时，列车驶入车站。
en: Just as that moment the train cameinto the station.

zh: 女孩泪流满面，恳求准许她溜进行李车厢。
en: The girl, tears streaming down her face, begged to be allowed to slip into the guard's van.

zh: 但站长不肯通融：规章不可违。
en: But the station master was adamant: rules could not be broken.

zh: 她只能目送列车驶向目的地，而自己被留在身后。
en: And she had to watch that train disappear towards her destination while she was left behind.

zh: 第三册 第六十课
en: Unit 3 Lesson 60
